  --board-id e119c091-e1fa-4596-9bc7-038ceab6adec \
  --column-id 591afb61-7289-46bf-ba07-21758afd44fe \
  --title "Architecture: Unified Backends via True Deferred Reads" \
  --description "Goal: open_context() establishes a connection with zero I/O. Data is loaded lazily on first
DataStore/CommandStore call. Eliminates the is_sqlite fork across all three apps (TUI, CLI, MCP).

PROBLEM
KanbanContext::load() today: (1) calls PersistenceStore::load() fetching full snapshot blob, (2) deserializes everything into
InMemoryStore, (3) loads command log. This is eager-load-everything. All three apps fork on is_sqlite because JSON and SQLite
have different initialization contracts.

TARGET ARCHITECTURE
DataStore + CommandStore are the sole runtime interface. PersistenceStore (blob) is used only for import/export/migration.
KanbanBackend gains lifecycle methods: flush(), reload(), needs_flush(), needs_save_worker(), on_undo_state_changed(),
instance_id().

STEP 1 — KanbanBackend lifecycle methods [DONE]
Add flush/reload/needs_flush/needs_save_worker/on_undo_state_changed/instance_id to KanbanBackend with default no-op impls.
SQLite impl: flush()=WAL checkpoint, reload()=no-op, needs_flush()=false. InMemoryStore uses all defaults.
Files: crates/kanban-service/src/backend.rs, crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs,
crates/kanban-domain/src/data_store.rs

STEP 2 — JsonDataStore lazy JSON backend [DONE]
New type in crates/kanban-service/src/json_backend.rs wrapping Arc<dyn PersistenceStore> + RwLock<Option<InMemoryStore>> +
AtomicBool dirty. Construction is zero-I/O. ensure_loaded() reads file on first access using block_in_place (requires
multi_thread runtime). flush() serialises cache to disk. reload() sets inner=None so next access re-reads.
needs_save_worker()=true (background flush worker needed). on_undo_state_changed() caches cursor+baseline for next flush.

STEP 3 — Simplify KanbanContext [DONE]
Single constructor KanbanContext::open(backend, config) — zero I/O. baseline_snapshot: Option<Snapshot> starts None, captured
lazily on first execute(). command_count restored lazily on first undo/redo via ensure_undo_state_initialized(). save()
delegates to backend.flush().await. reload() delegates to backend.reload().await and clears baseline_snapshot. Removed store:
Option<Arc<dyn PersistenceStore>> field, replace_store(), store() getter.
File: crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs

STEP 4 — StoreManager::make_backend() [DONE]
New method returning Arc<dyn KanbanBackend>. If is_sqlite → SqliteStore::open(). Else → JsonDataStore::new(make_store()). Also
 make_backend_sync() for sync contexts (uses block_on internally).
File: crates/kanban-service/src/store_manager.rs

STEP 5 — kanban_service::open_context() [DONE]
Single public entry point: sync_backend_with_file() + make_backend() + KanbanContext::open(). All three apps can call this
instead of duplicating the StoreManager dance.
File: crates/kanban-service/src/lib.rs

STEP 6 — Wire FileWatcher to backend.reload() [DONE]
auto_reload_from_external_change() in app/mod.rs now calls ctx.reload() → backend.reload() instead of store.load().await
directly. SaveCoordinator sends () flush signals instead of Snapshot blobs; save worker calls backend.flush().await on each
signal. TuiContext uses needs_save_worker() to decide whether to spawn the background worker.
Files: crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs, crates/kanban-tui/src/state/mod.rs, crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs,
crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/settings_handlers.rs

STEP 7 — Unify App Initialization [PENDING]
Replace divergent is_sqlite forks in all three apps with open_context().

TUI: Collapse App::new_with_store() + App::open_sqlite() into App::new(ctx: KanbanContext). Remove TuiContext::from_context();
 TuiContext::new(ctx) is the only constructor. SaveCoordinator::new() uses ctx.backend().needs_save_worker() instead of
store.is_some().

CLI: Remove if is_sqlite { App::open_sqlite() } else { App::new_with_store() } fork in app.rs. CliContext::load() calls
kanban_service::open_context() directly. Remove StoreManager parameter.

MCP: McpContext::new() calls kanban_service::open_context() directly. Remove StoreManager parameter.

Delete dead code: App::open_sqlite(), App::new_with_store(), TuiContext::from_context(), resolve_is_sqlite() helper.

TDD tests to write first:
- test_tui_context_single_constructor_json (save_rx is Some)
- test_tui_context_single_constructor_sqlite (save_rx is None)
- test_cli_init_and_run_json_unified_path
- test_cli_init_and_run_sqlite_unified_path
- test_mcp_list_boards_json_unified_path
- test_mcp_list_boards_sqlite_unified_path

Files to modify:
- crates/kanban-cli/src/app.rs
- crates/kanban-cli/src/context.rs
- crates/kanban-mcp/src/context.rs
- crates/kanban-mcp/src/server.rs
- crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs
- crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs

VERIFICATION (all steps)
1. cargo test --workspace — all tests pass
2. cargo run -- init --name Test test.json && cargo run test.json — JSON path works
3. cargo run -- init --name Test test.sqlite && cargo run test.sqlite — SQLite path works
4. Modify test.json externally while TUI open → conflict banner appears
5. Modify test.sqlite externally → next read reflects new state transparently
6. MCP tool calls persist for both backends
7. Undo/redo works on both backends after lazy baseline change" \
  --priority High

Verification

After running the command, open the TUI (kanban ~/notes/memes/kanban.json) and confirm the card
appears in the TODO column.

Context

The project has two backends (JSON, SQLite) and three apps (TUI, CLI, MCP) with divergent initialization paths. The goal is
not just a surface unification — the underlying requirement is true deferred reads: open_context() should establish a
connection and do zero I/O. Data is only read from disk/DB when a specific entity is first requested.

This solves all three pain points simultaneously:
- Startup time: nothing loaded until the UI actually renders
- Staleness: reload() on file-watch event is well-defined per backend
- Extensibility: remote backends (PostgreSQL, HTTP API) are natural — each DataStore method becomes a network call

---
Current State: Why the Model Is Wrong

The Snapshot Bootstrap Problem

KanbanContext::load(store, config) today:
1. Calls PersistenceStore::load() → fetches full snapshot blob
2. Deserializes everything into InMemoryStore (all boards, columns, cards, sprints)
3. Loads the entire command log
4. Takes a baseline_snapshot of the full state

This is eager-load-everything regardless of what the user actually needs to see.

The Two-Path Problem

JSON path:   KanbanContext::load(store) → InMemoryStore (full eager hydration)
SQLite path: KanbanContext::open_sqlite() → SqliteStore (bypasses snapshot, but
             still loads command log state upfront)

All three apps fork on is_sqlite. The fork exists because the two paths have fundamentally different initialization contracts.

Why SQLite Is Already Half-Right

SqliteStore already implements DataStore with real SQL queries — list_boards() is SELECT * FROM boards,
list_columns_by_board(id) is SELECT ... WHERE board_id = ?. There is no pre-load. The problem is that
KanbanContext::open_sqlite() still loads command log state eagerly, and the rest of the codebase bypasses this with the
snapshot model anyway.

---

The PersistenceStore blob interface (load/save full snapshot) is used as the initialization protocol for KanbanContext. This
forces everything through a full-load. The correct initialization protocol is: establish a connection, do nothing else.

The fine-grained DataStore trait (already defined in kanban-domain) is the right lazy interface. KanbanContext should always
read through DataStore methods, never through blob snapshots during normal operation. Snapshots become a utility for
import/export/migration only.

Target Architecture
---

Principle: DataStore + CommandStore as the Sole Runtime Interface

Normal operation:   KanbanContext → DataStore / CommandStore methods (lazy, per-entity)
Import/Export:      KanbanContext → PersistenceStore (snapshot blob, on demand)

Extend KanbanBackend with Lifecycle Methods

KanbanBackend (in kanban-service/src/backend.rs) gains three methods:

pub trait KanbanBackend: DataStore + CommandStore + Send + Sync {
    fn as_data_store(&self) -> &dyn DataStore;

    // New lifecycle:
    async fn flush(&self) -> KanbanResult<()> { Ok(()) }   // default no-op
    async fn reload(&self) -> KanbanResult<()> { Ok(()) }  // default no-op
    fn needs_flush(&self) -> bool { false }                 // default: always in sync
}

- SQLite: flush() = WAL checkpoint, reload() = no-op (reads are always live), needs_flush() = false
- JsonDataStore (new): flush() = serialize cache to file, reload() = re-read file + clear cache, needs_flush() = tracks dirty
state

JsonDataStore (New Type)

Lives in crates/kanban-service/src/json_backend.rs (has access to both InMemoryStore and JsonFileStore).

JsonDataStore {
    path: PathBuf,
    inner: RwLock<Option<InMemoryStore>>,   // None until first access
    file_store: Arc<JsonFileStore>,
    dirty: AtomicBool,
}

- Any DataStore or CommandStore method call checks inner.is_none() → if so, reads the JSON file and populates InMemoryStore
(the one-time lazy load)
- Subsequent calls are served from the in-memory cache
- Writes update the cache + set dirty = true
- flush() serializes the in-memory state to JSON via JsonFileStore
- reload() sets inner = None → next access re-reads the file

This is the one place where "lazy" still reads the full JSON file — because JSON is a single blob, there's no way to partially
 read it. But the load is deferred until something actually needs data, making open_context() instantaneous even for large
files.

KanbanContext — Single Constructor, Zero Initial I/O

pub struct KanbanContext {
    backend: Arc<dyn KanbanBackend>,
    app_config: AppConfig,
    baseline_snapshot: Option<Snapshot>,    // None until first write
    undo_cursor: usize,
    command_count: usize,
    dirty: bool,
    conflict_pending: bool,
}

impl KanbanContext {
    pub async fn open(backend: Arc<dyn KanbanBackend>, config: AppConfig) -> KanbanResult<Self> {
        // Zero I/O. Just wraps the backend.
        Ok(Self {
            backend,
            app_config: config,
            baseline_snapshot: None,    // lazy: captured on first write
            undo_cursor: 0,
            command_count: 0,           // lazy: read from CommandStore on first undo/redo
            dirty: false,
            conflict_pending: false,
        })
    }
}

Undo baseline captured lazily:
On first execute(commands) call, if baseline_snapshot.is_none(), take a snapshot of current backend state and store it. The
baseline is the state at the time of first write, which is the correct semantic anyway.

Command count restored lazily:
On first undo/redo, if needed, call backend.command_count() to restore cursor position from the backend.

Unified Public Entry Point

In crates/kanban-service/src/lib.rs:

pub async fn open_context(locator: &str, config: AppConfig) -> KanbanResult<KanbanContext> {
    let mut config = config;
    let sm = StoreManager::new(default_registry());
    sm.sync_backend_with_file(locator, &mut config);
    let backend: Arc<dyn KanbanBackend> = sm.make_backend(locator, &config).await?;
    KanbanContext::open(backend, config).await
}

All three apps:
let ctx = kanban_service::open_context(&file_path, config).await?;
// Zero I/O has happened. First render triggers actual data reads.

StoreManager::make_backend()

New method in StoreManager that returns Arc<dyn KanbanBackend>:

pub async fn make_backend(
    &self,
    locator: &str,
    config: &AppConfig,
) -> KanbanResult<Arc<dyn KanbanBackend>> {
    if self.is_sqlite(locator) {
        Ok(Arc::new(SqliteStore::open(locator).await?))
    } else {
        let store = self.make_store("json", locator)?;    // existing PersistenceStore
        Ok(Arc::new(JsonDataStore::new(store)))
    }
}

---
Staleness / External Change Handling

With reload() on KanbanBackend:
- FileWatcher fires → KanbanContext calls backend.reload()
- For JSON: clears the in-memory cache. Next DataStore call re-reads the file.
- For SQLite: no-op. Reads are always live against the DB.
- If dirty is true when reload fires → conflict_pending = true (existing conflict UI)

This replaces the current FileMetadata mtime/hash conflict detection in JsonFileStore with a cleaner model: the context owns
conflict state, not the store.

---
What Happens to PersistenceStore

PersistenceStore trait and its implementations stay. Their role narrows:
- Import: load a snapshot blob and call backend.apply_snapshot()
- Export: call backend.snapshot() and pass to PersistenceStore::save()
- Migration: blob-level version upgrades (V1→V2 JSON envelope etc.)
- NOT used: for normal app startup or runtime reads

StoreFactory / StoreRegistry stay for this purpose. StoreManager::make_store() stays for import/export flows.

---
### TDD Workflow (Mandatory for Every Step)

Each step follows Red → Green → Refactor:
1. Red: Write failing tests that specify the expected behaviour. Present for review before implementing.
2. Green: Minimum implementation to make tests pass.
3. Refactor: Clean up without breaking tests.

cargo test --workspace must pass at the end of every step.

---
Recommended Order of Work

### Step 1 — Extend KanbanBackend with lifecycle methods

Why: KanbanBackend (backend.rs:8) currently has only as_data_store(). We need flush, reload, needs_flush so that KanbanContext
 can delegate persistence without knowing which backend it holds.

Red — tests to write first (crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs, inline #[cfg(test)]):
test_sqlite_backend_needs_flush_returns_false
test_sqlite_backend_flush_executes_wal_checkpoint
test_sqlite_backend_reload_is_noop  (list_boards before and after reload returns same data)
test_in_memory_store_lifecycle_defaults_are_noop
Use real TempDir SQLite file. Pattern: SqliteStore::open(tmp.path()).await?.

Green — changes:

crates/kanban-service/src/backend.rs:
- Line 9 (after as_data_store): add three methods with default no-op impls:
async fn flush(&self) -> KanbanResult<()> { Ok(()) }
async fn reload(&self) -> KanbanResult<()> { Ok(()) }
fn needs_flush(&self) -> bool { false }

crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs:
- Locate the impl KanbanBackend for SqliteStore block (currently only has as_data_store)
- Add concrete impls:
  - flush(): call self.checkpoint().await (checkpoint method already exists at ~line 1654)
  - reload(): Ok(()) — SQLite reads are always live
  - needs_flush(): false — write-through

crates/kanban-domain/src/in_memory_store.rs:
- InMemoryStore will use the default no-op impls (no explicit impl needed; covered by blanket impl on KanbanBackend)

Refactor: Confirm the blanket impl at backend.rs:13-17 still compiles with the new default methods.

---
### Step 2 — Create JsonDataStore

Why: Currently the JSON path requires creating InMemoryStore + JsonFileStore separately, wired together inside
KanbanContext::load(). This forces eager loading on construction. JsonDataStore encapsulates this pair and makes loading lazy
— the file is not read until the first DataStore method call.

Red — tests to write first (inline #[cfg(test)] in new crates/kanban-service/src/json_backend.rs):
test_json_data_store_construction_does_no_io
  (assert file not read: create store pointing at non-existent path, construction succeeds)
test_json_data_store_list_boards_triggers_load
  (create JSON file with 1 board; construction ok; assert inner is None; call list_boards(); assert returns 1 board)
test_json_data_store_needs_flush_false_when_clean
test_json_data_store_needs_flush_true_after_upsert
test_json_data_store_flush_writes_to_disk
  (upsert board; flush(); create new JsonDataStore at same path; list_boards() returns the board)
test_json_data_store_reload_clears_cache
  (load; modify file externally; reload(); list_boards() returns updated data)
test_json_data_store_command_log_round_trip
  (append commands; flush(); create fresh store; command_count() returns correct value)
Use real TempDir JSON files via kanban_persistence_json::JsonStoreFactory.

Green — new file crates/kanban-service/src/json_backend.rs:
pub struct JsonDataStore {
    file_store: Arc<dyn PersistenceStore + Send + Sync>,
    inner: RwLock<Option<InMemoryStore>>,
    dirty: AtomicBool,
}
- fn new(store: Arc<dyn PersistenceStore + Send + Sync>) -> Self — stores only, no I/O
- Private fn ensure_loaded(&self) -> KanbanResult<()>:
  - Acquires write lock on inner
  - If None: calls file_store.load() (or returns empty InMemoryStore if !file_store.exists())
  - Deserializes StoreSnapshot.data into Snapshot

  - Calls InMemoryStore::apply_snapshot(snapshot)
  - Restores command log from file_store.get_command_log() into InMemoryStore
  - Sets inner = Some(store)
- Every DataStore and CommandStore method: calls ensure_loaded() then delegates to inner
- Write methods additionally set dirty.store(true, Ordering::Release)
- flush(): if dirty, serialize snapshot + command log → call file_store.save() + file_store.sync_command_log(); clear dirty
- reload(): set inner = None (next access re-reads file); clear dirty
- needs_flush(): return dirty.load(Ordering::Acquire)

Also add impl KanbanBackend for JsonDataStore (delegates to above lifecycle methods). Add to crates/kanban-service/src/lib.rs
module declaration.

Refactor: Extract snapshot serialization helper (reuse snapshot_to_json_bytes / snapshot_from_json_bytes from existing
kanban-persistence crate).

---
### Step 3 — Simplify KanbanContext

Why: KanbanContext currently has a store: Option<Arc<dyn PersistenceStore>> field (context.rs:45) whose presence/absence
encodes which backend path we're on. save() (lines 422-445) and reload() (lines 398-410) both have let Some(store) =
&self.store else { return Ok(()) } guards. baseline_snapshot (line 46) is eagerly set on construction, forcing a snapshot
read. All of this disappears with the new model.

Red — integration tests in new crates/kanban-service/tests/context_open.rs:
test_open_context_json_does_no_io_at_construction
  (point at non-existent path; KanbanContext::open(JsonDataStore::new(...), cfg) succeeds immediately)
test_open_context_sqlite_does_no_io_at_construction
  (same for SqliteStore)
test_open_context_first_list_boards_triggers_load
  (create JSON with 1 board; open context; list_boards() returns 1)
test_open_context_baseline_is_none_before_first_execute
  (ctx.baseline_snapshot is None before any execute)
test_open_context_baseline_captured_on_first_execute
  (create_board; ctx.baseline_snapshot is Some)
test_open_context_save_flushes_json_not_sqlite
  (JSON: save() causes dirty=false; SQLite: save() is no-op, no-op is fine)
test_open_context_reload_delegates_to_backend
  (modify JSON externally; ctx.reload(); list_boards() returns updated data)
test_undo_redo_still_work_after_lazy_baseline

Green — changes to crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs:

Struct (lines 42-51):
- Line 45: REMOVE store: Option<Arc<dyn PersistenceStore + Send + Sync>>
- Line 46: CHANGE baseline_snapshot: Snapshot → baseline_snapshot: Option<Snapshot>

New constructor (replaces load, open_sqlite, open_json, empty):
// Replaces lines 54-147
pub async fn open(backend: Arc<dyn KanbanBackend>, config: AppConfig) -> KanbanResult<Self> {
    Ok(Self {
        backend,
        app_config: config,
        baseline_snapshot: None,   // lazy: set on first execute()
        undo_cursor: 0,            // lazy: restored on first undo/redo
        command_count: 0,          // lazy: restored from backend.command_count() on first undo/redo
        dirty: false,
        conflict_pending: false,
    })
}
Remove load() (lines 54-99), open_sqlite() (lines 108-124), open_json() (lines 127-131), empty() (lines 133-147).

execute() — lazy baseline (lines 199-259):
- Lines 205-209: Before capturing pre-execution snapshot, if baseline_snapshot.is_none():
if self.baseline_snapshot.is_none() {
    self.baseline_snapshot = Some(self.backend.snapshot()?);
    self.command_count = self.backend.command_count() as usize;
    self.undo_cursor = self.command_count;
}

undo() — lazy command count (lines 267-300):
- If self.command_count == 0 and undo_cursor == 0 at start, restore from backend:
if self.command_count == 0 {
    self.command_count = self.backend.command_count() as usize;
    self.undo_cursor = self.command_count;
    self.baseline_snapshot = Some(self.backend.snapshot()?);
}

save() (lines 422-445):
- Remove let Some(store) = &self.store else { return Ok(()) } guard (lines 423-425)
- Remove the entire body; replace with:
pub async fn save(&self) -> KanbanResult<()> {
    self.backend.flush().await
}

reload() (lines 398-410):
- Remove let Some(store) = &self.store else { return Ok(()) } guard (lines 399-401)
- Replace body with:
pub async fn reload(&mut self) -> KanbanResult<()> {
    self.backend.reload().await?;
    self.baseline_snapshot = None;
    self.undo_cursor = 0;
    self.command_count = 0;
    self.dirty = false;
    Ok(())
}

Remove or adapt:
- replace_store() method (line 391) — REMOVE (no more store field)
- store() getter (line 395) — REMOVE

Refactor: All callers of ctx.store() and ctx.replace_store() — grep and fix. The is_dirty(), mark_dirty(), mark_clean() (lines
 367-375) remain unchanged.

---
### Step 4 — StoreManager::make_backend()

Why: StoreManager::make_store() (lines 106-112) returns Arc<dyn PersistenceStore>. We need make_backend() that returns Arc<dyn
 KanbanBackend>, hiding the JSON vs SQLite construction difference.

Red — tests (inline in store_manager.rs):
test_make_backend_json_path_returns_json_data_store
test_make_backend_sqlite_path_returns_sqlite_store
test_make_backend_detects_sqlite_by_magic_bytes
  (write a real SQLite file without extension; make_backend detects it correctly)
test_make_backend_detects_json_by_content
  (write a JSON file without extension; make_backend detects it correctly)
All use TempDir.

Green — add to crates/kanban-service/src/store_manager.rs after make_store() (line 112):
pub async fn make_backend(
    &self,
    locator: &str,
    config: &AppConfig,
) -> KanbanResult<Arc<dyn KanbanBackend>> {
    if self.is_sqlite(locator) {
        #[cfg(feature = "sqlite")]
        return Ok(Arc::new(SqliteStore::open(locator).await
            .map_err(|e| KanbanError::Database(e.to_string()))?));
        #[cfg(not(feature = "sqlite"))]
        return Err(KanbanError::Internal("SQLite feature not enabled".into()));
    }
    let store = self.make_store(
        config.effective_storage_backend(),
        locator
    )?;
    Ok(Arc::new(JsonDataStore::new(store)))
}

Refactor: Reuse is_sqlite() (lines 56-66) and detect_backend() (lines 71-90) — no duplication.

---
### Step 5 — kanban_service::open_context()

Why: This is the single public entry point that all three apps will call. It replaces the
StoreManager::sync_backend_with_file() + KanbanContext::load() / open_sqlite() dance that is currently duplicated in
CliContext::load() (context.rs:17-33), McpContext::new() (mcp/context.rs:15-31), and App::new_with_store() /
App::open_sqlite() (tui/app/mod.rs:198-361).

Red — integration tests in crates/kanban-service/tests/open_context.rs:
test_open_context_json_end_to_end
  (init JSON file; open_context(); create_board(); save(); reopen; list_boards() returns board)
test_open_context_sqlite_end_to_end
  (same for SQLite)
test_open_context_auto_detects_backend_from_magic_bytes
  (create unlabelled SQLite file; open_context detects SQLite; CRUD works)
test_open_context_new_file_starts_empty
  (point at non-existent path; open_context(); list_boards() returns [])

Green — add to crates/kanban-service/src/lib.rs (after default_registry() at line 39):
pub async fn open_context(locator: &str, config: AppConfig) -> KanbanResult<KanbanContext> {
    let mut config = config;
    let sm = StoreManager::new(default_registry());
    sm.sync_backend_with_file(locator, &mut config);
    let backend = sm.make_backend(locator, &config).await?;
    KanbanContext::open(backend, config).await
}

Refactor: Nothing to delete yet — keep old exports until Step 7.

---
### Step 6 — Wire FileWatcher to backend.reload()

Why: Currently App::auto_reload_from_external_change() (app/mod.rs:2210) loads from disk by calling store.load().await
directly — it knows about the PersistenceStore. After this step, external change handling goes through ctx.reload() →
backend.reload(), which works for both JSON (clears cache) and SQLite (no-op, reads are live). The FileMetadata mtime/hash
path in JsonFileStore can be retired.

Red — TUI integration test in crates/kanban-tui/tests/:
test_external_json_change_triggers_reload_when_clean
  (open JSON ctx; write new board to file externally; simulate watcher event;
   assert list_boards() returns the new board without manual reload)
test_external_json_change_sets_conflict_when_dirty
  (open JSON ctx; create board (dirty=true); write file externally; simulate watcher event;
   assert ctx.conflict_pending == true)
test_external_sqlite_change_is_transparent
  (open SQLite ctx; insert row externally; list_boards() immediately returns new row — no reload needed)

Green — changes to crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:

auto_reload_from_external_change() (line 2210):
- Replace the current store.load().await call with:
async fn auto_reload_from_external_change(&mut self) -> KanbanResult<()> {
    if self.ctx.is_dirty() {
        self.ctx.set_conflict_pending(true);
        return Ok(());
    }
    self.ctx.reload().await?;   // delegates to backend.reload()
    Ok(())
}
- Remove store reference; no more PersistenceStore access in TUI

Refactor: Remove FileMetadata import from JsonFileStore if it's no longer used externally. The conflict detection logic (mtime
 + size + hash) inside JsonFileStore::save() can remain as an internal guard — it's not the reload trigger anymore.

---
### Step 7 — Unify App Initialization

Why: After Steps 1-6, KanbanContext::open() is the correct single entry point. The if is_sqlite forks in all three apps become
 dead code.

TUI changes (crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs and tui_context.rs):

TuiContext::new(store) (tui_context.rs:22-39) and TuiContext::from_context(ctx) (tui_context.rs:45-58):
- Collapse to single TuiContext::new(ctx: KanbanContext) -> (Self, Option<Receiver<Snapshot>>, ...):
  - SaveCoordinator::new(ctx.backend().needs_flush()) — replaces store.is_some() at tui_context.rs:31
  - If needs_flush(): create save channel and return Some(rx)
  - If not: return None

App::new_with_store() (app/mod.rs:198-286) and App::open_sqlite() (app/mod.rs:293-361):
- Replace with single App::new(ctx: KanbanContext, config: AppConfig) -> KanbanResult<(Self, Option<Receiver<Snapshot>>)>
- No more store_manager.make_store() inside App; App just takes a pre-built KanbanContext

CLI fork (app.rs:285-301):
- Remove the if is_sqlite { App::open_sqlite(...) } else { App::new_with_store(...) } block
- Replace with:
let ctx = kanban_service::open_context(&effective_file, config).await?;
let (mut app, save_rx) = App::new(ctx, app_config)?;
app.set_error_log(error_log);
app.run(save_rx).await?;

CliContext::load() (kanban-cli/src/context.rs:17-33):
- Replace body with:
pub async fn load(locator: &str, config: AppConfig) -> KanbanResult<Self> {
    Ok(Self { inner: kanban_service::open_context(locator, config).await? })
}
- Remove store_manager parameter — no longer needed

McpContext::new() (kanban-mcp/src/context.rs:15-31):
- Replace body with:
pub async fn new(data_file: &str, config: AppConfig) -> KanbanResult<Self> {
    Ok(Self { inner: kanban_service::open_context(data_file, config).await? })
}
- Remove store_manager parameter

Red — tests to write first:
// kanban-cli/tests/
test_cli_init_and_run_json_unified_path
test_cli_init_and_run_sqlite_unified_path
// kanban-mcp/tests/
test_mcp_list_boards_json_unified_path
test_mcp_list_boards_sqlite_unified_path
// kanban-tui/tests/
test_tui_context_single_constructor_json
test_tui_context_single_constructor_sqlite
  (assert save_rx is Some for JSON, None for SQLite)

Refactor — delete dead code:
- App::open_sqlite() (app/mod.rs:293-361) — entire method
- App::new_with_store() (app/mod.rs:198-286) — entire method (replaced by App::new())
- TuiContext::from_context() (tui_context.rs:45-58) — entire method
- resolve_is_sqlite() helper in kanban-cli (wherever defined)
- KanbanContext::open_sqlite() (context.rs:108-124) — already removed in Step 3
- StoreManager parameter from CliContext::load and McpContext::new call sites

---
What This Unlocks

- Startup is instant: open_context() = connection only. First render triggers first read.
- Staleness handled uniformly: reload() is well-defined on both backends.
- Remote backends trivial: implement DataStore + CommandStore + KanbanBackend. Each method = a network call. needs_flush() =
false (write-through). flush() = no-op.
- Pagination path clear: DataStore methods already take no limit/offset, but they can be extended. The lazy model means adding
 list_cards_paginated(column_id, page, size) doesn't require rethinking the initialization protocol.
- No SQLite special-casing: both backends implement the same interface.

---
Files to Modify

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         File                         │                            Change                            │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ crates/kanban-service/src/backend.rs                 │ Add flush, reload, needs_flush to KanbanBackend              │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ crates/kanban-service/src/json_backend.rs            │ New: JsonDataStore (lazy InMemory + JsonFileStore)           │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs                 │ Single open() constructor, lazy baseline, remove store field │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ crates/kanban-service/src/store_manager.rs           │ Add make_backend() method                                    │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ crates/kanban-service/src/lib.rs                     │ Expose open_context()                                        │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs │ Impl KanbanBackend lifecycle methods                         │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ crates/kanban-cli/src/app.rs                         │ Remove is_sqlite fork                                        │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ crates/kanban-cli/src/context.rs                     │ Call open_context()                                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ crates/kanban-mcp/src/server.rs                      │ Call open_context()                                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ crates/kanban-mcp/src/context.rs                     │ Call open_context()                                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs                     │ Collapse two constructors                                    │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs                 │ Remove from_context() vs new() split                         │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

---
Non-Goals / Deferred

- Pagination / streaming: DataStore API stays as-is for now. The lazy model is a prerequisite — do this first.
- StoreFactory::create() async wart (block_in_place): separate concern, leave for later.
- Command log protocol divergence (JSON envelope vs SQLite tables): JsonDataStore handles this internally. No protocol
unification needed.
- ID type aliases vs newtypes, TOCTOU in modify_graph: out of scope.

---
Verification

1. cargo test --workspace — all existing tests pass
2. cargo run -- init --name "Test" test.json && cargo run test.json — JSON works, first render triggers file read
3. cargo run -- init --name "Test" test.sqlite && cargo run test.sqlite — SQLite works, each view triggers DB query
4. Modify test.json externally while TUI is open → conflict banner appears
5. Modify test.sqlite externally while TUI is open → next read reflects new state transparently
6. MCP tool calls persist correctly for both backends (write-through for SQLite, flush for JSON)
7. Undo/redo works correctly on both backends after the lazy baseline change
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cards.title: Architecture: Unified Backends via True Deferred Reads

cards.description:
# Sprint assignment dialog: group sprints by status with completed sprints in separate section

## Status: ✅ Implemented — PR #245

PR: https://github.com/fulsomenko/kanban/pull/245
Branch: `KAN-406/sprint-assignment-dialog-group-sprints-by-status-with-completed-sprints-in-separate-section`
Plan file: `/home/max/.claude/plans/checkout-339-and-plan-parsed-pony.md`

---

## Goal (original)

The sprint assignment dialog showed all assignable sprints in a flat list, filtered to `Planning + Active` only by `Sprint::assignable`. This blocked valid retrospective workflows ("this bug was actually fixed in sprint 12 — let me record that") and gave no visual distinction between historical and currently-active sprints.

## Final shipped behaviour

The dialog now renders two headed sections:

```
  (None)                  ← unassign option
Active / Planned          ← yellow-bold header
  > sprint-15/yarara
    sprint-14/sphinx-centered
Completed / Ended         ← yellow-bold header
    sprint-13/a-catchy-spring   (green — Completed)
    sprint-12/moonshot          (green — Completed)
    sprint-11/a-new-year        (red   — Ended: Active w/ end_date in past)
    sprint-10/a-merry-christmas (green — Completed)
```

- **Active / Planned** = `Planning` status OR (`Active` AND `end_date` is `None` or in the future). Default white style.
- **Completed / Ended** = `Completed` status (green) OR `Active` with `end_date <= now` (red, "ended without formal completion").
- **Cancelled** sprints stay hidden — out of scope for this card.
- Section headers appear only when their section is non-empty.
- `j`/`k` skip headers and clamp at section boundaries. Selection starts at the `(None)` entry.
- The dialog scrolls (`Paragraph::scroll`) to keep the selected entry on-screen when the entry list overflows the viewport.
- The "current sprint" marker now uses a status-aware rule:
  - In the top section: bold green + ` (current)` suffix (existing behaviour).
  - In the bottom section: ` (current)` suffix only — the green/red status colour wins, no bold-current override. This lets a card assigned to a completed sprint still read as "completed" first.

## Path chosen

When the user pivoted from "Cancelled = ended" to "ended = `end_date` in the past, regardless of status", we settled on:

- **Cancelled stays hidden** (option (i) from the design discussion). Cleanest scope; the green/red distinction the user asked for is purely about Completed-vs-overdue-Active.
- **Status colour wins over current-marker** in the bottom section. Reasoning: the `(current)` suffix already identifies the card's current assignment; the colour is the more important signal in this dialog.

## Implementation summary

### Domain layer — `crates/kanban-domain/src/sprint.rs`

- **Refactored** `Sprint::is_ended` from `&self -> bool` (which used `Utc::now()` internally) to `&self, now: DateTime<Utc> -> bool`. Three call sites updated to pass `chrono::Utc::now()`:
  - `crates/kanban-tui/src/ui/sprint_detail.rs:83`
  - `crates/kanban-tui/src/ui/board_detail.rs:182`
  - `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:1472`
- **Added** `Sprint::for_assignment_dialog(sprints: &[Sprint], board_id: Uuid, now: DateTime<Utc>) -> (Vec<&Sprint>, Vec<&Sprint>)`. Returns `(active_or_planned, completed_or_ended)`. Each section sorted by `sprint_number` descending. Cancelled and other-board sprints excluded.
- **Removed** the now-unused `Sprint::assignable`. Fully superseded.

### TUI builder — `crates/kanban-tui/src/components/sprint_assign_list.rs` (new)

Pure-logic module the dialog renders/handlers consume. Re-exported via `crates/kanban-tui/src/components/mod.rs`.

```rust
pub enum SprintAssignEntry<'a> {
    Header(&'static str),
    None,
    ActiveOrPlanned(&'a Sprint),
    Completed(&'a Sprint),
    Ended(&'a Sprint),
}

pub const ACTIVE_PLANNED_HEADER:  &str = "Active / Planned";
pub const COMPLETED_ENDED_HEADER: &str = "Completed / Ended";

pub fn build_entries<'a>(sprints, board_id, now) -> Vec<SprintAssignEntry<'a>>;
pub fn next_selectable(entries, cur)  -> Option<usize>;
pub fn prev_selectable(entries, cur)  -> Option<usize>;
pub fn sprint_id_of(entry)            -> Option<Uuid>;
pub fn scroll_offset_to_show(selected, total, height) -> usize;
```

`scroll_offset_to_show` is stateless — given (selected, total, height), it returns the row offset that keeps `selected` inside the viewport. No persisted scroll state.

### Single-card render — `crates/kanban-tui/src/components/selection_dialog.rs`

`SprintAssignDialog::render` iterates `build_entries(...)`, dispatches each entry to `render_entry_line`, then applies `Paragraph::new(lines).scroll((scroll_offset_to_show(...) as u16, 0))`. `options_count` returns `entries.len()` so the surrounding selection plumbing keeps working.

### Multi-card render — `crates/kanban-tui/src/ui/dialogs/cards.rs`

`render_assign_multiple_cards_popup` got the same treatment for symmetric behaviour when multi-selecting cards and bulk-assigning to a sprint.

### Handlers — `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/popup_handlers.rs`

Both `handle_assign_card_to_sprint_popup` and `handle_assign_multiple_cards_to_sprint_popup`:

- `j`/`k` call `next_selectable / prev_selectable` and write the result back to `dialog_input.sprint_assign_selection`.
- `Enter` matches on `entries[selection_idx]`:
  - `None` → existing unassign command.
  - `ActiveOrPlanned(_) | Completed(_) | Ended(_)` → existing `AssignCardsToSprint` with `sprint_id_of(entry)`.
  - `Header(_)` → unreachable (selection never points at a header).
- Both handlers share a small `sprint_assign_entries(&self) -> Vec<SprintAssignEntry<'_>>` helper that builds the entry vec from current `(board, sprints)` state.

### Open-dialog gate — `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs`

Replaced the `Sprint::assignable(...).len() > 0` precheck with an `entries.iter().any(|e| matches!(e, ActiveOrPlanned | Completed | Ended))` check. The dialog is now reachable on boards that have only Completed sprints (the retrospective case).

### Current-sprint lookup — `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:2143`

`get_current_sprint_selection_index` rewritten: scans `build_entries(...)` and returns the first index whose `sprint_id_of(entry) == Some(card_sprint_id)`. Falls through to `0` (the `(None)` entry) when not found.

## Test coverage

- **11 inline domain tests** in `crates/kanban-domain/src/sprint.rs` covering `is_ended` semantics, the bucket logic across all four sprint states, sort ordering, and other-board exclusion.
- **16 inline TUI builder tests** in `crates/kanban-tui/src/components/sprint_assign_list.rs`:
  - `build_entries` always emits `(None)` at index 0; section headers appear only when populated; Completed and Ended share the lower section.
  - `next_selectable`/`prev_selectable` skip headers and clamp at section boundaries.
  - `sprint_id_of` for each variant.
  - `scroll_offset_to_show`: list-fits / selected-in-first-viewport / selected-below-viewport / clamps-at-last-full-viewport / zero-height / empty-list.
- **9 integration tests** in `crates/kanban-tui/tests/sprint_assign_dialog_tests.rs`:
  - Active / Planned and Completed / Ended headers render conditionally.
  - Completed renders green, Ended renders red (cell-level `Color` inspection).
  - `j` skips the Active / Planned header.
  - End-to-end assigning to a Completed sprint via the single-card handler.
  - Same for Ended.
  - Same for the bulk-assign handler.
  - Current-sprint marker preserves status colour in the lower section.
  - Scroll: 30 planning sprints, navigate to the oldest, rendered output still contains its name.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --workspace -- -D warnings` clean.
- `cargo fmt --check` clean.

## Cross-references / supersedes

- **KAN-1** (filter out completed from sprint assign) — superseded by this work; intent inverted (group, don't hide).
- **KAN-118** (unfilter tasks list on completed sprint) — partial overlap; close pending verification.
- **KAN-140** (filter out completed sprints from assign list) — superseded.
- **KAN-1**'s scope (and the dropped "Cancelled excluded for now" semantics) leaves the Cancelled-bucket question open as a follow-up.

## Commit log on the branch

```
e737c09 refactor(domain): remove unused Sprint::assignable and run cargo fmt
11a0882 feat(tui): scroll sprint assign dialog so the selected entry stays visible
d13d4f9 test(tui): assert card state via ctx.get_card after sprint assign handler
0bb5a60 feat(tui): map current sprint to its sprint_assign_list entry index
b541d1e feat(tui): include completed and ended sprints in sprint-assign dialog open gate
fecce66 feat(tui): wire sprint assign handlers to skip-aware nav and entry-typed enter
6279c73 feat(tui): render multi-card sprint assign dialog with grouped sections
42d8144 feat(tui): render single-card sprint assign dialog with grouped sections
9c1774f test(tui): add failing integration tests for sprint assign dialog
4cd5e80 feat(tui): implement sprint_assign_list build_entries and nav helpers
5dac94a test(tui): add failing tests and stub sprint_assign_list module
633b16e feat(domain): implement Sprint::for_assignment_dialog
4dab89b test(domain): add failing tests for Sprint::for_assignment_dialog and is_ended
ff10e76 refactor(domain): inject now into Sprint::is_ended for testability
006743b chore: add changeset for KAN-406
```
cards.title: Sprint assignment dialog: group sprints by status with completed sprints in separate section

cards.description: UpdateSprint currently blocks ALL card_prefix changes (Set and Clear) once cards are assigned. Clearing the prefix is arguably safe — it would fall back to board.card_prefix rather than storing a stale value. Decide whether Clear should be allowed post-assignment and add a comment explaining the decision either way.
cards.title: Consider allowing sprint card_prefix Clear even after cards are assigned

cards.description:
## Reproduction

Today \`kanban -V\` (and \`kanban --version\`) prints:

```
$ kanban -V
Error: kanban 0.4.0
commit: 7b56f00e9dff6f7e9a738c9e0eae4d8e1caef7dc
                                ← extra blank line
$
```

Two distinct bugs:

1. **\`Error:\` prefix** — version is being treated as a fatal error and
   sent to stderr with exit code 1.
2. **Trailing blank line** — output ends with two newlines instead of one.

Expected:

```
$ kanban -V
kanban 0.4.0
commit: 7b56f00e9dff6f7e9a738c9e0eae4d8e1caef7dc
$
```

…on stdout, exit code 0, single trailing newline.

## Root cause

### 1. \`Error:\` prefix

\`crates/kanban-cli/src/main.rs:6-12\`:

\`\`\`rust
#[tokio::main]
async fn main() {
    if let Err(e) = CliApp::with_defaults().run().await {
        eprintln!(\"Error: {e}\");
        std::process::exit(1);
    }
}
\`\`\`

\`crates/kanban-cli/src/app.rs:96\`:

\`\`\`rust
let matches = cmd.try_get_matches_from_mut(args)?;
\`\`\`

When the user passes \`-V\` / \`--version\`, clap returns a
\`clap::Error\` with \`ErrorKind::DisplayVersion\`.
\`try_get_matches_from_mut\` surfaces it as \`Err\`; the \`?\` in
\`parse_cli\` re-wraps it via \`anyhow\`; \`main\`'s generic error
handler prints \`\"Error: {e}\"\` to **stderr** with exit code **1**.

The same pitfall affects \`--help\` (\`ErrorKind::DisplayHelp\`).

### 2. Extra trailing newline

\`crates/kanban-core/src/version.rs:4-8\`:

\`\`\`rust
pub const VERSION: &str = concat!(
    env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\"),
    \"\\ncommit: \",
    env!(\"GIT_COMMIT_HASH\")
);
\`\`\`

clap's \`Display\` for \`DisplayVersion\` already appends \`\\n\`. The
\`eprintln!\` in \`main.rs:9\` then appends **another** \`\\n\`,
producing the trailing blank line. Both bugs collapse to the same root
cause: clap's exit-style errors should never reach \`eprintln!(\"Error:
{e}\")\`.

## Proposed fix

\`\`\`rust
// crates/kanban-cli/src/app.rs
let matches = cmd
    .try_get_matches_from_mut(args)
    .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.exit());   // help/version → stdout, exit 0
\`\`\`

…or, to preserve the \`anyhow::Result\` return type for \`parse_cli\`,
pattern-match on \`e.kind()\` and call \`e.exit()\` only for
\`DisplayHelp\` / \`DisplayVersion\`. Both shapes resolve both bugs
because \`clap::Error::exit\`:

- writes to **stdout** for \`DisplayHelp\` / \`DisplayVersion\` (no
  \`Error:\` prefix),
- uses \`print!\` (single trailing newline, no double newline),
- exits **0** for help/version, with the appropriate code for real
  errors,
- routes real errors (\`InvalidValue\`, \`UnknownArgument\`, …) to
  stderr with exit code 2.

## Build-path constraint

**Principle**: when \`GIT_COMMIT_HASH\` is unset (or \`git rev-parse
HEAD\` fails), the \`commit:\` line is **omitted** from the output
entirely. This is already correctly implemented via the
\`has_git_commit\` cfg gate in \`crates/kanban-core/build.rs:9-31\`
and the matching \`#[cfg(has_git_commit)]\` /
\`#[cfg(not(has_git_commit))]\` branches in
\`crates/kanban-core/src/version.rs\`. The clap-error fix must not break
this gate — both \`VERSION\` variants must continue to render correctly.

| # | Path | \`GIT_COMMIT_HASH\` source | \`has_git_commit\` | \`commit:\` line |
|---|------|----------------------------|--------------------|------------------|
| 1 | Cargo dev build inside the repo | \`build.rs\` falls back to \`git rev-parse HEAD\` | yes | included |
| 2 | Local Nix flake (\`~/fulsomenko/kanban/default.nix:31-33\`) | \`preBuild\` exports from \`flake.nix\`'s \`self.rev\` | yes | included |
| 3 | Nixpkgs (\`~/fulsomenko/nixpkgs/pkgs/by-name/ka/kanban/package.nix:19\`) | \`env.GIT_COMMIT_HASH = finalAttrs.src.rev\` | yes | included |
| 4 | **Unpinned build** — e.g. \`cargo install kanban\` from crates.io, source extract with no git tree | env unset + \`git rev-parse\` fails → fallback \`\"unknown\"\` | **no** | **omitted** |

So \`-V\` output should be \`kanban X.Y.Z\\ncommit: <hash>\\n\` in
paths 1–3 and just \`kanban X.Y.Z\\n\` in path 4 — both with a single
trailing newline and no \`Error:\` prefix.

## Acceptance criteria

- \`kanban -V\` writes to **stdout**, exits **0**, prints
  \`kanban X.Y.Z[\\ncommit: <hash>]\\n\` with a single trailing newline.
- \`kanban --version\` behaves identically.
- \`kanban --help\` writes to stdout, exits 0, no \`Error:\` prefix.
- Invalid args (e.g. \`kanban --no-such-flag\`) still route through the
  existing \`eprintln!(\"Error: {e}\")\` path with non-zero exit.
- All four build paths in the table above produce clean output. In path
  4 the \`commit:\` line is omitted entirely.
- Integration test asserting on the format (probably in
  \`crates/kanban-cli/tests/cli_integration.rs\` using \`assert_cmd\`)
  for both stdout content and exit code, against both VERSION variants
  (with and without the commit line).

## Discovery

KAN-3 (\`add -v flag\`) is the original feature request that introduced
\`-V\` / \`--version\` — already **Done**, empty description, unrelated
to this regression. KAN-38 (\`branch name doesnt reflected the used
version\`) is a separate concern about branch-name generation, not
version output. No existing card covers the bugs reported here.


## Review notes (PR #250)

Six review items raised on PR #250. Status of each:

### 1. \`e.exit()\` is \`-> !\` — \`parse_cli\`'s Result is now leakier than it looks

**Status: not addressed (theoretical).**

After the fix, the only path that can return \`Err\` from \`parse_cli\`
is \`Cli::from_arg_matches(&matches)?\` (clap-derive mismatch — basically
impossible in practice). Every other clap error terminates the process
directly via \`std::process::exit\`.

Consequence: any in-process embedder of \`CliApp::run_with_args\` loses
the ability to handle a malformed arg list gracefully; they get a hard
\`exit(2)\` mid-test. Today there are no such embedders (all integration
tests spawn subprocesses via \`assert_cmd\`), so this is purely
theoretical. If it ever matters, options are:

- Document on \`run_with_args\`'s docstring that this entry point may
  \`exit()\` for invalid args.
- Pattern-match \`e.kind()\` and convert real-error kinds back into
  \`anyhow::Error\` so the Result remains the source of truth for
  in-process callers.

### 2. Comment reads as if it explains a \`match\` that isn't there

**Status: addressed in commit \`16ce3a5\`.**

Comment now reads "\`e.exit()\` dispatches per-kind internally — no
\`match e.kind()\` needed here" so a reader doesn't go hunting for a
match arm that isn't there.

### 3. Failure messages on the long-version test are sparser

**Status: addressed in commit \`16ce3a5\`.**

\`test_long_version_flag_writes_clean_to_stdout\` now has the same
\`"got: {:?}"\` failure annotations as the short-version test for
stderr emptiness, the \`"kanban "\` prefix, the \`"Error:"\`
non-prefix, and the trailing-newline shape.

### 4. \`kanban_no_config\` is duplicated between two test modules

**Status: not addressed.**

\`mod no_file_tests\` (KAN-332) and \`mod version_and_help_tests\`
(this PR) each define a byte-identical \`kanban_no_config(dir)\`
helper. Five lines of duplication. Could lift to module level and
have both inner modules \`use super::kanban_no_config;\`. Defensible
either way — keeping each test module self-contained vs. DRY. Held
because (a) only two call sites today, (b) collapses naturally when a
third module appears.

### 5. \`--help\` text contains \`Usage:\` — assertion format-coupled

**Status: not addressed (acceptable risk).**

\`assert!(stdout.contains("Usage:"))\` is fragile in the sense that a
clap major bump could re-case it to \`usage:\` or \`USAGE:\`. The
project pins clap and "Usage:" has been the convention for years, so
the assertion is meaningful as written. Any clap upgrade should re-run
this test as part of the upgrade checklist.

### 6. No explicit assertion on exit code 0

**Status: not addressed (low value).**

\`.success()\` from assert_cmd accepts any successful exit on the
host platform. Could tighten to \`.success().code(0)\` for explicit
verification, but \`success()\` is the codebase convention and the
distinction is academic on Unix where success means exit 0. Held;
would just add visual noise.

## Summary

- Addressed in this branch: **#2, #3** (commit \`16ce3a5\`).
- Held as acceptable: **#4, #5, #6** (style / theoretical).
- Held with documented option: **#1** (in-process embedder concern;
  no consumers today).
cards.title: Fix `kanban -V` output: drop `Error:` prefix and stray trailing newline

cards.description: In migrate() (sqlite_store.rs), the SELECT from board_prefix_counters is executed unconditionally within the version < 2 branch. If the table were missing (e.g. partial prior run), this surfaces a confusing error. Consider a pre-flight table-existence check or wrapping the query in a let _ = ... to produce a cleaner fallback path.
cards.title: Guard SQLite migration board_prefix_counters query against missing table

cards.description:
## Problem

The JSON backend stores its undo command log inside `kanban.json` — the user's data file — and never clears it between sessions. This is a design mishap with two compounding issues:

### 1. Cross-session replay is inherently broken for JSON

`JsonBackend::supports_indexed_snapshots()` returns `false`, so undo works by replaying all stored commands against the baseline snapshot. This replay is only safe within a single session, where the baseline and the commands form a consistent chain. Across sessions, entities referenced by old commands (cards, columns, sprints) may have been deleted, archived, or renamed — making replay fail with errors like 'card not found' (see KAN-404).

SQLite does not have this problem: it stores indexed snapshots alongside commands, so undo loads a snapshot directly without replay. Cross-session undo on SQLite is safe. On JSON it is not — and the current architecture gives it no path to become safe.

### 2. The command log does not belong in the data file

`kanban.json` is a human-readable, hand-editable data file. Embedding session-operational state (the undo command log) into it:

- Bloats the file with history that is not user data
- Surprises users who inspect or hand-edit the file
- Means external edits can silently invalidate the command log
- Couples an ephemeral concern (in-session undo) to a durable one (persistent data)

## Proposed Design

**Undo on the JSON backend should be in-session only.** The command log should be treated as ephemeral:

- On **write**: strip the command log from the JSON before saving to disk
- On **read**: start with an empty command log regardless of what is in the file
- On **session start**: `initialize_undo_state()` finds count=0, sets baseline to current snapshot, cursor=0 — clean slate every session

This means pressing undo immediately after opening a JSON file does nothing, which is the correct and unsurprising behaviour.

## Relationship to KAN-404

KAN-404 fixes the crash symptom by clearing stale commands at session start and adding a rollback guard. This card addresses the underlying design: the command log should never reach the file in the first place. KAN-404 is a safe immediate fix; this card is the proper long-term resolution.

## Impact

- Removes the undo crash (KAN-404) at the source rather than with a guard
- Keeps `kanban.json` as a clean data file
- Makes the JSON and SQLite backends honest about their undo capabilities: SQLite offers cross-session undo, JSON does not
cards.title: Design: JSON backend should not persist command log between sessions

cards.description: Current model is compile-time library composition (CliApp::register_backend). Explore whether we can achieve 'install kanban, drop plugin, done' without requiring users to recompile or own a forked binary. Consider: inventory crate (linker-section compile-time registration, still requires recompile), libloading .so approach (fragile, no stable Rust ABI), or alternative packaging strategies. Determine what the Rust community recommends and whether this is worth pursuing vs accepting compile-time composition as the ceiling.
cards.title: Investigate true runtime plugin system for storage backends
Regression: undo crashes with 'card not found' and corrupts in-memory state on JSON backend
Introduce ViewSnapshot abstraction to enforce render/service boundary

cards.description:
## Context

The current changeset mechanism (`.changeset/*.md`) populates the CHANGELOG by dumping git commit messages verbatim. This produces changelogs like:

```
- refactor(service): replace blanket KanbanBackend impl with manual impls
- fix(domain): only upsert cards whose sprint logs actually changed in MigrateSprintLogs
- feat(persistence-sqlite): implement CommandStore for SqliteDataStore
```

These are useful for developers reviewing PRs, but meaningless to stakeholders reading a changelog. A user doesn't care that we "replaced blanket KanbanBackend impl with manual impls" — they care that "SQLite databases open faster and undo/redo is instant."

## Problems

1. Changelogs read like `git log --oneline`, not release notes
2. No categorization by impact type (features, fixes, deprecations, breaking changes)
3. Internal refactorings appear alongside user-facing changes with equal weight
4. No distinction between what was added, changed, removed, or deprecated
5. **Date headers are wrong** — changeset entries created days/weeks before a release get stamped with the release date, not the date the change was actually made. All entries under a version appear to have happened on the same day, losing the development timeline. The date should reflect when the changeset was authored (or the merge date), not when `changelog generate` runs.

## Proposed format

Changeset files should use a structured, stakeholder-facing format:

```markdown
---
bump: minor
---

### Added
- SQLite storage backend — use `.sqlite`, `.sqlite3`, or `.db` file extensions
- Command-replay undo/redo system with full history persistence

### Changed
- Undo/redo operations now return `KanbanResult<bool>` instead of `bool`

### Fixed
- SQLite databases missing `card_counter` column now auto-migrate on open

### Removed
- `SqliteBlobStore` and `SqliteStoreFactory`

### Internal
- `DataStore` + `CommandStore` trait architecture
```

## Date header fix

The changelog generator should derive dates from either:
- The changeset file's git commit date (`git log --format=%aI -- .changeset/foo.md`)
- A `date` field in the changeset frontmatter (e.g. `date: 2026-04-18`)
- The merge commit date of the PR that introduced the changeset

Not from the date `changelog generate` is invoked.

## Automated generation

The stakeholder-facing text can be generated automatically rather than written by hand. Two tiers: deterministic (no dependencies) and LLM-enhanced (local, zero cost).

### Tier 1: Deterministic generator (no LLM)

A shell script that parses conventional commit messages and categorizes them under Keep-a-Changelog headers. No rewriting — the commit messages appear as-is, but grouped correctly. This is the baseline that should always exist.

**Full implementation:**

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# generate-changeset.sh — deterministic changeset from conventional commits
# Usage: ./scripts/generate-changeset.sh [base-branch] [output-file]
#
# Reads git log from base branch to HEAD, parses conventional commit
# prefixes, and outputs a categorized changeset in Keep-a-Changelog format.

BASE_BRANCH="${1:-develop}"
OUTPUT_FILE="${2:-.changeset/generated.md}"

# Verify we have commits to process
COMMIT_COUNT=$(git rev-list --count "$BASE_BRANCH..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
if [ "$COMMIT_COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "No commits found between $BASE_BRANCH and HEAD" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Collect commits, stripping hash prefix
COMMITS=$(git log --oneline --reverse "$BASE_BRANCH..HEAD" | sed 's/^[a-f0-9]* //')

# Category buckets
ADDED=""
CHANGED=""
FIXED=""
REMOVED=""
INTERNAL=""

while IFS= read -r msg; do
    # Skip empty lines
    [ -z "$msg" ] && continue

    # Strip conventional commit prefix: type(scope): description
    # Capture type and the description after the colon
    if [[ "$msg" =~ ^([a-z]+)(\([^)]*\))?:\ (.+)$ ]]; then
        TYPE="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        SCOPE="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"  # includes parens, e.g. (domain)
        DESC="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"

        # Strip parens from scope for display
        SCOPE_CLEAN="${SCOPE#(}"
        SCOPE_CLEAN="${SCOPE_CLEAN%)}"

        # Format: "description" or "scope: description" for non-obvious scopes
        if [ -n "$SCOPE_CLEAN" ]; then
            ENTRY="$SCOPE_CLEAN: $DESC"
        else
            ENTRY="$DESC"
        fi
    else
        # Non-conventional commit — put in Internal
        ENTRY="$msg"
        TYPE="chore"
    fi

    case "$TYPE" in
        feat)
            ADDED="${ADDED}- ${ENTRY}\n"
            ;;
        fix)
            FIXED="${FIXED}- ${ENTRY}\n"
            ;;
        refactor|perf)
            # Refactors that remove things go to Removed if description
            # contains "remove", "delete", "drop"
            if echo "$DESC" | grep -qiE '\b(remove|delete|drop|rip out)\b'; then
                REMOVED="${REMOVED}- ${ENTRY}\n"
            else
                CHANGED="${CHANGED}- ${ENTRY}\n"
            fi
            ;;
        test|docs|ci|build|style)
            INTERNAL="${INTERNAL}- ${ENTRY}\n"
            ;;
        chore)
            INTERNAL="${INTERNAL}- ${ENTRY}\n"
            ;;
        *)
            INTERNAL="${INTERNAL}- ${ENTRY}\n"
            ;;
    esac
done <<< "$COMMITS"

# Determine bump level from commit types
if echo "$COMMITS" | grep -qE '^feat'; then
    BUMP="minor"
elif echo "$COMMITS" | grep -qE '^fix'; then
    BUMP="patch"
else
    BUMP="patch"
fi

# Check for BREAKING CHANGE footer or ! suffix → major
if git log "$BASE_BRANCH..HEAD" --format="%B" | grep -qE '^BREAKING CHANGE:|^[a-z]+(\([^)]*\))?!:'; then
    BUMP="major"
fi

# Build output
{
    echo "---"
    echo "bump: $BUMP"
    echo "---"
    echo ""

    if [ -n "$ADDED" ]; then
        echo "### Added"
        echo -e "$ADDED"
    fi
    if [ -n "$CHANGED" ]; then
        echo "### Changed"
        echo -e "$CHANGED"
    fi
    if [ -n "$FIXED" ]; then
        echo "### Fixed"
        echo -e "$FIXED"
    fi
    if [ -n "$REMOVED" ]; then
        echo "### Removed"
        echo -e "$REMOVED"
    fi
    if [ -n "$INTERNAL" ]; then
        echo "### Internal"
        echo -e "$INTERNAL"
    fi
} > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Generated changeset at $OUTPUT_FILE ($COMMIT_COUNT commits, bump: $BUMP)" >&2
```

**What this gives you:**
- `feat(*)` → Added
- `fix(*)` → Fixed  
- `refactor(*)` with "remove/delete/drop" in message → Removed
- `refactor(*)` / `perf(*)` → Changed
- `test/docs/ci/chore(*)` → Internal
- Auto-detects bump level (patch/minor/major) from commit types + BREAKING CHANGE
- Zero dependencies — pure bash, works in any environment

**What it doesn't give you:**
- Rewriting developer jargon into stakeholder language ("implement CommandStore for SqliteDataStore" stays as-is)
- Deduplication of related commits (5 test-update commits stay as 5 entries)
- Grouping sub-features under a single high-level bullet

**Example output from KAN-348 branch:**
```markdown
---
bump: minor
---

### Added
- domain: add serde derives to FieldUpdate and domain update types
- domain: replace Command trait with hierarchical Command enum
- service: replace HistoryManager with cursor-based undo/redo
- tui: update to use Command enum
- persistence: add command log methods to PersistenceStore trait
- persistence-sqlite: implement SqliteDataStore with real SQL queries
- ...

### Fixed
- persistence-sqlite: add card_counter column migration for older databases
- tui: eliminate O(n²) card cloning in render loop
- tui: skip redundant redraws when no state has changed
- tui: drain buffered key events before redraw to prevent input lag
- ...

### Removed
- domain: remove UndoPointId and undo-point methods from DataStore trait
- domain: remove InMemoryDataStore; use InMemoryStore everywhere
- persistence: remove command log methods from PersistenceStore
- persistence-sqlite: delete SqliteBlobStore, SqliteStoreFactory and helper modules
- ...

### Internal
- test(persistence): add command log contract tests
- test(persistence-sqlite): add SqliteDataStore contract tests
- chore: cargo fmt
- ...
```

This is already much better than a flat commit list — categorized and scannable. Layer Tier 2 on top for polished prose.

### Tier 2: Local LLM enhancement (zero cost)

Takes the Tier 1 output and rewrites it using a locally-hosted model. The input is small (~2-5KB) and the task is straightforward summarization/categorization.

**Candidate models (via Ollama or llama.cpp):**
- `mistral-small` (24B) — good at structured output, runs on 16GB+ VRAM
- `qwen2.5-coder` (7B/14B) — understands code context, minimal hardware
- `llama3.1` (8B) — general purpose, fast inference on CPU
- `phi-3` (3.8B) — smallest viable option, runs anywhere

**Enhancement script (wraps Tier 1):**
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# generate-changeset-enhanced.sh — LLM-polished changeset
BASE_BRANCH="${1:-develop}"

# Generate deterministic baseline
./scripts/generate-changeset.sh "$BASE_BRANCH" /tmp/changeset-raw.md

RAW=$(cat /tmp/changeset-raw.md)

PROMPT="You are editing a changelog for a terminal-based kanban app.
Below is a categorized list of changes generated from git commits.
Rewrite each entry to be stakeholder-facing:
- Merge related commits into single bullets (e.g. 5 test updates → one line)
- Replace developer jargon with user-visible impact
- Keep the ### headers and --- frontmatter exactly as-is
- Keep entries concise (one line each)

$RAW"

ollama run mistral-small "$PROMPT"
```

**Integration options:**
- Standalone script invoked manually before PR
- Claude Code skill (`/changeset`) that shells out to `ollama`
- Git hook (pre-push or post-merge)
- Nix shell includes `ollama` so it's available in the dev environment

## Implementation plan

1. **Phase 1**: Add the Tier 1 bash script to `scripts/generate-changeset.sh`
2. **Phase 2**: Add changeset format guidelines to CLAUDE.md and CONTRIBUTING.md
3. **Phase 3** (optional): Add Tier 2 LLM wrapper script
4. **Phase 4**: Fix date header bug in changelog generator

## Files to change

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `scripts/generate-changeset.sh` | Tier 1 deterministic generator |
| `scripts/generate-changeset-enhanced.sh` | Tier 2 LLM wrapper (optional) |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Add changeset format guidelines under a new "Changesets" section |
| `CONTRIBUTING.md` | Document the expected changeset format for contributors |
| `.changeset/TEMPLATE.md` | Optional template file |
| Changelog generator | Fix date derivation to use changeset authoring date, not generation date |
cards.title: Replace git-commit-based changelogs with stakeholder-facing release notes

cards.description:
## Root cause

On the SQLite backend, `delete_archived_card` was only deleting from the `archived_cards` table. The `cards` table still held the record because the archive operation had soft-deleted it there (set `archived = true`) rather than removing the row. When SQLite returned the card list, the row reappeared as an active card.

## What was done

### Fix: delete_archived_card (KAN-383 core bug)
- `delete_archived_card` now deletes from both `archived_cards` and `cards` in a single transaction.
- Added explicit WAL checkpoint after the delete so the removal is immediately durable without needing a separate save call.

### Fix: WAL never checkpointed on other mutations (systemic gap)
Every other mutation (create card, update card, move, archive, undo, redo) was leaving WAL pages unchecked until app exit. Two-phase fix:

**Phase 1 — plumbing:**
- Added `flush()` as a default no-op to `DataStore` trait (`kanban-domain`).
- `SqliteStore` overrides it to run `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`.
- `KanbanContext::save()` calls `flush()` on the SQLite path (where `store` is `None`) instead of silently returning `Ok(())`.
- `TuiContext` gained matching `flush()` calls in `execute_commands_batch`, `undo`, `redo`, and `with_snapshot`.

**Phase 2 — refactor (service-layer ownership):**
- Moved `flush()` into `KanbanContext::execute()`, `undo()`, and `redo()` directly, so every mutation checkpoints the WAL regardless of whether the caller is TUI, CLI, or MCP.
- Removed all backend-aware branching (`has_save_channel()` + explicit `flush()`) from `TuiContext`. The save coordinator remains for its intended purpose (debounced async JSON saves) but is no longer entangled with WAL management.
- TUI, CLI, and MCP now go through identical service-layer code for durability — no interface-specific divergence.

## Files changed
- `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs` — delete_archived_card fix; flush() override
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/data_store.rs` — flush() default on DataStore
- `crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs` — flush() in execute/undo/redo; save() fix
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs` — removed backend-aware flush logic

## Tests added
- `test_delete_archived_card_checkpoints_wal` (persistence-sqlite)
- `test_save_checkpoints_wal_on_sqlite_path` (service)
- `test_execute_checkpoints_wal_without_save` (service)
- `test_tui_execute/undo/redo_checkpoints_wal_on_sqlite_path` (tui)

## Branch
`KAN-383/bug-x-in-archived-cards-view-restores-card-instead-of-hard-deleting-it-sqlite`
cards.title: Bug: x in archived cards view restores card instead of hard-deleting it (SQLite)

cards.description:
## Where / Why / How

**Where:** kanban-persistence-sqlite crate - SqliteStore implementation

**Why:** SQLite WAL (Write-Ahead Logging) mode is enabled for concurrency, but changes remain in .wal file instead of being flushed to main database. This makes backups incomplete and can cause data loss if not properly checkpointed.

**How:** Add explicit checkpoint functionality:
1. Implement checkpoint method in SqliteStore that executes PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)
2. Call checkpoint on graceful shutdown (drop/close for SqliteStore)
3. Consider periodic checkpoints during long-running operations
4. Optionally expose as public CLI command (kanban checkpoint)

## Current State
- WAL mode enabled: .pragma("journal_mode", "wal")
- Changes written to docs/sock-wal
- No explicit checkpoint mechanism
- WAL file can grow large (multiple MB) without consolidation

## Implementation Scope

### Phase 1: Core Fix (20 minutes) ✅
**Files:** `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs`

1. Add checkpoint method (line ~250):
```rust
pub async fn checkpoint(&self) -> KanbanResult<()> {
    sqlx::query("PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)")
        .execute(&self.pool)
        .await
        .map_err(|e| KanbanError::Database(e.to_string()))?;
    Ok(())
}
```

2. Call checkpoint in PersistenceStore::save() (line 1630-1637):
```rust
async fn save(&self, snapshot: StoreSnapshot) -> PersistenceResult<PersistenceMetadata> {
    let domain_snapshot: Snapshot = serde_json::from_slice(&snapshot.data)...?;
    self.apply_snapshot_async(domain_snapshot).await...?;
    self.checkpoint().await.ok();  // <-- ADD THIS: flush WAL to main db
    Ok(PersistenceMetadata::new(self.instance_id))
}
```

**Impact:** Every save operation (card create, update, etc.) automatically consolidates WAL into main database.

### Phase 2: CLI Command (optional, 15 minutes)
**Files:** `crates/kanban-cli/src/main.rs`

Add subcommand:
```bash
kanban docs/sock checkpoint
```

### Phase 3: Graceful Shutdown (optional, advanced)
**Files:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/main.rs` or `crates/kanban-cli/src/main.rs`

Ensure checkpoint runs before exit.

## Example: Current Problem
```
Before fix:
$ ls -lh docs/sock*
docs/sock      229K  (main database)
docs/sock-wal  2.3M  (unwritten changes!)
docs/sock-shm  32K   (shared memory)

After fix (following any save operation):
$ ls -lh docs/sock*
docs/sock      231K  (consolidated)
docs/sock-wal  32K   (minimal)
docs/sock-shm  32K   (shared memory)
```

## Done Condition
- [ ] SqliteStore has public checkpoint() async method
- [ ] Checkpoint executes PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)
- [ ] Called in PersistenceStore::save() after apply_snapshot_async
- [ ] docs/sock main database consolidates from .wal file on every save
- [ ] .wal file size remains minimal after operations
- [ ] No data loss or corruption on checkpoint
- [ ] Tests pass (existing tests should pass as-is)
- [ ] Optional: kanban CLI has 'checkpoint' subcommand for manual triggering
- [ ] Optional: graceful shutdown ensures checkpoint before exit

## Blocker For
- lost-sock PR #6 (Nix flake + devshell) - depends on reliable kanban board persistence
cards.title: kanban-sqlite: Add explicit WAL checkpoint on app exit
Fix tracing output leaking into TUI display as raw text

cards.description: PR #226 creates commands with deterministic UUIDs for reproducible replay. Add an integration test that: (1) executes a sequence of commands and saves the command log, (2) reloads from the same command log baseline, (3) asserts the resulting board state is byte-for-byte identical. Cover both JSON and SQLite backends. Ensures replay is stable across sessions.
cards.title: Add deterministic UUID replay test for command log

cards.description: Closed as cannot-reproduce on 2026-05-10. Empirical probe via MCP against ./test/boards.sqlite (ext4, Linux): single writes and 5-write bursts all leave WAL at 0 bytes immediately after the tool returns; main .sqlite mtime advances and bytes are physically present. External sqlite3 reader sees writes immediately. The full chain mutating_op! → save() → flush() → wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) is intact across MCP, CLI, and TUI (all converge on the same KanbanContext::save → backend.flush primitive). If a real symptom resurfaces, file a new card with the exact filesystem and reproduction.
cards.title: bug: MCP server does not flush writes to SQLite db immediately

cards.description: SqliteStore uses WAL mode with a max-2-connection pool. Add integration tests that verify: (1) multiple concurrent readers can query without blocking, (2) writes are serialised correctly under concurrent load, (3) no data races or deadlocks under rapid undo/redo interleaved with writes. Use tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread") and spawn tasks via tokio::spawn.
cards.title: Add SQLite concurrent access tests (multi-reader, single-writer)

cards.description:
Currently the TUI and CLI fall back to `kanban.json` in the current directory when no file is specified. Change this so the user must provide an explicit file path (or have it configured). Update README.md Quick Start to remove the comment implying a default file is created automatically.

# Related

- KAN-330 targets the same root symptom (unwanted `kanban.json` creation on TUI startup) from the implementation side. One resolution may close both — sequence accordingly.

# Where the fallback lives

- `crates/kanban-core/src/config.rs:5` — `pub const DEFAULT_JSON_FILENAME: &str = "kanban.json";` is the root constant that propagates everywhere.
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/cli.rs:11-12` — clap declares the CLI `FILE` arg with `env = "KANBAN_FILE"`, so env-var fallback already works without the file constant.
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/app.rs:253` — when the CLI arg is `None`, falls through to `kanban_service::config::resolve_storage_location(&config)` which currently returns the default filename.
- `crates/kanban-service/src/config.rs:183` — the reasoning comment for the current default; needs to be rewritten or removed.
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:2237` — TUI also hardcodes `"kanban.json".into()` as a config_storage_location fallback.

# What 'configured' means

We already accept `KANBAN_FILE` as an env var (see cli.rs:12). The settings/config file path is the other already-existing mechanism — see `kanban_service::config::load` and the settings UI in `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/settings_handlers.rs`. The change is to:

1. Stop synthesising a default filename when neither the CLI arg, env var, nor config setting provides one.
2. Emit a clear error pointing the user at the three ways to specify a file (`kanban <path>`, `KANBAN_FILE=...`, or set `storage_location` in the config).

# Scope

- Remove or repurpose `DEFAULT_JSON_FILENAME`. If the constant is only used as the fallback, drop it; if it is referenced for migration tooling or examples, keep it but stop using it as a runtime default.
- Update `resolve_storage_location` to return a `Result` that errors when no source provides a path, instead of silently defaulting.
- Update CLI `app.rs` and TUI startup to surface that error as a clean, actionable message (no panics, no silent file creation).
- Remove the `"kanban.json".into()` fallback in `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs` so the TUI cannot reach the path-resolution stage with a synthesised filename.
- Update README.md Quick Start, the example in `crates/kanban-mcp/README.md:67`, and any inline tests that assume the default (e.g. `crates/kanban-core/src/config.rs:195`).
- Add a regression test: running the CLI with no FILE arg, no `KANBAN_FILE` env, and no configured `storage_location` should fail with the actionable error and must not create any file in the cwd.

# Done when

- Running `kanban` (or just opening the TUI) in a fresh directory no longer creates `kanban.json`.
- The error message lists the three valid ways to provide a file.
- README and MCP docs reflect the new behaviour.
- KAN-330's symptom (silent file creation on startup) is resolved either by this card or by the work in KAN-330 itself, not by both.
cards.title: Require explicit file path — no implicit kanban.json default

cards.description: fetch_cards_with_filter in sqlite_store.rs accepts where_clause: &str interpolated via format!() — footgun for future SQL injection. Replace with enum CardFilter { All, ByColumn(Uuid), BySprint(Uuid) } or a builder that maps to parameterized SQL fragments.

Address PR #208 (0.4.0) code review feedback

cards.description: KanbanContext::execute() clones full store snapshot before every command for rollback. Save only affected entities instead of full snapshot. Commands should declare which entity types they touch; save/restore only those.
