- Public HTTPS URL (GitHub raw is fine: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fulsomenko/kanban/master/assets/icon.png` once an icon asset exists).

Add to the `<metadata>` section:

```xml
<iconUrl>https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fulsomenko/kanban/master/assets/icon.png</iconUrl>
```

Blocker: the project does not yet have an icon. This step is gated on creating one (or pointing at an existing logo if one exists elsewhere). If the project doesn't want a dedicated icon, drop this point and accept the placeholder.

### 2. Tighten `VERIFICATION.txt` step 3 wording

Current text:

> 3. Confirm it matches the `checksum64` value in chocolateyinstall.ps1 of the published Chocolatey package (visible at https://community.chocolatey.org/packages/kanban).

The phrasing is slightly circular — "to verify the package, look at the published package." Moderators generally pass this, but it can be tighter.

Suggested replacement:

> 3. Compare the computed SHA256 against the checksum displayed on the package's chocolatey.org page (https://community.chocolatey.org/packages/kanban). The page surfaces the embedded `checksum64` value from `chocolateyinstall.ps1` under "Package files."

Functionally identical but clearer about *where* the user finds the comparison target.

### 3. Clarify `packaging/chocolatey/README.md` placeholder syntax

The README's local-validation example uses Bash-style `$VERSION` interpolation embedded inside PowerShell commands:

```powershell
(Get-Content kanban.nuspec) -replace '\$version\$', '0.6.0' | Set-Content kanban.nuspec
```

A Windows-native reader might wonder whether `$VERSION` is meant to be a PowerShell variable they should `$VERSION = '0.6.0'` first, or whether it's a literal placeholder.

Two cleanup options:

- **A.** Replace `$VERSION` (the interpolation) and `<sha>` (the obvious placeholder) with consistent `<VERSION>`-style placeholders, and explicitly show the substitution as `'<VERSION>'` literally in code. Tradeoff: less copy-paste-able.
- **B.** Add a `$VERSION = '0.6.0'` line at the top of the example block, then use `$VERSION` consistently. Tradeoff: more lines, but copy-paste-runnable.

Pick **B** — more useful to a developer trying to validate locally.

## Acceptance

- [ ] If an icon asset exists or gets created: `<iconUrl>` added to `kanban.nuspec`, points at a live HTTPS URL serving a square PNG.
- [ ] `VERIFICATION.txt` step 3 reworded to be non-circular.
- [ ] `packaging/chocolatey/README.md` example block updated to be unambiguously PowerShell with explicit variable initialization.
- [ ] Single small commit, `chore(choco): polish nuspec/VERIFICATION/README`.

## Out of scope

- Designing or sourcing the icon — separate work; gated on someone producing a 64x64 PNG.
- Restructuring the chocolatey package source layout — not needed.

## Related

- **KAN-537** — parent (Chocolatey umbrella).
- **KAN-538** — sibling, source of the files being polished.
cards.title: choco: cosmetic polish (iconUrl, VERIFICATION wording, README clarity)

cards.description:
## Problem

After KAN-400 the MCP tool input fields accept names (and sprint numbers) in addition to UUIDs. The `crates/kanban-mcp/README.md` tool tables still label the types as `board: UUID`, `column: UUID`, `sprint: UUID`, which is narrower than the implementation. Tool descriptions also still read "Get a specific board by ID" rather than "by UUID or name".

## Update

- Type labels: `board: UUID` → `board: String` (or document inline as "UUID or name").
- Tool descriptions: "by ID" → "by UUID or name" (or "by UUID, name, or number" for sprints).
- Batch tools previously documented as `cards: String` (CSV) are now `cards: Vec<String>` — update those rows too.

## Scope

Pure docs change in `crates/kanban-mcp/README.md`. No code changes. Confirm by diffing the README content against the schema descriptions in `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs`.
cards.title: Update MCP README: schema labels and descriptions reflect name-accepting fields

cards.description:
## Behaviour

In ColumnView and GroupedByColumn views, pressing `h` or `l` to move a
card across columns leaves the selector on whatever card was previously
selected in the **target** column, instead of following the moved card.
Equivalently, multi-selecting cards and pressing `h`/`l` to move them
leaves the selector in the wrong place.

## Expected

After `h`/`l`, the selector follows the moved card into its new column.
For the multi-select case, the selector follows the *first* selected
card (matching the existing implementation intent at
`crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs:535` —
`first_card_id` is computed precisely for this purpose).

## Why it happens

Same root cause as KAN-403, just at the move-card handlers:

- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs:404::handle_move_card`
  ends with `self.select_card_by_id(card_id);` at line 480.
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs::move_selected_cards`
  ends with `self.select_card_by_id(card_id);` at line 536.

By the time `select_card_by_id` runs:

1. `self.ctx.move_card(card_id, target_column_id, None)` has updated the
   model — the card lives in `target_column_id` now.
2. The column-selection navigation block (lines 487–514) has shifted
   `self.dialog_input.column_selection` to the target column in
   ColumnView, so the *active task list* is now the target column's.
3. But the target column's `CardList::cards` vec is the cached one from
   the previous `prepare_frame()` — it does NOT yet contain the moved
   card's ID.
4. `select_card_by_id` → `task_list.select_card(card_id)` searches
   `self.cards`, doesn't find it, returns `false` silently.
5. The next render-loop `prepare_frame()` refreshes the task list and
   `CardList::update_cards` re-applies the saved `current_card` (the
   previously selected card in the target column). The moved card is
   ignored.

In **Flat** view this bug doesn't manifest — the active task list is
global and the card is in it before and after the move.

## Reproduction

```rust
// Add to crates/kanban-tui/tests/stale_model_regression_tests.rs
#[test]
fn test_move_card_right_selects_moved_card_in_kanban_view() {
    let mut app = App::test_default();
    let board = app.ctx.create_board("Board".to_string(), None).unwrap();
    let _todo = app.ctx.create_column(board.id, "Todo".to_string(), Some(0)).unwrap();
    let _doing = app.ctx.create_column(board.id, "Doing".to_string(), Some(1)).unwrap();
    let _done = app.ctx.create_column(board.id, "Done".to_string(), Some(2)).unwrap();
    app.prepare_frame();
    app.selection.board.set(Some(0));
    app.selection.active_board_index = Some(0);
    app.focus.active = Focus::Cards;

    // Two cards in Todo so there's a "prior selection" in Doing's
    // post-move task list to clobber the moved card's selection.
    app.input.set("Anchor".to_string());
    app.create_card();
    app.prepare_frame();
    app.input.set("Mover".to_string());
    app.create_card();
    app.prepare_frame();
    let mover_id = app.get_selected_card_id().unwrap();

    // Move "Mover" right: Todo → Doing.
    app.handle_move_card_right();
    app.prepare_frame();

    let selected = app.get_selected_card_id().expect("a card is selected");
    assert_eq!(
        selected, mover_id,
        "selector must follow the moved card into the target column, not stay on a prior card"
    );
}
```

A symmetric test should exist for `move_selected_cards` (multi-select
+ `l`).

## Fix — option 1: mirror KAN-403 per-handler

Insert `self.prepare_frame()` right before each `select_card_by_id`
call at the two sites above. One-line change per call site, two total.
Mirrors the fix landed for `create_card` in
[KAN-403 PR](https://github.com/fulsomenko/kanban/pull/260).

```rust
// handle_move_card, around line 480:
self.prepare_frame();
self.select_card_by_id(card_id);

// move_selected_cards, around line 536:
if let Some(card_id) = first_card_id {
    self.prepare_frame();
    self.select_card_by_id(card_id);
}
```

## Fix — option 2: centralize in `select_card_by_id` (preferred)

See companion card (filed alongside this one) for refactoring
`crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:1454::select_card_by_id` to refresh
the task list internally. That eliminates every per-handler workaround,
including the one already in `create_card`. Both this card's bug and
KAN-403's are subsumed.

If the companion card lands first, this card is automatically
resolved — close it as a duplicate.

## Acceptance

- Test `test_move_card_right_selects_moved_card_in_kanban_view` (and a
  matching `test_move_selected_cards_right_selects_first_moved_card`)
  passes.
- The existing `test_create_card_selects_newly_created_card_when_prior_selection_exists`
  (added in KAN-403) stays green.
- `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`,
  `cargo fmt --check` all clean.
- Manual sanity: `kanban /tmp/test.json`, create two cards in Todo,
  select the second, press `l`. Expected: focus follows to Doing
  column, the moved card stays selected.

## Files modified

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs` | Insert `self.prepare_frame()` before `select_card_by_id` at line 480 (`handle_move_card`) and line 536 (`move_selected_cards`). Two lines added. |
| `crates/kanban-tui/tests/stale_model_regression_tests.rs` | Two new tests (single-card and multi-select). |

## Architectural principle

Selection / cursor logic stays inside the TUI app. The fix lives in
`crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs`; nothing leaks into
`kanban-service` or `kanban-domain`. Verified during KAN-403 — no
service- or domain-layer code references `select_card_by_id`,
`active_card`, or `selected_card`.

## Risk

Very low. Same shape as the KAN-403 fix that just merged. The added
`prepare_frame` call is idempotent — the next render-loop call is
redundant but harmless.

## Relationship to other cards

- **KAN-403** (PR #260): identical bug at `create_card`, now fixed.
  This card is the natural follow-up surfaced by KAN-403's plan
  ("`handle_move_card` may have a related bug in ColumnView").
- **Companion refactor card** (filed alongside): centralize the
  refresh in `select_card_by_id`. Implementing the companion subsumes
  this card.

## Commit shape

If going with option 1 (per-handler):

1. `test(tui): cover selector-follows-card on h/l move (red)` — two new
   tests, both failing.
2. `fix(tui): refresh task list before selecting moved card` —
   two-line insertion.
3. `chore: add changeset for KAN-NNN`.

If going with option 2: see companion card.
cards.title: fix(tui): selector does not follow card after move across columns in kanban view

cards.description: Sprint log fields (sprint_id, started_at, status) and edge fields (edge_type, direction) in upserts.rs use unwrap_or_default() which inserts empty strings into NOT NULL columns. On malformed JSON input this creates unloadable rows. Replace with required_str() for consistency.
cards.title: Replace unwrap_or_default with required_str in SQLite upserts

cards.description:
## Problem

`find_cards_by_identifier` (in `kanban-domain::search`) only matches active cards. Consequences for the user:

- `kanban card restore KAN-5` errors with `Card not found: 'KAN-5'` even when KAN-5 is in the archive.
- `kanban card delete KAN-5` (permanent delete of an archived card) hits the same gap.

Today's workaround: list archived cards (`kanban card list --archived`), copy the UUID, then `kanban card restore <uuid>`.

## Fix

Have the resolver search both active and archived cards. For ambiguity (a number that matches an active card and an archived one), prefer active.

Options:
- Add an `include_archived: bool` flag to `find_cards_by_identifier`.
- Add a separate `find_archived_cards_by_identifier` that handlers consult when the active lookup misses.
- Have `KanbanOperations::resolve_card_id` itself fall back to archived.

Affects both CLI and MCP because they share the trait resolver.

## Test

Add to `crates/kanban-cli/tests/cli_integration.rs::name_resolution_tests`:

- `test_card_restore_archived_by_identifier` — archive `KAN-5`, restore it by `KAN-5` (not by UUID), assert success.
- `test_card_delete_archived_by_identifier` — same idea for `card delete`.

## Context

Discovered while smoke-testing KAN-400. Pre-existing limitation, not a regression — the same `find_cards_by_identifier` behaviour predates this PR.
cards.title: Resolve KAN-N identifier for archived cards on restore/delete

cards.description:
## Problem
Save errors only logged, not visible to user. User unaware that changes weren't persisted.

## Impact
- User loses data unknowingly
- Thinks changes are saved when they're not
- No way to retry

## Tasks
1. Add error channel to App struct
2. Create unbounded error channel in run()
3. Update save worker to send errors to channel:
   - ConflictDetected errors
   - Save failures (disk full, permissions, etc.)
4. Add error_rx.recv() branch to tokio::select!
5. Update UI to display save errors prominently:
   - Red background for critical errors
   - Display in footer or top of screen
   - Show ⚠ warning icon

## Files to Modify
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/app.rs`
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/ui.rs`

## UI Requirements
- Save errors must be RED with warning icon
- Must be visible without scrolling
- Should persist until user acknowledges or new save succeeds

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Error channel created
- [ ] Save worker sends all errors to UI
- [ ] UI displays errors prominently
- [ ] Manual test: Disk full error shows in UI
- [ ] Manual test: Conflict error shows in UI
cards.title: Add User-Visible Save Error Notifications

cards.description:
## Problem

Most CLI errors print as a one-line JSON envelope to stderr so scripts can parse them. But errors that bubble up via `?` from a service method bypass the envelope and print as bare `Error: <message>` via `anyhow`.

Repro:

```
$ kanban /tmp/test.json column delete <SomeColumnWithArchivedCards>
Error: validation error: Cannot delete column <UUID>: column contains archived cards
```

Expected:

```
{"success":false,"api_version":"0.5.1","error":"validation error: Cannot delete column <UUID>: column contains archived cards"}
```

Pattern: resolution errors (from the resolvers in `KanbanOperations`) get the envelope because handlers explicitly call `output::output_error(&e.to_string())`. Service errors during the actual mutation reach `main` as `anyhow::Error` and get printed by clap/anyhow's default handler.

## Fix

Two options:

1. In each handler, wrap the mutating call in a `match` (or `.map_err(|e| output::output_error(&e.to_string()))`) instead of `?`. Mechanical but lots of touch sites.
2. Convert the top-level `main` to catch any leaked `anyhow::Error` and emit it through `output::output_error` before returning the exit code. One-touch, applies uniformly.

Option 2 is recommended. Make sure the exit code stays non-zero on error.

## Test

Add a CLI integration test that triggers a service-level validation error (e.g. `column delete` on a column with archived cards, or `sprint complete` on a non-active sprint) and asserts the stderr is a parseable `{"success":false,"error":"..."}` JSON object.

## Context

Discovered while smoke-testing KAN-400. Pre-existing inconsistency. Not a regression — exposed when the cleaner resolver errors made the inconsistency more visible.
cards.title: CLI: service-level errors should flow through the JSON envelope
extract generic RadioList component and build SprintPicker adapter on top

cards.description:
## Problem

`column reorder <COLUMN> --position N` sets the target column's `position` to `N` but doesn't renumber its siblings, so two columns can end up sharing the same position.

Repro:

```
$ kanban /tmp/test.json column create --board B --name TODO     # position 0
$ kanban /tmp/test.json column create --board B --name Doing    # position 1
$ kanban /tmp/test.json column create --board B --name Done     # position 2
$ kanban /tmp/test.json column reorder Done --position 0
$ kanban /tmp/test.json column list --board B
# Done  position 0
# TODO  position 0   <-- duplicate
# Doing position 1
```

Expected: setting Done to position 0 should shift TODO to 1 and Doing to 2 (or equivalent — every column on the board has a unique position).

## Fix

The reorder command lives in `kanban-service/src/context.rs` (`reorder_column`) and the underlying `Command::Column(ColumnCommand::Reorder(...))`. The renumbering pass that should follow a position write is missing.

Pattern to follow: card moves within a column already do this kind of compacting (see `card_lifecycle::compute_move_positions_*` in `kanban-domain`). The same shape applies to columns within a board.

## Test

Add an integration test in `kanban-service/tests/` (or extend `move_cards.rs`-style coverage):

- Create three columns at positions 0, 1, 2.
- Reorder the last one to 0.
- Assert positions are now `{Done: 0, TODO: 1, Doing: 2}` with no duplicates.

## Context

Discovered while smoke-testing KAN-400. Pre-existing service-layer behaviour.

cards.description:
## Problem

After KAN-400's `locked_session` → `locked_read` / `locked_write` split,
the `mutating_op!` macro in `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs` is the last
holdout. It's still used by only two call sites:

- `tool_create_board` (no resolution, single mutation)
- `tool_import_board` (no resolution, single mutation)

Both could trivially use `locked_write` with a one-line closure:

```rust
async fn tool_create_board(...) -> Result<CallToolResult, McpError> {
    let board = locked_write(&self.ctx, |ctx| {
        ctx.create_board(req.name, req.card_prefix).map_err(kanban_err_to_mcp)
    }).await?;
    to_call_tool_result(&board)
}
```

Two consequences of keeping the macro:

1. **Duplicated doc**. The `# Reload semantics and undo limitations`
   block appears verbatim on both `mutating_op!` and `locked_write`.
   Future changes to one are likely to skip the other; the docs will
   rot out of sync.
2. **Two ways to spell the same operation.** Tool authors have to
   choose between the macro and the closure form. The closure form is
   strictly more general (resolution-bearing or not) and the type
   system enforces correctness on it.

## Fix

1. Migrate `tool_create_board` and `tool_import_board` to
   `locked_write(&self.ctx, |ctx| { ... })`.
2. Delete the `mutating_op!` macro definition and its doc block.
3. Keep `read_op!` for the trivial reads that don't resolve
   (`tool_list_boards`, `tool_list_archived_cards`, `tool_undo`,
   `tool_redo`, `tool_get_card`'s UUID fast path) — that macro is
   genuinely shorter than the closure form and there's nothing to
   dedupe.

After: one canonical write helper (`locked_write`) with one canonical
doc block. Net delete in `lib.rs`.

## Verification

```bash
cargo test --workspace
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check
```

The existing 36 MCP integration tests cover both `tool_create_board`
and `tool_import_board`; no test changes required.

## Context

KAN-400 follow-up. Surfaced during PR #275 review-4. Strictly a doc
+ code-organization win; no behaviour change.
cards.title: Retire mutating_op! macro in kanban-mcp (kills duplicated reload-semantics doc)

   - Delete card A
   - Verify: graph no longer has blocker edge

2. test_delete_column_with_cards_fails
   - Create column with cards
   - Attempt to delete column
   - Verify: returns error, column still exists

3. test_delete_sprint_unassigns_cards
   - Create sprint with assigned cards
   - Delete sprint
   - Verify: cards no longer have sprint_id

4. test_update_nonexistent_card_fails

   - Verify: returns NotFound error

5. test_import_with_invalid_references_fails
   - Create JSON with card referencing non-existent column
   - Attempt import
   - Verify: returns validation error with details

6. test_unbounded_save_queue_never_drops
   - Queue 200+ saves rapidly
   - Verify: all saves complete, none dropped

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 tests written
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Tests use realistic data
- [ ] Error messages are checked, not just success/failure

cards.description:
DomainError had typed helpers (board_not_found, card_not_found, column_not_found, sprint_not_found, archived_card_not_found, tag_not_found). After PR #200, the command layer uses KanbanError::not_found(entity, id) instead.

Outcome (PR #310): all six helpers had zero non-test callers and were removed. wip_limit_exceeded is retained because real callers still exist (KanbanContext::move_card and the WIP-limit check in commands/mod.rs).

The helpers were pure byte-identical aliases for the same DomainError::NotFound value — the entity string only flows into Display and is never matched on, so they offered no typo-safety or programmatic value worth preserving. Removing them is a SemVer-breaking API change for any external consumer of kanban-domain, so the changeset is bumped 'minor', not 'patch'. If we ever want compile-time entity safety, the right design is replacing the &'static str entity with an Entity enum — that's a separate, larger card.
cards.title: Remove unused DomainError convenience methods

cards.description:
The domain model fully supports card relations — parent/child hierarchies, blocking dependencies, and relates-to links — through `DependencyCommand` variants in `kanban-domain/src/commands/dependency_commands.rs`. The service layer's `KanbanContext::execute()` already dispatches these commands. Nothing in the CLI or MCP touches them yet.

## What to expose

**Parent/child:**
- Set parent: `card relation set-parent <child> --parent <parent>`
- Remove parent: `card relation remove-parent <child> --parent <parent>`
- Create subcard: `card relation create-subcard --parent <parent> --board <board> --column <column> --title <title>`

**Blocking:**
- Add: `card relation add-blocks <blocker> --blocks <blocked>`
- Remove: `card relation remove <source> --target <target>`

**Relates-to:**
- Add: `card relation add-relates-to <card-a> --relates-to <card-b>`

**Query:**
- `card get` response should include parents, children, blockers, blocked-by, and related card IDs (sourced from `KanbanContext::graph()`)
- `card list --parent <card>` to filter by parent

## Implementation path

1. **Service layer** (`kanban-service/src/context.rs`): No changes needed — `execute()` already handles `DependencyCommand`. Add a `graph()` accessor (already exists at line 125) and expose relation lookups as helpers if needed.
2. **CLI** (`kanban-cli/src/cli.rs` + `handlers/`): Add a `card relation` subcommand group with the operations above. Wire through `DependencyCommand` via `ctx.execute()`.
3. **MCP** (`kanban-mcp/src/`): Add tools: `tool_set_parent`, `tool_remove_parent`, `tool_add_blocks`, `tool_remove_dependency`, `tool_add_relates_to`, `tool_create_subcard`. Enrich `tool_get_card` response with relation data from the graph.

## Unification concern

Currently TUI, CLI, and MCP each implement card operations independently with no shared contract enforcing parity. When a new operation is added to the domain (as happened here), it silently falls through all three surfaces. This should be addressed by:

- Defining a trait (e.g. `KanbanOperations`) in `kanban-service` that enumerates every user-facing operation
- Having CLI handlers, MCP tools, and TUI actions all implement or delegate through this trait
- Failing to implement a method would then be a compile error rather than a silent gap

This is a broader architectural refactor but relations are a good forcing function for it since they need to land in all three surfaces at once.
cards.title: Add parent/child card relation support to MCP and CLI

cards.description:
## Stakeholder Challenge

Both the CLI and MCP binaries silently fail when given unknown commands. Users receive no error feedback, making it unclear whether a command is invalid, a typo, or a file path. This undermines discoverability and creates a poor user experience — especially for the MCP server where tool availability should be explicit.

Example:
- `kanban init2` exits silently with no error
- `kanban-mcp unknown-command` treats it as a data file path
- No validation or rejection of unrecognized inputs

## Technical Problem

The CLI and MCP binaries each have separate, ad-hoc command parsing logic (clap for CLI positional args, custom argument handling in MCP main). There is no shared abstraction for:
- What commands are valid
- How to validate incoming requests
- How to respond to unknown commands

This violates SOLID principles (Single Responsibility, Open/Closed) and creates duplicate logic.

## Proposed Solution

Create a unified command parser trait with trait-based polymorphism:

1. **Extract `CommandParser` trait** in kanban-core:
   ```rust
   pub trait CommandParser {
     fn parse_command(&self, input: &str) -> Result<Command, CommandError>;
     fn available_commands(&self) -> Vec<&'static str>;
   }
   ```

2. **CLI Implementation** (kanban-cli):
   - Implements CommandParser
   - Uses clap for parsing
   - Returns CLI-formatted errors (stderr messages)

3. **MCP Implementation** (kanban-mcp):
   - Implements CommandParser
   - Validates tool calls against available tools
   - Returns MCP protocol error responses (JSON)

4. **Shared validation logic**:
   - Unknown command detection
   - Command existence checking
   - Common error types (UnknownCommand, InvalidArgs, etc.)

## Alternative Paths

### A1: Minimal fix (status quo + validation)
Add validation to each binary independently without shared abstraction:
- CLI: Add unknown command check in main.rs
- MCP: Add tool whitelist validation

**Pros**: Quick, minimal code change
**Cons**: Duplicate validation logic, no single source of truth, harder to maintain consistency

### A2: Shared error types only
Create shared  enum in kanban-core, but keep parsing separate:
- Both binaries use the same error types
- Parsing logic remains in each binary

**Pros**: Some code reuse, better error consistency
**Cons**: Still duplicate parsing logic, doesn't scale as commands grow

### A3: Monolithic parser (rejected)
Single parser in kanban-core that handles both CLI and MCP:
- One parser for all inputs
- Each binary wraps the output

**Cons**: Violates Open/Closed principle, mixing concerns, CLI-specific logic leaks into core

## Recommended Solution: Proposed

The **Proposed Solution** (trait-based) is recommended because it:

**Pros:**
- Single source of truth for command definitions
- SOLID principles (SRP, OCP, DIP)
- Easy to add new commands (implement trait once)
- Testable in isolation (mock implementations for tests)
- Clear separation of concerns (parsing vs. error handling vs. output formatting)
- Scales well as commands and applications grow
- Explicit about which commands are available to each application

**Cons:**
- Slightly more upfront design/code
- Requires refactoring existing CLI parsing
- MCP needs new validation layer

## Implementation Sketch

1. Define `Command` enum and `CommandParser` trait in kanban-core
2. Create `CliCommandParser` impl in kanban-cli using clap (refactor existing main.rs logic)
3. Create `McpCommandParser` impl in kanban-mcp with tool whitelist validation
4. Update each binary's main() to use the parser
5. Add tests for each implementation
6. Document available commands per application

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CommandParser trait defined and documented
- [ ] CLI parser validates unknown commands, returns clear error messages
- [ ] MCP parser validates tool calls, rejects unknown tools with MCP error response
- [ ] Both implementations tested independently
- [ ] No regressions in existing CLI/MCP behavior
- [ ] Documentation updated with command availability per application
cards.title: Unify command parsing across CLI and MCP with trait-based architecture

cards.description:
## Why are we doing this? (Stakeholder)

Undo/redo and the audit log are two separate stories with different lifetimes that have been conflated under one storage abstraction. The original goal — "stop holding 200 full state clones in RAM and remove the divergent code paths" — was met by the first attempt (PR #281), but code review surfaced a deeper smell: undo still works by *deriving state* from the command log (`apply_snapshot(baseline) + replay`). That treats the entity tables (CRUD) as recoverable from a log, which is the wrong model. SQLite is a CRUD store; commands mutate state via forward CRUD operations; undo should be a forward CRUD operation too.

This iteration replaces "replay from baseline" with **inverse commands**: every undoable command captures its own inverse at execute time using pre-state read from the DataStore. Undo runs the inverse against current state — no snapshot, no replay, no wipe-and-restore. The persisted command log becomes pure audit (foundation for KAN-36), and the per-session UndoStack becomes a separate first-class concept.

## Two Entities, Two Lifetimes

| Concept | Lifetime | Purpose | Where it lives |
|---|---|---|---|
| **CommandLog** | Append-only forever (persisted) | Audit of every action the system performed | Storage layer (`SqliteStore.command_log` table; JSON in-memory) |
| **UndoStack** | Per-session, transient | The user's "take back / reapply" affordance | `KanbanContext` (in-memory) |

Conflating them was the root cause of the snapshot-shaped operations: a single `CommandStore` trait had to serve both audit (any history) and undo (live, invertible). Splitting them lets each story have the right shape.

## Design Principles

1. **Entity tables are the source of truth.** Undo and redo are forward CRUD operations against current state, never `apply_snapshot(*)` to derive state from a log.
2. **Every undoable command has an inverse.** Captured at execute time using pre-state read from the `DataStore`. The inverse is a normal `Command` and is executed through the same `execute()` path.
3. **CommandLog records everything that happened**, including user-initiated undoes and redoes (which are themselves commands). It is the audit trail.
4. **UndoStack is per-session** and tracks `(forward, inverse)` pairs for the user's own actions in this session.
5. **Rollback on failed batch execution uses native transactions** for SQLite (sqlx `BEGIN`/`ROLLBACK`) and cheap in-memory snapshot for JSON. The per-execute `backend.snapshot()` call goes away.

## SOLID Rationale

| Principle | After plan |
|---|---|
| **SRP** | `KanbanContext` orchestrates only. `UndoStack` owns the per-session undo concept. `AuditLog` (renamed `CommandStore`) is purely append-only audit. |
| **OCP** | New `with_transaction` extension point on `KanbanBackend`. New backends slot in without `KanbanContext` edits. |
| **LSP** | Single invariant: undo executes the inverse against current state. Backend choice doesn't change undo semantics. |
| **ISP** | `AuditLog` exposes append/range-read only. `UndoStack` is a separate concrete type with its own minimal interface. |
| **DIP** | Undo semantics are uniform; transaction shape is delegated through the backend trait. No runtime capability checks. |

## Out-of-Scope Caveat

**Cross-session undo is deferred.** The user identified the underlying problem: two users editing the same file can produce a state where one user's undo would silently overwrite another user's change. That needs a proper conflict-invalidation design and is a separate card. For now, undo lives entirely within the current session. The persisted command log accumulates as audit history but doesn't drive undo across sessions.

## Plan (8 Phases on the Existing Branch)

**Phase 1 — Revert cross-session bits.** Drop the `apply_snapshot(empty) + replay` SQLite path. Both backends use in-session-only undo. SQLite `open` becomes O(1) — no in-memory log mirror. Keep the on-disk `command_log` table; it's the audit foundation.

**Phase 2 — Transactions in `KanbanBackend`.** New `with_transaction<F>` method. SQLite uses sqlx native transactions. JSON uses cheap in-memory state clone. `KanbanContext::execute` wraps the command loop. Eliminates `before = backend.snapshot()` from the happy path.

**Phase 3 — `UndoStack` + `Command::capture_inverse` scaffolding.** New `UndoStack` struct holding `(forward, inverse)` pairs. New `Command::capture_inverse(&self, store: &dyn DataStore) -> KanbanResult<Option<Vec<Command>>>` trait method (default `Ok(None)`). `execute()` captures inverses when available; `undo()` prefers the stack and falls back to the existing replay path when no inverse is captured. Two systems coexist temporarily.

**Phase 4 — Tier 1 inverses (16 simple commands).** `CreateBoard → DeleteBoard`, `MoveCard → MoveCard` (swap), `Update*`/sprint status changes/dependency adds. One commit per command (or close-related group) with a test verifying inverse-driven undo restores pre-state.

**Phase 5 — Tier 2 inverses (10 medium commands).** Single-entity capture: `UpdateBoard`, `RestoreCard`, `DeleteCard`, `RemoveDependencyCommand` (needs edge-type capture — small struct enrichment), `UpdateSprint`, `DeleteSprint`, etc.

**Phase 6 — Tier 3 inverses (9 complex/cascade).** Bulk capture: `ArchiveCards`, `AssignCardsToSprint`, `Delete*ByColumns/Board` cascade commands, `DeleteCardEdges`, `DeleteBoard` (heaviest), `ImportEntities`, `CompactColumnPositions` (needs explicit pre-position capture). `CreateSprint` with `auto_consume_name` needs board-state capture.

**Phase 7 — Remove replay fallback, drop `baseline_snapshot`.** All commands have inverses. Fallback is unreachable; delete it. Drop the `baseline_snapshot` field. Make `capture_inverse` required. The snapshot smell is fully gone from the undo path.

**Phase 8 — Audit log readiness polish.** Rename `CommandStore` → `AuditLog` (or heavily revise rustdoc). Add `actor`/`source` columns to `command_log` (NULL today, reserved for KAN-36). Document the two-entity model.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] No `apply_snapshot` call anywhere in `execute`/`undo`/`redo` paths. `Snapshot` lives only in import/migration paths.
- [ ] Every undoable command's `capture_inverse` is implemented and tested.
- [ ] SQLite `open` is O(1) — no log rehydration.
- [ ] Per-execute `backend.snapshot()` is gone; rollback uses `with_transaction`.
- [ ] `UndoStack` and `AuditLog` (or `CommandStore` reframed) are separate concepts with separate types and tests.
- [ ] Per-session undo behaviour is identical for JSON and SQLite.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` clean after every commit in the phased rollout.
- [ ] CLI / TUI / MCP code untouched.

## Out of Scope (Followup Cards)

1. **Cross-session undo** — needs conflict-invalidation design.
2. **Audit log UI** (KAN-36) — hooks land in Phase 8; the UI itself is separate.
3. **Multi-actor / multi-user** — `actor` column reserved but unused.
4. **Log compaction** — for very long audit histories. Only relevant once cross-session undo lands.

## History

PR #281 was the first attempt (pure command-replay with `apply_snapshot(baseline) + replay`). Code review surfaced that snapshot-shaped operations were still in the undo path. This iteration redesigns around inverse commands. PR #281 stays open; phased commits land on the same branch.
cards.title: Replace snapshot-based undo/redo with command-replay history system
Sprint completion leaves board.active_sprint_id stale when completing the active sprint

cards.description:
**Shipped in PR #311 (merged 2026-05-27).**

Cards can now be assigned to a sprint at creation time across the TUI, CLI, and MCP. The "create card in the active sprint" workflow collapses from two interactions into one, with the unassigned path preserved as the default when no sprint is requested.

## What landed

**Domain** (`kanban-domain`)
- `CreateCardOptions` gains `sprint_id: Option<Uuid>`.
- `CreateCard::execute` validates the sprint belongs to the same board, then calls `Card::assign_to_sprint` so creation + assignment are one undoable command.
- Undo deletes the card (which carries the sprint_id and sprint_log inline); redo replays the same command with the embedded timestamp.

**CLI** (`kanban-cli`)
- `kanban card create --assign / -a` accepts a UUID, sprint name, sprint number, or no value.
- Bare `--assign` resolves to the board's sole active non-ended sprint; errors clearly when zero or multiple match.

**MCP** (`kanban-mcp`)
- `tool_create_card` accepts an optional `sprint_id` resolved via `mcp_resolve_sprint_in_board`.
- Schema hints LLM callers to pass the active sprint id when one exists.

**TUI** (`kanban-tui`)
- New stateful `SprintPicker` embedded in the Create Task dialog below the title input.
- Pre-checks the sole Active non-ended sprint so Enter immediately confirms the assignment.
- Tab and Esc/Down toggle focus between title and picker; Up/Down/j/k navigate; Space toggles the checkbox; Enter submits.
- Focused element renders with a bright border.
- The old stateless picker was renamed to `SprintPickerView`; the bare `SprintPicker` name now belongs to the stateful component (composition: SprintPicker → SprintPickerView → RadioList).

## Post-review polish (6 follow-up commits)

The first review surfaced six concerns; all were addressed before merge:

1. `5e8dc8b8` `refactor(domain)`: `Card::update` and `Card::assign_to_sprint` now require an explicit `now` parameter. Removed the brittle `card.updated_at = now` reset in `CreateCard::execute` — the property is now structural, not bookkeeping.
2. `07177ad7` `fix(tui)`: When `reset_for_card_assignment` is called with a sprint id the filter excludes (e.g. a Completed sprint on an ActiveOnly picker), Selection now carries the id so bare Enter is a safe no-op re-assignment instead of silently doing nothing.
3. `55f44b28` `fix(tui)`: New `reset_for_bulk_card_assignment` opens with Selection::Unset so a bare Enter on the bulk-assign dialog is a no-op. Previously it mass-unassigned every selected card.
4. `9304653e` `chore(tui)`: `debug_assert!` when `entry_to_cursor` reaches a header — surfaces navigation regressions in tests instead of silently snapping to (None).
5. `2a3969d0` `refactor(tui)`: Removed unused `SprintPickerView::for_board`; tests migrated to `for_new_card`.
6. `b2b7d0c2` `fix(tui)`: `SprintPicker` tracks `bound_board_id`; new `selected_sprint_id_for(board_id)` returns None on mismatch. All three picker-consuming handlers (create-card, single-assign, bulk-assign) now refuse stale-board sprint state so a board switch between dialog open and submit can't leak a UUID into a write command.

## Test coverage

- 3 domain command tests + 2 timestamp invariants on `Card`
- 3 service integration tests including undo
- 6 CLI integration tests covering `--assign` variants
- 3 MCP integration tests
- 11 dialog interaction tests
- 8 SprintPicker unit tests (race-resistance, board mismatch, hidden-sprint, bulk-vs-single)
- 1 bulk-assign regression guard

`cargo test --workspace`: 1999 passing. Clippy + fmt clean.

## Follow-ups filed

- KAN-565 — Add redo determinism test for create-card-with-sprint
- KAN-566 — Make UpdateCard and AssignCardsToSprint replay-deterministic
- KAN-567 — Symmetrize SprintPicker stale-board guard with `explicitly_unassigned_for`
- KAN-568 — Drop unused params from `reset_for_bulk_card_assignment`
- KAN-569 — Rename board-switch defense test to reflect what it actually checks

cards.description:
## Stakeholder summary — Where, Why, How

**Where.** Card detail view (`kanban-tui`), Parents and Children relationship boxes. Reproduced today (2026-05-23) by opening a card with at least one parent, focusing the Parents panel (key `4`), and pressing `Enter` on the highlighted parent.

**Why.** This is a navigation regression. Users navigate the kanban graph by drilling into the parents/children boxes — that is the only in-TUI way to traverse the relationship graph without using `kanban-cli`. The interaction is silently broken: the detail view appears to stay on the same card while the parents box visually empties out, which looks like data loss to a user even though the underlying graph is intact. With KAN-504 just merged and parent/child edges now populated for ~32 cards in this board, this surface gets a lot more daily use than it did when the regression first landed.

**How.** Single-line fix in `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs` — the two relationship-navigation helpers update the index but not the UUID source of truth that the rendered card is keyed off. Adding the matching `active_card_id = Some(new_card_id)` mutation alongside each `active_card_index = Some(new_idx)` write restores the intended behaviour. A small regression test exercises the round trip to pin the fix.

---

## Engineering investigation

### Reproduction

1. Open the TUI against any board with parent/child edges (e.g. `~/notes/memes/kanban.json` after KAN-504 merge — card KAN-496 has 13 parents/children).
2. Navigate into card KAN-496 detail view.
3. Press `4` to focus the Parents panel.
4. Highlight the parent entry (j/k as needed).
5. Press `Enter`.

**Expected:** detail view reloads with the parent (e.g. KAN-483) — its title, metadata, description, and its own parents/children populate the panels.

**Actual:** the detail view still shows KAN-496. The Parents box visually empties out (header still says "Parents" but no entries shown). Pressing `4` again, `Enter` again etc. compounds the broken state.

### Root cause

The rendered detail card is resolved by UUID, not by index:

`crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:1462-1466`
```rust
pub fn get_card_for_detail_view(&self) -> Option<Card> {
    self.selection
        .active_card_id
        .and_then(|id| self.model.card(id).cloned())
}
```

This was deliberate — commit `9c79172 fix(tui): resolve card detail card by UUID instead of HashMap index` (KAN-364) introduced `active_card_id` precisely so that index-based card identification would never silently point at the wrong card when the model's internal ordering shifted.

But the relationship-navigation helpers were never updated to participate in the new contract. They mutate the index alone:

`crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs:1111-1156` (`navigate_to_selected_parent`)
```rust
self.selection.card_navigation_history.push(current_card_idx);
self.selection.active_card_index = Some(parent_idx);  // <-- only the index
self.focus.card_focus = CardFocus::Title;
let new_parents = self.get_current_card_parents();
let new_children = self.get_current_card_children();
self.relationship.parents_list.update_item_count(new_parents.len());
self.relationship.children_list.update_item_count(new_children.len());
return;
```

The "fallback to first parent" branch at lines 1138-1155 has the same shape (and same omission).

`crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs:1158-1202` (`navigate_to_selected_child`) is the mirror image with the identical bug at lines 1168 and 1189.

The Backspace return-from-history branch at line 344 has the same shape and same omission (`active_card_index = Some(previous_idx)` with no UUID update).

Other paths that set `active_card_index` and are NOT broken because they go through a code path that also sets `active_card_id`:
- `navigation_handlers.rs:493` — the canonical "open card from list" path; sets `active_card_id` directly.
- The "enter detail mode from card list" path drives both fields.

So the bug is specifically the three intra-detail-view navigation paths (selected-parent Enter, selected-child Enter, Backspace return).

### Why the symptom looks like "parents box clears"

After the broken navigation:
- `active_card_index` now points at the parent's row in `model.cards()`.
- `active_card_id` still holds the original card's UUID.
- `get_card_for_detail_view()` resolves to the *original* card → title/metadata/description are unchanged on screen.
- `get_current_card_parents()` reads off `active_card_index` (via the index → card lookup) → it correctly returns the *new card's* parents (often empty because most top-of-graph cards have no parents).
- `parents_list.update_item_count(0)` empties the rendered list.

The visual effect: card stays, parents box clears. Same mechanism produces the children-box clearing if Enter is pressed on a child.

### Offenders (file:line list)

| File | Line | Mutation | Missing |
|---|---|---|---|
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs` | 1122 | `active_card_index = Some(parent_idx)` | `active_card_id = Some(parent_id)` |
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs` | 1143 | `active_card_index = Some(parent_idx)` (fallback to first parent) | `active_card_id = Some(parents[0])` |
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs` | 1168 | `active_card_index = Some(child_idx)` | `active_card_id = Some(child_id)` |
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs` | 1189 | `active_card_index = Some(child_idx)` (fallback to first child) | `active_card_id = Some(children[0])` |
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs` | 344 | `active_card_index = Some(previous_idx)` (Backspace return) | `active_card_id = (resolve from `model.cards()[previous_idx].id`)` |

---

## Proposed solution

Update each of the four (or five — see Backspace below) call sites to mutate `active_card_id` alongside `active_card_index`. The cleanest form is to extract a tiny helper since the two fields are semantically one operation:

```rust
fn set_active_card(&mut self, idx: usize, id: uuid::Uuid) {
    self.selection.active_card_index = Some(idx);
    self.selection.active_card_id = Some(id);
}
```

Then call sites become:

```rust
// navigate_to_selected_parent — primary branch
self.selection.card_navigation_history.push(current_card_idx);
self.set_active_card(parent_idx, parent_id);
self.focus.card_focus = CardFocus::Title;
```

The Backspace branch at line 344 needs an additional read to resolve the UUID:

```rust
if let Some(previous_idx) = self.selection.card_navigation_history.pop() {
    let previous_id = self.model.cards().get(previous_idx).map(|c| c.id);
    self.selection.active_card_index = Some(previous_idx);
    self.selection.active_card_id = previous_id;
    ...
}
```

### Tests to add

Three integration tests in `crates/kanban-tui/tests/`:

1. `test_enter_on_parent_navigates_detail_to_parent_card`
   - Seed two cards with parent_child edge.
   - Drive into detail view of the child.
   - Press `4`, `Enter`.
   - Assert `app.get_card_for_detail_view().unwrap().id == parent_id`.

2. `test_enter_on_child_navigates_detail_to_child_card` — mirror.

3. `test_backspace_returns_to_previously_focused_card`
   - Seed a 3-card chain (A → B → C).
   - Detail into B, navigate up to A via Enter on parent.
   - Press Backspace.
   - Assert `get_card_for_detail_view()` returns B.

If feasible, also a property-style check: any code path that writes `active_card_index` writes `active_card_id` consistently. Could be enforced via a `pub(crate)` setter on `Selection` and replacing all bare field writes with the setter — but that's a separate refactor card.

### Alternatives weighed

| Option | Pros | Cons |
|---|---|---|
| **A — Add `active_card_id` update at each call site (proposed)** | Minimal diff (~5 lines + helper); preserves existing structure; pinned by tests. | Two fields stay separately mutable; future code can repeat the bug. |
| B — Make `active_card_id` the single source of truth, derive `active_card_index` lazily | Eliminates the duplicate-state class of bug permanently. | Larger refactor across `handlers/*.rs`; touches `Selection` struct shape; risks scope creep in a bugfix. |
| C — Encapsulate both fields behind a `set_active_card(id)` method, store as a single struct | Same ergonomics as B but no shape change; rename catches all call sites at compile time. | Still ~10-15 touched call sites; bigger PR than the bug warrants. |
| D — Keep `active_card_index` as the truth, drop `active_card_id` | Smallest theoretical diff. | Reverts KAN-364, reintroduces that bug. Off the table. |

Recommendation: ship A as the bugfix; open a separate card for B/C as a hardening refactor if drift between the two fields becomes a recurring issue.

### Acceptance criteria

- [x] `navigate_to_selected_parent` updates both `active_card_index` and `active_card_id` (both branches).
- [x] `navigate_to_selected_child` updates both (both branches).
- [x] Backspace return-from-history at detail_view_handlers.rs:344 updates both.
- [x] All three integration tests above pass against the fixed code.
- [x] Manual repro on `~/notes/memes/kanban.json` against KAN-496: Enter on a parent navigates the detail view to that parent, Enter on a child to that child, Backspace returns to the previous card.
- [x] Workspace gate clean (fmt / clippy / test).

### Out of scope

- Wider audit of every `active_card_index` write site outside the detail view (the documented-broken three are sufficient — other paths go through `navigation_handlers.rs:493` which already sets the UUID).
- Refactoring `Selection` to deduplicate the two fields — separate card if pursued.
- Any change to the underlying graph or persistence layers.

### Risk

Low. The fix is local, the tests pin the contract, and the new helper makes future calls naturally correct. The only subtle case is the Backspace branch, which needs to look up the UUID from the index it's restoring — that lookup uses the same `model.cards().get(idx)` pattern that already exists in the rendering layer.

### Suggested priority

Medium-high. Not a data-corruption bug but a daily-use feature that broke silently. The misleading "parents box clears" symptom makes it look worse than it is.

---

## Resolution (2026-05-25, PR #290)

Shipped a stronger fix than the originally proposed Option A. The investigation's "Alternatives weighed" table had Option C ("encapsulate both fields behind a single struct") — that is what landed, but with **private fields** on the inner struct so drift is structurally impossible rather than just policed by helpers. Plus three additional entry points surfaced during code review that the original investigation had not enumerated.

### Final shape

`crates/kanban-tui/src/app/selection.rs`:
```rust
pub struct ActiveCard {
    index: usize,        // private
    id: uuid::Uuid,      // private
}
impl ActiveCard {
    pub fn new(index: usize, id: uuid::Uuid) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn index(&self) -> usize { ... }
    pub fn id(&self) -> uuid::Uuid { ... }
}

// SelectionHub: a single field replaces the two parallel ones
pub active_card: Option<ActiveCard>,
```

Constructor requires both values; accessors return by value; mutation goes through whole-Option assignment. The compiler now catches the entire bug class at compile time. No `set_active_card` / `clear_active_card` helpers needed — the struct constructor *is* the helper.

### Additional offenders fixed beyond the original investigation

Code review surfaced three more handler sites with the same drift pattern that the original "Offenders" table missed:

- `CardListAction::Select` arm (sprint-detail Enter → push into CardDetail mode).
- `CardListAction::Edit` arm (sprint-detail `e` → push into CardDetail mode).
- The Backspace stale-index defensive branch left `active_card_index` populated when `previous_idx` no longer mapped to a card — fixed to clear `active_card` entirely.

### Layers shipped (commit-bisectable)

1. **Original Red→Green→Refactor** for the three documented paths via a `SelectionHub::set_active_card` helper.
2. **Folded in the three additional entry points** that code review surfaced.
3. **Collapsed the two parallel fields** into `Option<ActiveCard>` (~43 mechanical site updates, all compiler-verified). Helpers from layer 1 dropped as redundant.
4. **DRY + symmetry**: extracted `refresh_relationship_counts` (six-line duplication × five sites); introduced `RelationSide` enum + `navigate_to_related_card` so the parent/child mirror is type-level. `navigate_to_selected_parent` / `_child` collapse to zero-arg delegators.

### Test coverage delivered

8 inline tests in `handlers::detail_view_handlers::tests` (3 originally proposed + 5 added during the post-review expansion):
- parent navigation with list selection
- child navigation with list selection
- Backspace return with valid previous_idx
- sprint-detail Enter (CardListAction::Select)
- sprint-detail `e` (CardListAction::Edit)
- Backspace stale-index recovery clears `active_card` entirely
- parent navigation with **no** list selection (fallback path)
- child navigation with **no** list selection (fallback path)

### Follow-up

UX gaps surfaced during this work (back-nav discoverability, focus-restriction lift, breadcrumb, forward stack) tracked as **KAN-532**.
cards.title: Card detail: Enter on parent/child does not navigate to that card (active_card_id stale)

cards.description: with_transaction's default impl uses snapshot()+apply_snapshot() for rollback. SqliteBackend inherits this default, so the rollback path on a failed batch does SELECT * → INSERT * across every entity table — expensive. Override with_transaction on SqliteBackend with native sqlx BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK so the failure path is cheap. Also opens the door to making append_commands atomic with entity mutations on disk (currently atomic only in RAM via SessionCommandLog). Out of KAN-191 scope; came up in third-pass review.
cards.title: SqliteBackend: native sqlx transaction override for with_transaction

cards.description: card.sprint_logs is denormalised sprint history living on the card entity. KAN-191 had to add a synthetic RestoreCardSprintAttachment command just to round-trip it cleanly through undo (otherwise Assign/Unassign inverses would push new log entries instead of restoring pre-state, bloating the vec). The architectural fix: derive sprint history from the audit log (or a dedicated card_sprint_history table) rather than mutating the card on every assign. Removes the synthetic command and the field from Card. Came up in KAN-191 third-pass review.
cards.title: Move card.sprint_logs off the card; derive sprint history as a projection

cards.description:
## Where, Why, and How it Affects You

**Where:** Card detail view in the TUI -- any action you take on the currently-open card immediately after an external write lands (CLI update, MCP tool call, another TUI window saving).

**Why it breaks:** The board is sorted by UpdatedAt descending. When an external writer touches any card, the TUI file watcher reloads and re-sorts the list. The card you were viewing moves to a new position in the list. The TUI still remembers the old position (index) and uses it to look up your card. It finds whatever card now occupies that slot -- which can be a completely different card.

**Visible symptoms:**
- Press `e` on a card's Metadata section while the TUI has just auto-reloaded from an external write: the external editor opens the wrong card's JSON.
- Open the Manage Parents or Manage Children dialog in the same window: the eligible-card list is built relative to the wrong card, so cycle-detection excludes the wrong subtree.
- The parent/child relation counts shown in the detail sidebar can reflect the wrong card.

---

## Engineering Detail

### Root cause

`ActiveCard` carries both an index (`usize`) and a UUID. The index is correct at the moment the user navigates to a card. After an external reload, `KanbanContext` replaces the card list with a freshly sorted snapshot. The index is now stale. Every site that resolves the active card with `self.model.cards().get(active.index())` silently returns the wrong card rather than failing loudly.

### Offenders

All five sites are in `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs`:

| Line | Function | Consequence of stale index |
|------|----------|---------------------------|
| 238 | `'e'` key -- `CardFocus::Metadata` branch | External editor opens wrong card's metadata JSON |
| 980 | `handle_manage_parents` | Parent-management dialog built against wrong card; wrong ancestors excluded from cycle check |
| 1033 | `handle_manage_children` | Child-management dialog built against wrong card; wrong descendants excluded from cycle check |
| 1086 | `get_current_card_parents` | Sidebar parent count and list reflect wrong card |
| 1095 | `get_current_card_children` | Sidebar child count and list reflect wrong card |

Note: `return_to_previous_card_from_detail_history` (line 1129) has a similar pattern but is already in scope for KAN-520.

### Suggested fix

Replace every `self.model.cards().get(active.index())` with an ID-based lookup. The UUID in `ActiveCard` is stable across reloads; the index is not.

**Before (line 238):**
```rust
if let Some(active) = self.selection.active_card {
    if let Some(card) = self.model.cards().get(active.index()) {
        let card_id = card.id;
        // ...
```

**After:**
```rust
if let Some(active) = self.selection.active_card {
    if let Some(card) = self.model.cards().iter().find(|c| c.id == active.id()) {
        let card_id = card.id;
        // ...
```

Apply the same substitution at lines 980, 1033, 1086, and 1095. The pattern is identical in each case: swap `.get(active.index())` for `.iter().find(|c| c.id == active.id())`.

### Tests to write (Red first)

One test per offender verifying that after a reload that changes sort order, the function still operates on the originally-selected card (by ID), not whatever card happens to occupy the old index.

### Relation to KAN-520

KAN-520 fixes navigation (Enter/Backspace) not updating `active_card_id` at all. This card fixes a separate issue: the ID is correct but the index used for lookup is stale. Both stem from `ActiveCard` carrying an index that can drift; the long-term fix is to stop using the index for model lookups entirely and rely solely on the UUID.
cards.title: fix detail view card lookup to use id not index after external file reload

cards.description:
## Background

The repo's `.gitignore` does not currently exclude `.claude/`. This directory is created and populated by the Claude Code CLI when it operates against this project:

- `.claude/settings.local.json` — per-machine CLI settings (already globally ignored via the user's home gitignore, but vulnerable to a `git add -A` pulling it in if global ignores aren't applied).
- `.claude/worktrees/` — agent-spawned git worktrees, can grow to tens of megabytes per active session.

None of this belongs in the project repository:

- The settings file is per-machine and contains paths/preferences specific to one developer's environment.
- The worktrees are scratch space for parallel agent runs; they're already tracked as worktrees via `.git/worktrees/<id>/` (proper git mechanism), and the `.claude/worktrees/<id>/` paths are just symlinked checkouts.

Today nothing is *tracked* under `.claude/`, but a careless `git add .` would stage the entire tree. Adding `.claude/` to `.gitignore` is preventive hygiene.

## Scope

Add `.claude/` to `.gitignore`, with a brief comment for future readers.

Suggested location: after the `# IDE` section (since the Claude Code CLI is a developer tool in the same conceptual bucket).

Suggested diff:

```diff
 # IDE
 .vscode/
 .idea/
 *.swp
 *.swo
 *~
 .direnv

+# Claude Code CLI (per-machine settings + scratch worktrees for agent runs)
+.claude/
+
 # OS
```

## Acceptance

- [ ] `.claude/` listed in `.gitignore`.
- [ ] Brief comment explaining what `.claude/` contains.
- [ ] `git check-ignore .claude/worktrees/some-agent-dir` returns the matched pattern (verification that it works).
- [ ] No file under `.claude/` is currently tracked in git (sanity — `git ls-files .claude | wc -l` returns 0; verified pre-PR).

## Out of scope

- **Cleaning up the existing dangling worktrees** — orthogonal cleanup; file separately if desired.
- **Project-shared Claude configuration** (e.g. `.claude/agents/*.md`) — none currently exist. If you ever want some, the ignore pattern is broad enough that you'd need a `!.claude/agents/` selective un-ignore. Cross that bridge when it's needed.

## Why this matters

- A future contributor could accidentally `git add -A` and commit their worktrees, blowing up the repo size.
- A future contributor unfamiliar with Claude Code may not realize `.claude/` is tool-managed and could include it in a PR.
- The directory's contents are session-ephemeral; tracking them would be noise.

## Related

- No parent card; standalone hygiene fix.
cards.title: Add .claude/ to .gitignore (CLI tool scratch + per-machine settings)

cards.description: crates/kanban-service/tests/inverse_commands.rs runs every test only against InMemoryStore. delete_board_cascade.rs uses a dual-backend macro that covers JSON + SQLite. Migrate inverse_commands.rs (or at least the round-trip + cascade tests) to the same pattern so serialization round-trip and persistence-layer interactions for synthetic commands (RestoreSprintPool, RestoreCardSprintAttachment, SetArchivedCardsSprint) are exercised on real backends. Came up in KAN-191 third-pass review.
cards.title: Run inverse-command tests against JSON and SQLite backends, not just InMemoryStore

How can we align the tui and the mcp to implement the interface?
