cards.description:
**Files:** `crates/kanban-domain/src/filter/card_filter.rs:11` (trait), `crates/kanban-domain/src/operations.rs:10` (struct)

Two public items named `CardFilter` in the same crate — a trait (predicate interface) and a struct (data bag of filter options for `KanbanOperations::list_cards`). Consumers must disambiguate via path.

Rename the struct in `operations.rs` to `CardFilterCriteria` or `CardListFilter`. The struct is only used in the `KanbanOperations::list_cards` method signature so rename scope should be small.
cards.title: Resolve CardFilter naming collision

cards.description:
KAN-191 introduced three synthetic-only commands as escape hatches for undo of denormalised state: RestoreCardSprintAttachment (sprint_logs on card; tracked by KAN-476), RestoreSprintPool (sprint-name pool on board), SetArchivedCardsSprint (sprint binding on archived cards via cascade). All three reject capture_inverse and exist only inside inverse batches.

The pattern is a tell: the domain model has denormalised state that the command pipeline can't round-trip through ordinary forward commands. Audit each piece and decide whether to (a) remodel the underlying state as a derived projection / separate entity, (b) accept the synthetic-command pattern as the chosen escape hatch and document it as a sanctioned pattern, or (c) something else.

Came up in KAN-191 final review.
cards.title: Audit synthetic-only commands; consider remodeling denormalised entity state

cards.description:
`CONTRIBUTING.md` has drifted from the current architecture and from `CLAUDE.md`. A read of the file against develop tip turns up stale file paths, broken links, examples that no longer compile, and convention text that contradicts what every recent PR actually does. New contributors reading just `CONTRIBUTING.md` get a picture of the project that does not match reality, and reviewers end up correcting the same things by hand.

This card spells out each action point against the file as it exists at develop tip, with rationale, so a future implementer can apply the changes without re-deriving any of the reasoning.

---

## 1. Remove the `MVP-123/my-feature` branch example without replacing it

**Where**: line 281, "Development Workflow" code block, and any other prose in lines 277-282 that prescribes a branch-naming shape.

**Change**: drop the `git checkout -b MVP-123/my-feature` line. Replace either with a tracker-agnostic placeholder like `git checkout -b <branch-name>` or leave it out entirely. Remove any text that recommends a specific branch-naming convention.

**Why**: the kanban platform driving the maintainer's `KAN-NNN/...` branch names is currently single-player on the maintainer's machine and contributors cannot create cards in it. Inventing a different example like `feature/short-slug` would still impose a convention. Until kanban itself supports multiplayer, the cleanest answer is to let contributors name branches however they prefer and stay silent about format in the contributor-facing doc.

---

## 2. Remove the broken `CONFLICT_RESOLUTION.md` link

**Where**: line 216, "Multi-Instance Support" bullet under "Persistence Features".

**Change**: drop the parenthetical "(see [CONFLICT_RESOLUTION.md](CONFLICT_RESOLUTION.md) for data loss scenarios and limitations)", or replace with a one-sentence summary of the limitation inline.

**Why**: `CONFLICT_RESOLUTION.md` does not exist in the repo. The link 404s when read on GitHub and dead-ends anyone trying to understand the conflict model. There is an open card (KAN-156, High priority) to actually write the conflict-resolution doc. Until that card lands, the link is worse than the absence of one because it implies the doc exists.

---

## 3. Update PR title format guidance

**Where**: lines 368-371.

**Change**: replace `Use format: <branch-name>` with `Use format: <type>(<crate>): <description>` and note that internal commits may prefix with a card number (`KAN-NNN`) but contributors do not need to.

**Why**: the current guidance is meaningless because `<branch-name>` is a placeholder, not a format. Every merged PR in the CHANGELOG follows `KAN-NNN <type>(<crate>): <description>` shape. Contributors should follow the conventional-commit form and let the maintainer add the card prefix on merge if internal tracking warrants. This is consistent with how the maintainer already amends merge commits.

---

## 4. Update commit format examples to include crate scope

**Where**: lines 392-415.

**Change**: change the template from `<type>: <description>` to `<type>(<crate>): <description>` where `<crate>` is the crate name without the `kanban-` prefix (e.g. `tui`, `service`, `mcp`). Update every example bullet accordingly.

**Why**: every recent commit in the log uses a crate scope, and `CLAUDE.md` documents this as the convention. The current `CONTRIBUTING.md` examples (`feat: add sprint filtering to task view`) silently teach contributors to omit the scope, which the maintainer then has to fix on merge. Aligning the doc with practice removes that friction.

---

## 5. Update the test-naming pattern

**Where**: line 245.

**Change**: replace `Use descriptive test names: test_card_completion_toggle` with `Use the pattern test_<scenario>_<expected_outcome>` and give an example like `test_move_card_to_full_column_returns_wip_limit_error`.

**Why**: `CLAUDE.md` already prescribes this pattern (under the "Testing" section) and the existing test suite mostly follows it. The contributor-facing example uses a less-specific name and so undersells the convention. Aligning both docs makes the standard discoverable to humans, not just to AI agents reading `CLAUDE.md`.

---

## 6. Mention TDD as the project's working style

**Where**: insert near the start of "Writing Tests", around line 241.

**Change**: add a paragraph stating that the project follows Red-Green-Refactor TDD: write a failing test, make it pass with minimum code, refactor. Recommend it for any non-trivial change but do not make it a blocking requirement for one-line bug fixes.

**Why**: `CLAUDE.md` declares TDD mandatory, but `CLAUDE.md` is read by AI agents helping the maintainer. The contributor-facing doc currently says nothing about TDD, so external contributors have no signal that the project leans on the workflow. The pragmatic answer is: surface the practice to set expectations, but do not gate small PRs on it (otherwise the "Bug Fixes welcome" entry we just added in KAN-414 would ring hollow).

---

## 7. Fix the `Card::new` test example

**Where**: line 256.

**Change**: update the snippet to match the real signature, e.g. `let mut card = Card::new(&mut board, column_id, "Test".to_string(), 0);` with `board` constructed earlier in the test.

**Why**: the current example would not compile against today's `Card::new(board: &mut Board, column_id: ColumnId, title: String, position: i32)` signature. A contributor copying it into a test gets a compile error from the doc, which both wastes their time and undermines the doc's credibility for everything else around it.

---

## 8. Update all `kanban-tui/src/app.rs` references to point at `app/`

**Where**: lines 72-78 (module organization list), line 163 (`Update Application State in kanban-tui/src/app.rs`).

**Change**: replace `app.rs` with `app/` (or `app/mod.rs` where the exact entry point matters). For the module list, either omit the `app` line or restate it as `app/ - application-state submodules (mode, focus, selection, multi_select, view, persistence, etc.)`.

**Why**: `app.rs` was split into a directory of 14 sub-modules in v0.3.4 (KAN-224). Anyone following the doc to find application state ends up at a path that does not exist. Whatever guidance points there needs to point at the directory instead.

---

## 9. Refresh the TUI module listing

**Where**: lines 72-78.

**Change**: either prune the list to only the still-existing top-level entries (`events.rs`, `editor.rs`, `dialog.rs`, etc.) plus the major directories (`app/`, `handlers/`, `components/`, `keybindings/`, `state/`), or drop the listing entirely and link to `crates/kanban-tui/src/` in the repo.

**Why**: the current six-entry list misses the bulk of the actual tree (`handlers/`, `components/`, `keybindings/`, `state/`, `card_list*.rs`, `error_log.rs`, `markdown_renderer.rs`, etc.). Listing six out of twenty-plus entries reads to a newcomer as if those six are all there is. Either the list has to be honest about scope or the doc should defer to `git ls`.

---

## 10. Generalize the format-version claim

**Where**: line 215, "Persistence Features" bullets.

**Change**: replace `Automatic V1->V2 migration on load with backup creation` with `Automatic format-version migration on load with backup creation`.

**Why**: v0.4.0 added V2->V3 (KAN-341 card identifier redesign), and there will be more in the future. Pinning the doc to one specific version pair means the doc is wrong the day the next migration ships. Phrasing it as "format-version migration" describes the contract (any old version is migrated to current on load, with backup) without locking the wording to a moment in time.

---

## 11. Fix the publish-order list to include the JSON/SQLite backend crates

**Where**: lines 322-329.

**Change**: insert `kanban-persistence-json` and `kanban-persistence-sqlite` in publish order after `kanban-persistence` and before `kanban-service`. Update the surrounding count from six crates to nine.

**Why**: those two crates were extracted from `kanban-persistence` in v0.4.0 (CAT-260) and are part of the actual publish pipeline today. Anyone reading this section to understand release mechanics gets a six-crate picture that has not been accurate for months.

---

## 12. Expand "Adding New Features" to cover CLI and MCP

**Where**: lines 154-176.

**Change**: after the existing TUI-focused steps, add bullets describing how to expose the new feature through (a) `kanban-cli` via a clap subcommand and (b) `kanban-mcp` via a tool annotated with `schemars` descriptions. Point at `crates/kanban-cli/src/commands/` and `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs` respectively.

**Why**: the section as written reads as if the TUI is the only consumer of the domain layer. Since v0.3.0, the MCP server has held a 37+ tool surface at full CLI parity, and the CLI itself is the primary scripting interface. A contributor adding a feature without touching CLI/MCP is shipping it half-baked, and the doc currently does not warn them.

---

## 13. Document `aur-publish.yml` in the CI/CD section

**Where**: lines 533-547.

**Change**: add a third workflow entry between `ci.yml` and `release.yml`, briefly describing what `aur-publish.yml` does and noting that it runs inline inside `release.yml` plus is kept as a `workflow_dispatch` fallback.

**Why**: the workflow exists in `.github/workflows/aur-publish.yml` and was wired into the release path in v0.5.0 (KAN-397). The CI/CD section currently describes two of three workflows, which leaves a contributor unaware of AUR publishing entirely.

---

## 14. Update the release cadence wording

**Where**: line 308, "Release Cadence".

**Change**: replace `develop -> master releases at the end of the sprint` with something like `targeting roughly weekly releases from develop to master, batched whenever a meaningful set of changesets has accumulated`.

**Why**: the "end of the sprint" phrasing implies a fixed sprint-bounded cadence that the project does not actually follow. The maintainer's stated goal is roughly weekly releases, and the recent CHANGELOG shows event-driven batching (v0.4.0 to v0.6.0 inside about ten days). Stating the target plus the reality keeps the doc honest without overcommitting.

---

## 15. Add a brief MCP / backend pointer section (optional)

**Where**: new section near the bottom of "Architecture Principles", or under "Areas for Contribution".

**Change**: a short paragraph pointing at `crates/kanban-mcp/README.md` for MCP development, and noting that the `kanban-service` crate exposes `json` and `sqlite` optional features that select which backends compile in.

**Why**: contributors looking to extend the MCP tool set or to develop a new persistence backend currently have no entry point in `CONTRIBUTING.md`. A two-sentence pointer is enough to avoid having to write the full guide in the audit PR; the full guides can land in follow-up cards.

---

## Acceptance criteria

- Every file path, module name, struct name, and function signature referenced in `CONTRIBUTING.md` exists in the repo and matches the current shape.
- No example code snippet (Rust, shell, mermaid) describes a stale API.
- Commit and PR formatting guidance is consistent with what shows up in the git log and what `CLAUDE.md` says.
- Workspace structure, dependency graph, and publish order match the 9-crate reality.
- The doc includes pointers to how to develop against the CLI and MCP entry points, not just the TUI.
- The broken `CONFLICT_RESOLUTION.md` link is removed.
- The doc no longer recommends any particular branch-naming convention.

## Out of scope

- Restructuring the document layout beyond the targeted fixes above.
- Writing the full MCP / backend development guides (pointers only; full guides as follow-up cards).
- Reconciling `CLAUDE.md` and `CONTRIBUTING.md` into a single source. Separate decision.
- Re-introducing a branch-naming convention. Defer until multiplayer kanban makes a shared identifier scheme practical.
cards.title: Audit and refresh CONTRIBUTING.md for correctness

cards.description: SettingsViewProvider pushes 'e/Enter' inside Configuration match arm AND unconditionally pushes another 'e' at the bottom. 'e' appears twice in keybinding context for Configuration focus.
cards.title: Fix duplicate 'e' keybinding in settings provider

cards.description:
Investigation into how closely the codebase follows the conventions documented in `CONTRIBUTING.md` and `CLAUDE.md` (file length, modularization, code style, clean architecture and SOLID claims). Run via four parallel subagents against develop tip on 2026-05-19. Each finding below carries a file or trait reference so a follow-up implementer can act without re-deriving the analysis.

This card is a **findings document**, not a fix specification. Specific cleanup actions should land as follow-up cards, listed at the bottom.

---

# Headline summary

- **File length**: 26% of production files exceed the 300-line guideline; 9 production files are over 1000 lines, with the worst at 2975. The TUI and domain crates are the densest offenders.
- **Modularization**: layering matches the diagram in `CLAUDE.md` and no production code imports across layers it should not. Concrete `KanbanContext` leaks into upper layers as a type binding instead of a trait.
- **Code style**: `KanbanResult<T>` discipline is solid and `updated_at` invariants are honored, but task-ID references (`KAN-...`) appear in production source and tracing strings, oversized functions are concentrated in `kanban-tui/src/handlers`, and several `pub` types should be `pub(crate)`.
- **SOLID**: persistence layering, command-pattern undo, and the upper-layer facade pattern are real strengths. `KanbanOperations` is a 51-method god-trait, `KanbanContext` is a 1317-line god-object, `Command` is a closed enum that violates OCP, and `StoreManager::make_backend` hard-codes a SQLite branch that bypasses its own registry, partly invalidating the documented "pluggable backend" claim.

---

# 1. File length compliance

Source: full `wc -l` sweep over `crates/**/*.rs`, separating production from `tests/`.

**Distribution (production, 208 files total)**

| Bucket | Count | % |
|---|---|---|
| < 300 lines | 154 | 74.0% |
| 300 to 500 | 26 | 12.5% |
| 500 to 1000 | 19 | 9.1% |
| > 1000 | 9 | 4.3% |

**Per-crate offenders over 300 lines**

| Crate | Production files | Over 300 |
|---|---|---|
| `kanban-tui` | 87 | 19 |
| `kanban-domain` | 40 | 17 |
| `kanban-service` | 22 | 5 |
| `kanban-cli` | 13 | 3 |
| `kanban-core` | 16 | 4 |
| `kanban-persistence-json` | 8 | 2 |
| `kanban-mcp` | 4 | 2 |
| `kanban-persistence` | 16 | 1 |
| `kanban-persistence-sqlite` | 2 | 1 |

**Top 15 production offenders**

| Lines | Path | Type |
|---|---|---|
| 2975 | `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs` | mod.rs |
| 1811 | `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs` | leaf |
| 1593 | `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs` | lib.rs |
| 1391 | `crates/kanban-domain/src/in_memory_store.rs` | leaf |
| 1317 | `crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs` | leaf |
| 1254 | `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs` | leaf |
| 1195 | `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/card_commands.rs` | leaf |
| 1100 | `crates/kanban-service/src/config.rs` | leaf |
| 1020 | `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/navigation_handlers.rs` | leaf |
| 997 | `crates/kanban-domain/src/search/mod.rs` | mod.rs |
| 995 | `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/sprint_commands.rs` | leaf |
| 859 | `crates/kanban-domain/src/card_lifecycle.rs` | leaf |
| 832 | `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs` | leaf |
| 784 | `crates/kanban-domain/src/board.rs` | leaf |
| 742 | `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/board_commands.rs` | leaf |

**Highest-payoff decomposition targets** (leaf modules, not entry points):

1. `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs` (1811) - split by aggregate (board, card, sprint, graph, command log).
2. `crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs` (1317) - factor out undo bookkeeping, dirty/conflict state, and batch helpers (see SOLID findings).
3. `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs` (1254) and `card_handlers.rs` (832) - split by entity.
4. `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/card_commands.rs` (1195) and `sprint_commands.rs` (995) - distribute across lifecycle, state, relationship concerns.
5. `crates/kanban-service/src/config.rs` (1100) - separate configuration value handling from persistence orchestration.

The `app/mod.rs` (2975) is a special case: after KAN-224 split `app.rs` into a directory of sub-modules, `mod.rs` itself remained a giant aggregator. Decomposition there is harder because the `App` struct ties everything together; suggested approach is incremental extraction of related method clusters into the existing sub-modules.

---

# 2. Modularization and dependency flow

Source: declared `Cargo.toml` deps plus `use kanban_*` imports across `crates/**/src/`.

**Compliance with documented diagram**: high. No production source under `crates/<x>/src/` imports a layer it should not. Test files under `tests/` reach into concrete backends, which is expected.

**Per-crate declared deps** (production only):

| Crate | Internal deps | Matches diagram? |
|---|---|---|
| `kanban-core` | none | yes |
| `kanban-domain` | `core` | yes |
| `kanban-persistence` | `core`, `domain` | yes |
| `kanban-persistence-json` | `per`, `core`, `domain` | yes |
| `kanban-persistence-sqlite` | `per`, `core`, `domain` | yes |
| `kanban-service` | `core`, `domain`, `per`, `json` (optional), `sqlite` (optional) | yes |
| `kanban-tui` | `core`, `domain`, `per`, `svc` | yes |
| `kanban-cli` | `core`, `domain`, `per`, `svc`, `tui` (optional) | yes |
| `kanban-mcp` | `core`, `domain`, `per`, `svc` | yes |

**Concerns**:

- `kanban-tui` declares a direct production dep on `kanban-persistence`. Inspection shows TUI uses persistence types transitively via `kanban-service`, so the explicit edge may be wider than necessary. Either narrow the dep or have `kanban-service` re-export the few persistence types TUI needs.
- `kanban-cli` and `kanban-mcp` import the concrete `KanbanContext` (`crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs:9`, `crates/kanban-mcp/src/context.rs:7`, `crates/kanban-cli/src/context.rs:7`). The trait `KanbanOperations` is already implemented on `KanbanContext` (`crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs:701`), so frontends could hold a `dyn KanbanOperations` or a generic. They currently do not.

---

# 3. Code style

Source: deep read of 8 representative production files spread across the crates.

**Solid patterns observed**:

- `KanbanResult<T>` discipline across domain, service, persistence layers; `anyhow` correctly confined to `kanban-cli` per CLAUDE.md.
- Trait delegation hygiene in `crates/kanban-mcp/src/context.rs` and `crates/kanban-service/src/sqlite_backend.rs` (1-3 line delegations).
- `updated_at` is bumped on every mutating method in `crates/kanban-domain/src/card.rs`.
- Atomic-writer plus envelope-versioning in `crates/kanban-persistence-json/src/json_file_store.rs` with explanatory test docstrings.

**Recurring issues**:

1. **Task-ID references in production code and tracing strings**. CLAUDE.md says comments should not reference the current task, fix, or callers. Occurrences:
   - `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs:260-265` (KAN-405, KAN-191 in code paragraph).
   - `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs:307-309,328-329,338-342,352-353` (more KAN-191/KAN-405 references).
   - `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs:404` ("Command log (audit foundation; not yet wired through SqliteBackend)").
   - `crates/kanban-persistence-json/src/json_file_store.rs:25-28,188,204,261,291-294,333-336,350-353` ("pre-KAN-405").
   - `crates/kanban-service/src/sqlite_backend.rs:16-18,173-174` ("not yet wired through this backend", "separate piece of work").
   - `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs:388-389` (KAN-403).

2. **Oversized functions**:
   - `crates/kanban-cli/src/app.rs:236` `run_with_args` ~120 lines.
   - `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs:303` `create_card` ~91 lines, `:404` `handle_move_card` ~78 lines, `:773` `handle_manage_children_from_list` ~59 lines.
   - `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs:501` `write_board_with_conn` ~82 lines, `:604` `write_card_with_conn` ~60 lines, `:808` `apply_snapshot_async` ~64 lines.
   - `crates/kanban-persistence-json/src/json_file_store.rs:218` `save` ~52 lines (just over).
   - `crates/kanban-tui/src/editor.rs:53` `edit_in_external_editor` ~55 lines.

3. **Self-evident "what" comments**:
   - `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs:43` "Exit selection mode (keep selections)", `:46` "Enter selection mode and select current card", `:596` "Try to find a card in the same column", `:611` "Select the last remaining card in the column", `:776,784,794,798,815,820,824` step-by-step narrative inside `handle_manage_children_from_list`.
   - `crates/kanban-persistence-json/src/json_file_store.rs:218-255` five "what" comments inside `save`.

4. **Leaky `pub` surface**:
   - `crates/kanban-domain/src/card.rs:60-79` `Card` struct fields are all `pub`. With setter methods that bump `updated_at`, direct field mutation lets callers bypass the timestamp invariant. Same applies to `CardSummary` at `:82-99`.
   - `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs:392` `pub fn pool(&self) -> &Pool<Sqlite>` exposes the sqlx pool publicly.
   - `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs:408-468` `append_command_batch`, `load_all_command_batches`, `truncate_command_log_after`, `shift_command_log` are documented as "not yet wired through" yet exposed publicly.
   - `crates/kanban-persistence-json/src/json_file_store.rs:29-83` `JsonEnvelope` and its methods are public but only used inside the crate.

5. **Type-alias IDs instead of newtypes**: `crates/kanban-domain/src/card.rs:9` has `pub type CardId = Uuid;`. Per the project rule "Newtype pattern (BoardId, CardId, ColumnId)", these should be tuple structs with `From`/`Into` impls so a `BoardId` cannot be passed where a `CardId` is expected. Same shape likely for `BoardId`, `ColumnId`.

6. **Parameter style ergonomics**:
   - `crates/kanban-domain/src/card.rs:122,171,176` `Card::new`, `update_title`, `update_description` take `String` instead of `&str` or `impl Into<String>`. Callers consistently `.to_string()`.
   - Other places that take `String` are usually constrained by trait signatures (e.g. `KanbanOperations` requires `String` parameters), so changing them is a trait-level decision, not a leaf-level edit.

---

# 4. Clean architecture and SOLID

Source: full read of `KanbanOperations`, `DataStore`, `CommandStore`, `KanbanBackend`, `PersistenceStore`, `StoreFactory`, `StoreRegistry`, `Command`, `MigrationStrategy`, `ConflictResolver`, plus `KanbanContext` and `StoreManager`.

### Per-crate SRP

| Crate | Verdict | Note |
|---|---|---|
| `kanban-core` | clean | tiny shared types |
| `kanban-domain` | **muddled** | also owns `KanbanOperations` (an orchestration trait) and `InMemoryStore`, blending models with orchestration concerns |
| `kanban-persistence` | mostly clean | broad but every module is persistence-adjacent |
| `kanban-persistence-json` | clean | |
| `kanban-persistence-sqlite` | clean | |
| `kanban-service` | **mixed** | `KanbanContext` orchestration plus `StoreManager` plus `KanbanBackend` plus `JsonDataStore` plus `SqliteBackend` plus config resolution live together |
| `kanban-tui` | clean within remit | |
| `kanban-cli` | clean | thin facade over `KanbanContext` |
| `kanban-mcp` | mostly clean | `lib.rs` is 1593 lines and could be split |

### Trait analysis

| Trait | Location | Methods | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| `KanbanOperations` | `crates/kanban-domain/src/operations.rs` | **51** | god-trait. Doc-comment explicitly justifies bundling: "forces both implementations to add it". Inverts ISP. |
| `DataStore` | `crates/kanban-domain/src/data_store.rs` | **40** | borderline god-interface. Defensible because backends genuinely need full CRUD coverage. |
| `CommandStore` | `crates/kanban-domain/src/command_store.rs` | 4 | clean |
| `KanbanBackend` | `crates/kanban-service/src/backend.rs` | 7 plus supertraits | inherits the combined ~51 methods, so any backend impl is huge |
| `PersistenceStore` | `crates/kanban-persistence/src/traits.rs` | 6 | clean |
| `StoreFactory` | `crates/kanban-persistence/src/registry.rs` | 3 (`name`, `matches_content`, `create`) | clean. **CLAUDE.md documents the old signature** (`name`/`supported_patterns`/`matches`/`create`) - doc is stale. |
| `StoreRegistry` | same | concrete, 6 methods | clean |
| `Command` enum | `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/mod.rs:23-71` | closed enum, dispatched via `match` to 6 sub-domains | **OCP violation**: a new command domain requires editing the enum and every `match` (`execute`, `description`, `capture_inverse`) plus every upper layer that serializes commands. |
| `Serializer`, `MigrationStrategy`, `ConflictResolver`, `ChangeDetector` | `crates/kanban-persistence/src/traits.rs` | 2/2/2/4 | well-segregated |

### KanbanContext as god-object

`crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs` is 1317 lines, 82 functions, two `impl` blocks. Owns: the backend, the undo stack, dirty/conflict flags, the app config, accessors for boards/columns/cards/sprints/graph/snapshot, persistence lifecycle (`save`, `reload`, `mark_dirty`, `mark_clean`), undo/redo control (`undo`, `redo`, `can_undo`, `clear_history`, `undo_depth`), batch operations with detailed reporting (`archive_cards_detailed`, `move_cards_detailed`, `assign_cards_to_sprint_detailed`), private compute helpers for status and column invariants, then the full 45-method `KanbanOperations` impl. **Suggested factoring**: a `MutationDispatcher` (execute, undo), a `SessionState` (dirty, conflict, undo stack), and a `KanbanOperations` impl as free functions over `&dyn KanbanBackend`.

### DIP

- **Good**: `KanbanContext` holds `Arc<dyn KanbanBackend>`; `StoreManager` holds `Arc<StoreRegistry>` with `Box<dyn StoreFactory>` factories; `Command::capture_inverse` takes `&dyn DataStore`.
- **Worse**: `kanban-mcp/src/context.rs:7` and `kanban-cli/src/context.rs:7` import the concrete `KanbanContext`. The trait exists; the binding is concrete.
- **Worse**: `crates/kanban-service/src/lib.rs:43-45,62-68` re-exports concrete `JsonStoreFactory`/`SqliteStoreFactory` and ships a `default_registry()` that hard-codes both, pulling both backends into the service crate's public surface.

### OCP for backends

The "pluggable storage backend" claim is partly false. To add a `kanban-persistence-postgres`, an implementer must touch at minimum:

1. `crates/kanban-service/src/lib.rs` to add a `PostgresStoreFactory` re-export and feature-gate it in `default_registry()`.
2. `crates/kanban-service/src/store_manager.rs:97-126` `make_backend` contains an explicit `if self.is_sqlite(locator)` branch that constructs `crate::sqlite_backend::SqliteBackend` directly, bypassing the registry. A new backend either needs its own privileged branch or a refactor moving backend construction fully behind `StoreFactory`.
3. A `KanbanBackend` adapter that lifts `PersistenceStore` to `DataStore + CommandStore` (today `JsonDataStore` and `SqliteBackend` are sibling concretes in `kanban-service`, not a generic adapter).

The `StoreFactory` registration is genuinely pluggable; the `KanbanBackend` construction is not.

---

# Cross-cutting themes

- **Doc drift**: CLAUDE.md documents the old `StoreFactory` API (`name`/`supported_patterns`/`matches`/`create`); the real trait is `name`/`matches_content`/`create`. Aligns with the broader CONTRIBUTING.md audit in KAN-481.
- **Encapsulation drift**: `pub` fields and `pub` types that should be `pub(crate)` accumulate across `card.rs`, `sqlite_store.rs`, `json_file_store.rs`. Pattern, not isolated.
- **Task-ID anchoring**: KAN-191, KAN-403, KAN-405 are embedded in source. These references rot once the corresponding card archives. Belongs in commit messages.
- **Decomposition lagging refactors**: KAN-224 split `app.rs` into `app/`, but `app/mod.rs` is now 2975 lines. Either the sub-modules need to absorb more of the content, or `mod.rs` itself needs a second pass.

---

# Suggested follow-up cards

This audit names problems; fixes belong in scoped cards. Recommended split:

1. **Split `KanbanContext` god-object** into mutation dispatcher, session state, and a slimmer trait impl. Large; spans `kanban-service`.
2. **Decompose `KanbanOperations` god-trait** into focused per-aggregate traits, with the existing trait kept as a marker that composes them, so consumers can depend on a narrower surface.
3. **Strip task-ID references from source** across `sqlite_store.rs`, `json_file_store.rs`, `sqlite_backend.rs`, `card_handlers.rs`. Mechanical; low risk.
4. **Encapsulate `Card` fields** to `pub(crate)` with getters so `updated_at` cannot be bypassed.
5. **Lift type-alias IDs to real newtypes** (`BoardId`, `CardId`, `ColumnId` as tuple structs).
6. **Decompose `sqlite_store.rs`** into per-aggregate modules.
7. **Decompose `card_handlers.rs`, `detail_view_handlers.rs`, `navigation_handlers.rs`** by entity.
8. **Move backend construction fully behind `StoreFactory`** by removing the `is_sqlite` privileged branch in `StoreManager::make_backend`; verify a hypothetical third backend can register without touching `store_manager.rs`.
9. **Reopen `Command` to extension** by considering a trait-object approach (`Box<dyn Command>`) or at minimum documenting the closed-enum trade-off in CLAUDE.md so the OCP claim is no longer aspirational.
10. **Drop `Card::new`/`update_*` from `String` to `&str`+`impl Into<String>`** where the trait does not force the signature.
11. **Refresh CLAUDE.md `StoreFactory` description** to match the real signature.

---

# Out of scope

- Fixing any of the issues. This card is the audit; each item above becomes its own card if accepted.
- Performance, security, accessibility audits. Limited to the four focus areas requested.
- Test coverage analysis. A separate audit pass.
- Doc audit of `CONTRIBUTING.md` itself (already tracked in KAN-481) or of `README.md` (partly tracked in KAN-402, fully not yet).
cards.title: Architecture audit: file length, modularization, code style, SOLID compliance

cards.description:
# STATUS: implemented in PR #317 (in review, branch in DOING)

Branch: `KAN-656/fix-chocolatey-artifact-poll-head-redirect-to-s3-returns-403`
PR: https://github.com/fulsomenko/kanban/pull/317
Commits:
- `90e9644c` — primary fix: collapse HEAD poll + download/hash into one `gh release view --json assets` call
- `c12b2fab` — review follow-ups: explicit `permissions: contents: read` + `2>$null` on `gh` stderr
- `97f90a57` — expand changeset to mention the follow-up changes

---

## Stakeholders

- **Release manager (Max)** — if the poll flakes, the Chocolatey job spuriously fails post-release.
- **Windows users** — slower or failed Chocolatey publish means the new version is unavailable on Windows for hours/days.

## Where

- `.github/workflows/release.yml:309-332` — (old) `publish-chocolatey` job's `Wait for release asset to be fetchable` step.
- `.github/workflows/release.yml:334-346` — (old) subsequent `Compute Windows ZIP SHA256` step that downloaded and hashed the asset.
- `.github/workflows/release.yml:309-340` — (new) single `Read Windows ZIP digest from GitHub Release` step.
- `.github/workflows/release.yml:304-305` — (new) `permissions: contents: read` on the `publish-chocolatey` job.

## Why (root cause)

The previous poll used `Invoke-WebRequest -Method Head` against the GitHub release-download URL. GitHub release-download URLs **302-redirect to S3-style presigned URLs** on `release-assets.githubusercontent.com`. PowerShell's `Invoke-WebRequest` auto-follows the redirect. The signed redirect target is **cryptographically signed for a specific HTTP method** — a URL signed for GET returns 403 on HEAD.

Well-documented S3 / presigned-URL behavior:

- AWS re:Post — [403 when accessing S3 signed HTTPS URL with Postman or Python](https://repost.aws/questions/QUXlm00GaUSd2GAWQfkVf47w/403-when-accessing-s3-signed-https-url-with-postman-or-python).
- Gitea #35086 — [HEAD requests to attachments always result in 403 Forbidden if using minio](https://github.com/go-gitea/gitea/issues/35086).
- grab #43 — [Response from github is 403 when downloading a release file](https://github.com/cavaliergopher/grab/issues/43).

Result: the poll's HEAD would have chased the 302, hit 403, treated the asset as not-ready, and exhausted the 150s budget. Latent — this code path has not fired yet; 0.7.0 will be the first release that produces a Windows artifact.

## How (the 2026 approach)

`gh release view --json assets` returns a structured `state` field per asset, and since the [2025-06-03 changelog](https://github.blog/changelog/2025-06-03-releases-now-expose-digests-for-release-assets/) it also exposes the SHA256 digest in the `digest` field. So both the readiness signal AND the checksum we needed to compute locally are right there in the API response.

Verified live against `cli/cli` v2.93.0:

```json
{
  "name": "gh_2.93.0_checksums.txt",
  "state": "uploaded",
  "digest": "sha256:f62a3bc9dedc88262c9c2b56eb653cb3ded6bde8076bdbb151f4cce9c8729da5",
  "size": 1950,
  ...
}
```

### Replacement step (shipped)

```yaml
- name: Read Windows ZIP digest from GitHub Release
  id: sha
  shell: pwsh
  env:
    VERSION: ${{ needs.release.outputs.new }}
    GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
  run: |
    $asset = "kanban-v$env:VERSION-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip"
    $ok = $false
    for ($i = 1; $i -le 30; $i++) {
      $json = gh release view "v$env:VERSION" --repo $env:REPO `
        --json assets --jq ".assets[] | select(.name == `"$asset`")" 2>$null
      if ($json) {
        $obj = $json | ConvertFrom-Json
        if ($obj.state -eq 'uploaded' -and $obj.digest) {
          $sha = $obj.digest -replace '^sha256:', ''
          Write-Output "Found $asset: sha=$sha"
          "sha=$sha" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT -Append
          $ok = $true
          break
        }
        Write-Output ("Attempt {0}: state='{1}' digest='{2}'" -f $i, $obj.state, $obj.digest)
      } else {
        Write-Output ("Attempt {0}: asset not yet listed" -f $i)
      }
      Start-Sleep -Seconds 5
    }
    if (-not $ok) {
      Write-Error "Release asset never became ready after 30 attempts (~150s)"
      exit 1
    }
```

And on the job header:

```yaml
publish-chocolatey:
  name: Publish to Chocolatey
  needs: [release, build-windows]
  if: needs.release.outputs.has_changesets == 'true'
  runs-on: windows-latest
  permissions:
    contents: read    # explicit — protects against future org-default tightening
  steps:
    ...
```

### Why this is the *proper* 2026 way

- **Authoritative source for the digest**: GitHub generates and stores it server-side at upload time.
- **Method-agnostic**: GitHub REST API via `gh`, no signed-URL HEAD/GET asymmetry.
- **Single round-trip per poll** instead of HEAD → 302 → S3 403 → retry.
- **Pre-existing `gh` auth**: uses `secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN` already in scope on the runner; no separate credential.
- **`state == "uploaded"` AND non-empty `digest`** are both required — handles the brief window where an asset is committed to the Release but the digest hasn't been populated yet.
- **Explicit permissions scope**: `contents: read` on the job protects against future org-level token-default tightening silently breaking the lookup.
- **Suppressed stderr (`2>$null`) on the per-iteration call**: keeps the action log clean while the release tag is still being created upstream (no scary red error text on iterations 1-2 before the tag exists).
- **Single source of truth**: the digest the chocolatey package verifies against is the same digest GitHub serves to other clients.

## Offender example (real-world reproduction)

```
$ curl -I "https://github.com/.../releases/download/v0.7.0/kanban-v0.7.0-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip"
HTTP/2 302
location: https://release-assets.githubusercontent.com/...?X-Amz-Signature=...

$ curl -I "https://release-assets.githubusercontent.com/...?X-Amz-Signature=..."
HTTP/2 403   # signed for GET, not HEAD

$ curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://release-assets..."
200          # GET works fine on the same URL
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [x] The `publish-chocolatey` job no longer issues HEAD requests against `releases/download/...`.
- [x] The asset readiness check uses `gh release view --json assets` and reads `state`.
- [x] The SHA256 used to template `$checksum64$` in the chocolatey nuspec is read from the `digest` field, not computed locally.
- [x] The `Compute Windows ZIP SHA256` step is removed (merged into the readiness step).
- [x] Explicit `permissions: contents: read` on the job.
- [x] `2>$null` suppression on the per-iteration `gh` call to keep logs clean.
- [x] `yamllint` clean.
- [x] Downstream step (`Stage Chocolatey package with substitutions`) reads `${{ steps.sha.outputs.sha }}` unchanged.
- [ ] Real-run validation deferred to the first 0.7.x release with Windows artifacts.

## References

- GitHub Changelog: [Releases now expose digests for release assets (2025-06-03)](https://github.blog/changelog/2025-06-03-releases-now-expose-digests-for-release-assets/)
- [gh release view manual](https://cli.github.com/manual/gh_release_view)
- [REST API endpoints for release assets](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/releases/assets)
- [Gitea #35086](https://github.com/go-gitea/gitea/issues/35086), [grab #43](https://github.com/cavaliergopher/grab/issues/43), [AWS re:Post on signed-URL HEAD/GET asymmetry](https://repost.aws/questions/QUXlm00GaUSd2GAWQfkVf47w/403-when-accessing-s3-signed-https-url-with-postman-or-python).
cards.title: Fix Chocolatey artifact poll: HEAD redirect to S3 returns 403

cards.description:
# STATUS: implemented in PR #318 (in review)

Branch: `KAN-657/document-chocolatey-re-push-recovery-release-recovery-md`
PR: https://github.com/fulsomenko/kanban/pull/318
Commit: `5af99676`

Decision against earlier card draft: runbook lives at
`packaging/chocolatey/RECOVERY.md`, NOT `docs/release-recovery.md`. The
constraint being documented (no re-push of same id+version) is
chocolatey-specific. Top-level `docs/` does not exist in this repo;
creating it for a single chocolatey-specific runbook would be an odd
new pattern. Reconsider only if more publishers (e.g. winget) gain
similar quirks.

---

## What shipped

1. **`packaging/chocolatey/RECOVERY.md`** — 130-line runbook with:
   - Diagnosis flowchart keyed on what `community.chocolatey.org/packages/kanban/<VERSION>` shows
   - Four scenarios fully covered:
     - Push succeeded but workflow reported failure → do nothing, wait
     - Malformed nupkg → cut X.Y.Z+1
     - API key rejected → rotate, re-run
     - Moderation backlog → wait
   - Anti-patterns section (do not re-run after confirmed push; do not edit X.Y.Z in place; do not let runbook drift from workflow error message)
   - "Reading this in a hurry" quick-reference table

2. **`.github/workflows/release.yml`** — `choco push` step now:
   - Pre-checks `community.chocolatey.org/api/v2/Packages(Id='kanban',Version='X')` and exits 0 if 200 returned (already-published case → job safe to re-run)
   - Wraps the actual push in try/catch that on failure prints the runbook pointer + "do not simply re-run this job" warning

3. **`packaging/chocolatey/README.md`** — paragraph cross-linking RECOVERY.md.

## Pre-check honest limitation (documented in runbook)

Packages in `Submitted` or `Waiting for Reviewer` status are NOT
visible via the public v2 OData API. So the pre-check returning 404
does not prove the package is unpublished — it might be in the
moderation queue. The runbook tells operators to verify on
community.chocolatey.org/packages/kanban/<VERSION> before deciding to
"safely re-run".

## Acceptance criteria

- [x] Recovery runbook exists, covers all four scenarios with actionable steps
- [x] Workflow `choco push` step prints recovery pointer on failure
- [x] Pre-publish idempotency check added (with documented caveat)
- [x] Cross-link from packaging/chocolatey/README.md
- [x] Changeset added (patch bump)
- [x] `yamllint` clean
- [ ] Real-run validation deferred to first 0.7.x release
cards.title: Document Chocolatey re-push recovery (release-recovery.md)

cards.description:
Follow-up from KAN-483 (architecture audit). Scopes the decomposition of `crates/kanban-service/src/config.rs` (1100 lines) into a `config/` submodule. The file is 702 lines of code + 398 lines of tests, so splitting the inline tests alone brings the production file under the 300-line guideline.

This is the lowest-risk file of the audit's modular cleanup set: a flat module of free functions over `AppConfig` with zero state, no backend handle, no flags, no caching. It is the recommended **first** card to land to validate the test-split pattern before tackling harder files.

## Current structure

| Block | Lines | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Imports | 1-6 | |
| `config_path` (cfg-gated by OS) | 8-25 | path discovery |
| `load`, `load_from` | 27-61 | load API |
| `save`, `save_to` | 63-92 | save API |
| `move_config` | 94-116 | cross-fs rename + copy/delete fallback |
| `effective_configuration_location` | 118-124 | resolver |
| `resolve_storage_location` | 130-140 | resolver |
| `validate` | 142-171 | validation |
| `has_non_default_values` / `is_all_defaults` | 173-237 | default detection |
| `strip_defaults` | 239-281 | mutates config in place |
| `AppConfigDto` + inherent impl | 283-322 | DTO struct |
| `impl Editable<AppConfig> for AppConfigDto` | 324-360 | trait impl |
| `write_config_file` (Unix / non-Unix) | 362-381 | atomic write helper |
| `try_load_from` | 383-395 | error-surfacing load |
| `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` | 397-1100 | 39 unit tests, 703 lines |

## Proposed module structure

Convert `config.rs` to a `config/` directory.

| File | Purpose | Target lines |
|---|---|---|
| `config/mod.rs` | module declarations + `pub use` re-exports preserving the current public API | ~50 |
| `config/path.rs` | `config_path()` (the OS-cfg block) | ~30 |
| `config/io.rs` | `load`, `load_from`, `save`, `save_to`, `try_load_from`, `move_config`, `write_config_file` (Unix/non-Unix) | ~180 |
| `config/effective.rs` | `effective_configuration_location`, `resolve_storage_location` | ~30 |
| `config/validate.rs` | `validate` | ~40 |
| `config/defaults.rs` | `has_non_default_values`, `is_all_defaults`, `strip_defaults`, plus the storage-field invariant doc | ~110 |
| `config/dto.rs` | `AppConfigDto`, its inherent impl, and `impl Editable<AppConfig> for AppConfigDto` | ~90 |
| `config/tests.rs` (or `config/tests/`) | all 39 inline tests, declared from `mod.rs` via `#[cfg(test)] mod tests;` | ~700 (one file) or 3 split files of ~220-280 each |

If the team enforces the 300-line rule on test files, split into:
- `config/tests/io.rs` (~280): `save_to`, `load_from`, `move_config`, `try_load_from`, `write_config_file`
- `config/tests/dto.rs` (~220): `AppConfigDto` round-trips, serialization order, format-change extension sync
- `config/tests/defaults.rs` (~200): `has_non_default_values`, `strip_defaults`, `validate`

## State ownership

`config.rs` has no state. The decomposition is purely a file split; nothing about ownership changes.

## Public API impact

Public surface today is reachable via `kanban_service::config::*` (the module is `pub mod config;` in `lib.rs:15`). `lib.rs:25` also re-exports `AppConfigDto` as `kanban_service::AppConfigDto`.

After the split, `crates/kanban-service/src/config/mod.rs` re-exports the same identifiers with `pub use`, so:

- `kanban_service::config::load_from` keeps working
- `kanban_service::AppConfigDto` keeps working (via `lib.rs:25`)
- `kanban_service::config::AppConfigDto` keeps working

No consumer changes needed.

## Tests

39 inline tests at lines 397-1100. They move wholesale into `config/tests.rs` (or sub-split). The test imports change from `use super::*;` to `use super::super::*;` if put in `config/tests/foo.rs`, or `use crate::config::*;` if `tests.rs` is a sibling of `mod.rs`.

## Sequencing (4 commits)

1. `refactor(service): create config submodule with path + io split` - convert `config.rs` to `config/mod.rs`, extract `path.rs` and `io.rs`. Tests still in `mod.rs` for now.
2. `refactor(service): extract config validation, effective, and defaults`.
3. `refactor(service): extract AppConfigDto into config/dto.rs`.
4. `refactor(service): move config inline tests into config/tests.rs` - final file-shrink. `mod.rs` ends at ~50 lines.

Optional 5th cosmetic commit if the test split into `config/tests/` is wanted.

## Risks

- `#[cfg(target_os = "...")]` blocks for `config_path` and `write_config_file` must keep the cfg gating intact when moving. CI on macOS/Linux catches a missed branch, but cross-compiling for Windows does not unless someone runs `cargo check --target x86_64-pc-windows-gnu`. Smoke locally after the move.
- `tempfile::NamedTempFile::persist` returns its own error type; `write_config_file` already handles this with `.map_err(|e| e.error)`. Do not refactor that line during the move.
- `mod tests` paths use `super::*`; the import-rewrite step is mechanical but easy to typo. Run `cargo test --package kanban-service config::` after each commit.
- `Editable<AppConfig>` is from `kanban-core`; `dto.rs` needs the `kanban_core::Editable` import.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every file in `crates/kanban-service/src/config/` is under 300 lines.
- `cargo test --package kanban-service` passes at every commit boundary.
- `cargo check --target x86_64-pc-windows-gnu` smoke-passes after the `io.rs` extraction.
- Public API at `kanban_service::config::*` and `kanban_service::AppConfigDto` is byte-identical before and after.
- No new `pub` items introduced. Any items dropped from `pub` to `pub(crate)` along the way are listed in the PR body for review.

## Related

Parent audit: KAN-483.
cards.title: Decompose kanban-service/src/config.rs (1100 lines) into config/ submodule

cards.description:
# STATUS: in progress on branch KAN-655/add-negative-path-tests-for-card-create-assign

## Stakeholders

- **Future maintainers** of `card create --assign` and the equivalent MCP path — without negative tests, regressions in the error path land silently.
- **CLI users** who mistype a sprint name and need a meaningful error rather than a panic or successful-looking no-op.
- **MCP clients** — agents trying to learn the schema by trial-and-error need clean, descriptive errors that pattern-match cleanly.

## Where (current state, verified)

- `crates/kanban-service/tests/create_card_with_sprint.rs` — three happy-path tests (UUID-assigns, no-sprint-default, undo). No negative paths.
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/card_commands.rs:258` — `context.get_sprint(sprint_id)?` returns `KanbanError::not_found("sprint", id)` for unknown UUID.
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/card_commands.rs:259-264` — cross-board validation branch currently returns `KanbanError::validation(...)` with the message `"sprint {id} belongs to board {b} but card is being created on board {a}"`. **No structured variant** — message-only.
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/card.rs:23,315` — `--assign` wiring via `resolve_assign_sprint`, returns `Result<Option<Uuid>, String>` where the String is `KanbanError.to_string()`.
- `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs:1014-1040` — `tool_create_card` calls `mcp_resolve_sprint_in_board` then `create_card`. Errors flow through `kanban_err_to_mcp` to `McpError::invalid_params(d.to_string(), None)`.
- `crates/kanban-cli/tests/cli_integration.rs:1684-1713` (positive `--assign` test), `:1778-1800` (negative `--assign` with no value).
- `crates/kanban-mcp/tests/integration.rs` — no card-create-with-sprint tests; pattern is direct `McpContext` method calls (no tool-router invocation).

## Why

KAN-556 is a release-headline minor feature crossing TUI/CLI/MCP. Happy paths covered; every error branch untested. Specifically the cross-board validation at `card_commands.rs:259-264` has zero coverage and any refactor could silently break it.

## Scope

Two production changes plus six tests.

### Production change (small, in-scope)

**Promote the cross-board case to a typed error variant.** Today the branch returns `KanbanError::validation(format!(...))`, which can only be asserted on by message substring — brittle to wording. Add a new `DomainError::SprintBoardMismatch { sprint_id, sprint_board, card_board }` variant, return it from the cross-board branch, and assert structurally in the new tests.

This also fixes a latent inconsistency: every other not-found-style error in the domain is a typed variant; the cross-board check is the odd one out.

CLI/MCP error rendering is unaffected — both call `.to_string()` on the error, and the new variant's `Display` impl produces the same human-readable message as the current `format!`.

### Tests added

**Domain/service (extend `crates/kanban-service/tests/create_card_with_sprint.rs`):**

1. `test_create_card_with_unknown_sprint_uuid_returns_not_found`
   - Pass `CreateCardOptions { sprint_id: Some(Uuid::new_v4()), .. }` against a board with no such sprint.
   - Assert `matches!(err, KanbanError::Domain(DomainError::NotFound { entity: "sprint", .. }))` and `err.is_not_found()`.

2. `test_create_card_with_cross_board_sprint_returns_typed_error`
   - Two boards A and B, column on A, sprint on B.
   - Assert `matches!(err, KanbanError::Domain(DomainError::SprintBoardMismatch { .. }))`.
   - Also assert message contains "board" as a sanity check, but the variant is the load-bearing assertion.
   - Pins the production-code change above as well as the validation branch.

**CLI (extend `crates/kanban-cli/tests/cli_integration.rs` near existing `--assign` tests at line 1700):**

3. `test_card_create_with_assign_unknown_name_fails_with_useful_error`
   - `kanban card create --assign nonexistent ...`
   - `.failure()`, `.stderr(predicate::str::contains("sprint"))`, `.stderr(predicate::str::contains("nonexistent"))`.

4. `test_card_create_with_assign_unknown_uuid_fails_with_useful_error`
   - Same with a random UUID.
   - Same predicates on the UUID string.

**MCP (extend `crates/kanban-mcp/tests/integration.rs` following the existing direct-method-call pattern):**

5. `test_mcp_create_card_with_unknown_sprint_name_returns_error`
   - Direct call to `mcp_ctx.mcp_resolve_sprint_in_board("nonexistent", board.id)`.
   - Assert error message contains "sprint" and "nonexistent".

6. `test_mcp_create_card_with_cross_board_sprint_returns_typed_error`
   - Two boards, sprint on B, call create_card on A with sprint B's UUID.
   - Assert the surfaced error matches the `SprintBoardMismatch` variant via the structured error path.

## Out of scope (per discovery)

The card's original draft listed "ambiguous sprint name" and "sprint number lookup without board context" as missing coverage. Per `crates/kanban-domain/src/operations.rs:235-315`, those scenarios live in the **resolver** (`resolve_sprint_id_global`), not in the create-card command. `CreateCard::execute` only ever receives a UUID; name/number resolution happens upstream. Resolver-level negative coverage is a separate ticket if needed.

## How

Order of operations:

1. Add `DomainError::SprintBoardMismatch { sprint_id, sprint_board, card_board }` variant in `crates/kanban-domain/src/error.rs`. `Display` impl reproduces the existing message format.
2. Change `card_commands.rs:259-264` to return the new variant instead of `KanbanError::validation(...)`.
3. Write the six tests above. Per TDD note: these document existing behavior plus the new variant; tests should pass on first run.
4. Run focused test suites then full workspace.

## Testing / validation

- `cargo test -p kanban-domain` (new error variant)
- `cargo test -p kanban-service --test create_card_with_sprint`
- `cargo test -p kanban-cli --test cli_integration -- card_create_with_assign`
- `cargo test -p kanban-mcp --test integration -- create_card`
- `cargo test --workspace`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` + `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `DomainError::SprintBoardMismatch` variant added with fields `{ sprint_id, sprint_board, card_board }`.
- [ ] `card_commands.rs` cross-board branch returns the new variant; message text preserved.
- [ ] `cargo tarpaulin -p kanban-domain --include-files 'src/commands/card_commands.rs'` shows the cross-board branch covered.
- [ ] All six tests added and passing.
- [ ] CLI tests invoke the real binary via `assert_cmd` and assert stderr contains both "sprint" and the offending name (UUID or string).
- [ ] MCP tests exercise the same code via the direct-method-call pattern used in `integration.rs`.
- [ ] No regressions in workspace test suite; fmt + clippy clean.
- [ ] Changeset added at `.changeset/kan-655-card-create-assign-negative-tests.md` with `bump: patch`. Description notes both the new error variant and the test additions.
cards.title: Add negative-path tests for card-create --assign

cards.description:
Follow-up from KAN-483 (architecture audit). Scopes the decomposition of `crates/kanban-domain/src/card_lifecycle.rs` (859 lines). Pure-function file with no trait impls and no I/O. **Lowest-risk domain split**; recommended as a warm-up for the domain-layer cleanup set.

## Current structure

| Block | Lines |
|---|---|
| `dedup_preserving_order` helper | 15-22 |
| `compute_move_positions` | 35-47 |
| `MoveDirection`, `CompletionToggleResult`, `CardMoveResult` types | 50-71 |
| `sorted_board_columns`, `next_position_in_column` | 74-83 |
| `compute_completion_toggle` (53 lines) | 91-143 |
| `compute_card_column_move` (55 lines) | 149-203 |
| `target_column_for_status`, `target_status_for_column_move` | 212-262 |
| `compact_column_positions` | 265-279 |
| `should_auto_complete_new_card` | 284-290 |
| `resolve_restore_column` | 297-308 |
| `migrate_sprint_logs` | 313-340 |
| Tests (~31 tests, 517 lines) | 342-859 |

## Proposed module structure

Convert into directory `card_lifecycle/`.

| File | Purpose | Target lines |
|---|---|---|
| `card_lifecycle/mod.rs` | re-exports + `MoveDirection`, `CompletionToggleResult`, `CardMoveResult` + `sorted_board_columns`, `next_position_in_column`, `dedup_preserving_order` | ~110 |
| `card_lifecycle/positions.rs` | `compute_move_positions`, `compact_column_positions` | ~50 |
| `card_lifecycle/movement.rs` | `compute_completion_toggle`, `compute_card_column_move`, `target_column_for_status`, `target_status_for_column_move` | ~165 |
| `card_lifecycle/creation_restore.rs` | `should_auto_complete_new_card`, `resolve_restore_column`, `migrate_sprint_logs` | ~55 |
| `card_lifecycle/tests/positions.rs` | `compute_move_positions`, `compact_column_positions`, `dedup_preserving_order` tests | ~190 |
| `card_lifecycle/tests/movement.rs` | toggle + column-move tests + `sorted_board_columns` | ~210 |
| `card_lifecycle/tests/creation_restore.rs` | auto-complete + restore-column + migrate-sprint-logs tests | ~120 |

## Cluster-to-destination

| Cluster | Current lines | Destination |
|---|---|---|
| `dedup_preserving_order` | 15-22 | `mod.rs` |
| `compute_move_positions` | 35-47 | `positions.rs` |
| `MoveDirection` + result structs | 50-71 | `mod.rs` |
| `sorted_board_columns`, `next_position_in_column` | 74-83 | `mod.rs` |
| `compute_completion_toggle` | 91-143 | `movement.rs` |
| `compute_card_column_move` | 149-203 | `movement.rs` |
| `target_column_for_status`, `target_status_for_column_move` | 212-262 | `movement.rs` |
| `compact_column_positions` | 265-279 | `positions.rs` |
| `should_auto_complete_new_card` | 284-290 | `creation_restore.rs` |
| `resolve_restore_column` | 297-308 | `creation_restore.rs` |
| `migrate_sprint_logs` | 313-340 | `creation_restore.rs` |
| Tests for `sorted_board_columns`, toggle, column-move (11) | 365-559 | `tests/movement.rs` |
| Tests for compact + compute_move_positions + dedup (11) | 562-592, 737-858 | `tests/positions.rs` |
| Tests for auto_complete + restore + migrate (9) | 594-735 | `tests/creation_restore.rs` |

## Public API impact

`lib.rs:6` declares `pub mod card_lifecycle;`. The directory module preserves the path. All free functions are referenced as `crate::card_lifecycle::*` from `kanban-service` / `kanban-tui` (verify before committing). `mod.rs` re-exports everything (`pub use positions::*; pub use movement::*; pub use creation_restore::*;`) so `kanban_domain::card_lifecycle::compute_move_positions` etc. still resolve.

## Tests

31 inline tests split across three test sub-files. Shared test helpers `test_board`, `add_columns`, `test_card` at lines 347-361 move to `card_lifecycle/tests/common.rs`.

## Sequencing (4 commits)

1. `refactor(domain): convert card_lifecycle to a directory module; split shared types and helpers from movement logic`.
2. `refactor(domain): extract position computation into card_lifecycle::positions`.
3. `refactor(domain): extract creation/restore/migration helpers into card_lifecycle::creation_restore`.
4. `test(domain): split card_lifecycle tests by concern`.

## Risks

Nothing crosses I/O or trait boundaries; pure functions. Lowest-risk file of the domain set. Re-export discipline in `mod.rs` is the only thing between this and downstream breakage; using `pub use module::*` for each submodule keeps it mechanical.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every file in `crates/kanban-domain/src/card_lifecycle/` is under 300 lines.
- `cargo test --package kanban-domain` passes at every commit boundary.
- Public API at `kanban_domain::card_lifecycle::*` is unchanged.
- No new `pub` items introduced.

## Related

Parent audit: KAN-483.
cards.title: Decompose kanban-domain/src/card_lifecycle.rs (859 lines) into card_lifecycle/ submodule
