cards.description:
Follow-up from KAN-483 (architecture audit). Scopes the decomposition of `crates/kanban-domain/src/search/mod.rs` (997 lines). `search/` is already a directory module, so this split is just adding siblings to `mod.rs`.

## Current structure

| Block | Lines |
|---|---|
| `CardSearcher` trait | 9-12 |
| `TitleSearcher` + impl | 15-40 |
| `BranchNameSearcher` + impl (with `get_branch_name` helper) | 43-106 |
| `CardIdentifierSearcher` + impl | 109-144 |
| `SearchBy` enum + dispatch | 147-161 |
| `CompositeSearcher` + `Default` | 166-201 |
| `parse_identifier`, `ParsedIdentifier` | 203-226 |
| `find_cards_by_identifier` | 236-266 |
| `format_ambiguous_matches` | 271-282 |
| `find_boards_by_name`, `find_columns_by_name` | 285-302 |
| `find_sprints_by_query_on_board`, `find_sprints_by_query_global` | 313-366 |
| `CompositeSearcher` `CardSearcher` impl (separated from struct) | 368-377 |
| Tests (~36 tests, 619 lines) | 379-997 |

## Proposed module structure

| File | Purpose | Target lines |
|---|---|---|
| `search/mod.rs` | `CardSearcher` trait, `SearchBy` enum, `CompositeSearcher` (including its `CardSearcher` impl, consolidated), re-exports | ~95 |
| `search/searchers.rs` | `TitleSearcher`, `BranchNameSearcher`, `CardIdentifierSearcher` + their `CardSearcher` impls + private `get_branch_name`/`get_identifier` helpers | ~135 |
| `search/identifier.rs` | `ParsedIdentifier`, `parse_identifier`, `find_cards_by_identifier`, `format_ambiguous_matches` | ~95 |
| `search/by_name.rs` | `find_boards_by_name`, `find_columns_by_name`, `find_sprints_by_query_on_board`, `find_sprints_by_query_global` | ~85 |
| `search/tests/searchers.rs` | TitleSearcher, BranchNameSearcher, CardIdentifierSearcher, CompositeSearcher tests | ~160 |
| `search/tests/identifier.rs` | `find_cards_by_identifier_*` tests | ~310 |
| `search/tests/by_name.rs` | board / column / sprint name and on_board / global tests | ~190 |

## Cluster-to-destination

| Cluster | Current lines | Destination |
|---|---|---|
| `CardSearcher` trait | 9-12 | `mod.rs` |
| `TitleSearcher` | 15-40 | `searchers.rs` |
| `BranchNameSearcher` + branch generation | 43-106 | `searchers.rs` |
| `CardIdentifierSearcher` | 109-144 | `searchers.rs` |
| `SearchBy` enum | 147-161 | `mod.rs` |
| `CompositeSearcher` + impls | 166-201, 368-377 | `mod.rs` |
| `ParsedIdentifier` + `parse_identifier` | 203-226 | `identifier.rs` |
| `find_cards_by_identifier` | 236-266 | `identifier.rs` |
| `format_ambiguous_matches` | 271-282 | `identifier.rs` |
| `find_boards_by_name`, `find_columns_by_name` | 285-302 | `by_name.rs` |
| `find_sprints_by_query_on_board`, `find_sprints_by_query_global` | 313-366 | `by_name.rs` |
| Searcher tests (8) | 389-478, 791-801 | `tests/searchers.rs` |
| Identifier tests (22) | 481-815 | `tests/identifier.rs` |
| Name/sprint tests (13) | 818-996 | `tests/by_name.rs` |

## Public API impact

`lib.rs:50-54` re-exports `find_boards_by_name`, `find_cards_by_identifier`, `find_columns_by_name`, `find_sprints_by_query_global`, `find_sprints_by_query_on_board`, `format_ambiguous_matches`, `BranchNameSearcher`, `CardSearcher`, `CompositeSearcher`, `SearchBy`, `TitleSearcher`. `mod.rs` must `pub use searchers::*; pub use identifier::*; pub use by_name::*;` so every symbol stays at `kanban_domain::search::*`. `CardIdentifierSearcher` is currently `pub` but not in `lib.rs` re-exports; keep that asymmetry to avoid widening the API.

## Tests

36 inline tests split into three sub-files.

## Sequencing (4 commits)

1. `refactor(domain): extract concrete CardSearcher implementations into search::searchers`.
2. `refactor(domain): extract identifier parsing and find_cards_by_identifier into search::identifier`.
3. `refactor(domain): extract entity-name lookups into search::by_name`.
4. `test(domain): split search tests by concern`.

## Risks

- `BranchNameSearcher::get_branch_name` calls `board.effective_branch_prefix(...)`; verify the method's signature and visibility don't change with the move (they don't; only the caller moves).
- `CompositeSearcher`'s `CardSearcher` impl at lines 368-377 is separated from the struct by free functions; when consolidating into `mod.rs`, place it right next to the struct definition for readability.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every file in `crates/kanban-domain/src/search/` is under 300 lines.
- `cargo test --package kanban-domain` passes at every commit boundary.
- All items re-exported from `lib.rs:50-54` resolve unchanged.

## Related

Parent audit: KAN-483.
cards.title: Decompose kanban-domain/src/search/mod.rs (997 lines) into focused search submodules

cards.description:
Follow-up from KAN-483 (architecture audit). Scopes the decomposition of `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/navigation_handlers.rs` (1020 lines). The file mixes three concerns: pure pagination math, `impl App` viewport glue, and actual navigation handlers. Recommended as the first of the three TUI-handler splits because the boundaries are the cleanest.

## Current structure

| Lines | Cluster |
|---|---|
| 1-3 | imports |
| 5-20 | `PageBoundary` struct + helpers (pure pagination math) |
| 22-149 | `count_headers_in_viewport`, `compute_page_boundaries`, `compute_page_boundaries_with_headers` (pure pagination math) |
| 151-228 | `find_current_page`, `calculate_jump_target_down`, `calculate_jump_target_up` (pure pagination math) |
| 230-344 | `impl App` viewport calc: `get_adjusted_viewport_height`, `compute_pages_for_current_view` |
| 346-447 | `handle_focus_switch`, `handle_navigation_down`, `handle_navigation_up` (board/card focus + j/k navigation) |
| 450-541 | `handle_selection_activate`, `handle_escape_key`, `is_kanban_view` (focus / selection / view-mode predicate) |
| 543-591 | `handle_kanban_column_left/right`, `handle_column_or_focus_switch` (column-aware horizontal navigation) |
| 593-627 | `handle_jump_to_top`, `handle_jump_to_bottom` (gg / G) |
| 629-717 | `handle_jump_half_viewport_up/down` (Ctrl-U / Ctrl-D) |
| 720-1019 | `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (21 unit tests of pure helpers) |

## Proposed module structure

Convert `navigation_handlers.rs` into a `navigation/` directory.

| File | Purpose | Target lines |
|---|---|---|
| `handlers/navigation/mod.rs` | slimmed file holding the five core handlers (`handle_focus_switch`, `handle_navigation_down`/`_up`, `handle_selection_activate`, `handle_escape_key`) and `pub mod` declarations | ~180 |
| `handlers/navigation/pagination.rs` | pure math + the existing test module | ~580 (code ~280, tests ~300; if strict about 300-line tests, split tests into sibling file) |
| `handlers/navigation/viewport.rs` | `get_adjusted_viewport_height`, `compute_pages_for_current_view` | ~130 |
| `handlers/navigation/kanban_columns.rs` | kanban-mode horizontal nav | ~70 |
| `handlers/navigation/jump.rs` | gg/G/Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D | ~130 |

Alternative: keep them flat siblings (`navigation_pagination.rs` etc.) if the maintainer prefers no nested directory.

## Cluster-to-destination

| Cluster | Current lines | Destination |
|---|---|---|
| `PageBoundary` + 5 free fns + the 21 tests | 5-228, 720-1019 | `navigation/pagination.rs` (pure module, no `impl App`) |
| `get_adjusted_viewport_height`, `compute_pages_for_current_view` | 230-344 | `navigation/viewport.rs` (`impl App` block, `pub(crate)` methods) |
| Core handlers (`handle_focus_switch`, `handle_navigation_*`, `handle_selection_activate`, `handle_escape_key`) | 346-528 | `navigation/mod.rs` (slimmed) |
| `is_kanban_view`, `handle_kanban_column_*`, `handle_column_or_focus_switch` | 530-591 | `navigation/kanban_columns.rs` |
| `handle_jump_to_top`, `handle_jump_to_bottom`, `handle_jump_half_viewport_*` | 593-717 | `navigation/jump.rs` |

## Public API impact

All call sites in `app/mod.rs`:
- `handle_navigation_down` (mod.rs:731, 1041, 1174)
- `handle_jump_half_viewport_up/down` (mod.rs:778-779, 894, 898)

All other methods are called internally from other handlers. Splitting via submodules preserves inherent-method visibility on `App`. Free functions in `pagination.rs` (`compute_page_boundaries` etc.) become `pub(crate)` in their new module and are imported by `viewport.rs` and `jump.rs`. `is_kanban_view` is also called from `card_handlers.rs:451`; must remain `pub` or be re-exported.

## Sequencing (4 commits)

1. Carve out `pagination.rs` (pure functions + tests). Zero `impl App` coupling, lowest risk.
2. Carve out `viewport.rs` (the two `impl App` viewport helpers). They are called from `handle_navigation_down`/`_up` (still in main file) and from the jump handlers.
3. Carve out `jump.rs`. Jump tests in `pagination.rs` already exercise pure math.
4. Carve out `kanban_columns.rs`. `is_kanban_view` stays `pub` so `card_handlers.rs:451` keeps working.

## Risks

- `is_kanban_view` used outside this file (`card_handlers.rs:451`); must remain `pub` or be re-exported.
- The borrow-juggling in `handle_navigation_down` (lines 367-388) reads `get_adjusted_viewport_height` (immutable), then borrows `self.view.strategy` mutably, then reads viewport again. Moving `get_adjusted_viewport_height` to a sibling impl block does not change anything (Rust merges all `impl App` across the crate).
- The test module's `column_boundary` helper and inline `PageBoundary { start, end }` literals depend on `PageBoundary` being visible to tests; declare it `pub(crate)` in `pagination.rs`.

## Acceptance criteria

- Production files in `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/navigation/` are under 300 lines (tests file may exceed if the team accepts test-file exemption).
- `cargo test --package kanban-tui` passes at every commit boundary.
- All call sites in `app/mod.rs` remain unchanged.

## Related

Parent audit: KAN-483.
cards.title: Decompose kanban-tui handlers/navigation_handlers.rs (1020 lines) into navigation/ submodule

cards.description:
## Stakeholders

- **Users upgrading a V1 or V2 JSON file to 0.7.x** — anyone whose `kanban.json` predates KAN-415-ish (V2) or KAN-256-ish (V1), opened by a 0.7.0+ binary. Files are unrecoverable if the destructive V→V7 chain fails partway through.
- **Both async (`Migrator::migrate`) and sync (`migrate_to_v7_sync`) load paths** — they share the gap; the fix needs to land in both, or in the shared policy helper plus a coordinated orchestrator reorder.

## Where

- `crates/kanban-persistence-json/src/migration/backup.rs:15-22` — `pre_v7_backup_path_for` returns `None` for both `FormatVersion::V1` and `FormatVersion::V2`.
- `crates/kanban-persistence-json/src/migration/migrator.rs:71-76` (async) and `crates/kanban-persistence-json/src/json_file_store.rs:94-101` (sync, post-KAN-650) — both orchestrators run `migrate_v1_to_v2*` then `migrate_v2_to_v3*` **before** checking the backup policy, so V1 and V2 sources are overwritten before the policy gets to fire.
- `crates/kanban-persistence-json/src/json_file_store.rs:154` — `migrate_v1_to_v2_sync` writes `.v1.backup` then **removes it on V1→V2 success** (`let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&backup_path)`). The V1 backup only protects the V1→V2 transition itself, not the subsequent destructive chain.
- `crates/kanban-persistence-json/src/migration/migrator.rs:142-188` — `Migrator::migrate_v1_to_v2` (async) does the same: backup created, then removed on success.
- Misleading comment in `pre_v7_backup_path_for` that V2 "is shape-stable through V2→V3 and needs no backup" — true for the V2→V3 step alone, **not** true for the V→V7 chain that runs after.

## Why

### The V2 gap (originally documented)

`pre_v7_backup_path_for(V2, path)` returns `None`, so the destructive `split_graph` + `v6_to_v7_rename` chain runs against the now-V3 file on disk with no backup. If either step fails:

1. The original V2 content is gone (overwritten in place by `migrate_v2_to_v3_sync` / `migrate_v2_to_v3`).
2. No `.v2.backup` was ever written.
3. The user has a partially-transformed V3 (or V6) file on disk and nothing to roll back to.

### The V1 gap (caught in PR #321 final review)

The V1 case looks safer because `migrate_v1_to_v2_sync` writes its own `.v1.backup`. But that backup is **removed on V1→V2 success**. The full trace:

1. V1 source → `migrate_v1_to_v2_sync` writes `.v1.backup`, transforms file, removes `.v1.backup` on V1→V2 success.
2. `migrate_v2_to_v3_sync` runs against the now-V2 file. No backup.
3. `pre_v7_backup_path_for(V1, path)` returns `None` (V1 is excluded).
4. Destructive chain runs against the now-V3 file with **no backup**.
5. If split_graph or v6_to_v7_rename fails, the user has a partially-transformed file on disk and **no V1 original to recover from** — the `.v1.backup` was already cleaned up two steps ago.

The V1 case is structurally identical to the V2 case: in both, the per-step backup (or absence thereof) covers only one transition and is gone by the time the destructive chain runs.

### Why this matters

This is a pre-existing gap that both async and sync share. KAN-650 brought the sync path to parity with async, which means the gap is now consistently present in both rather than only in sync. The fix needs to live in the shared backup policy plus a coordinated change in both orchestrators.

Real-world likelihood: low. V1 and V2 files are old vintage. Most users have long since migrated. But a fresh checkout of an old branch, a backup tape restore, an old fork — these can all surface V1/V2 files in a 0.7.x environment.

## How

Two approaches:

### Option A (recommended): extend the backup policy to cover V1 AND V2

Change `pre_v7_backup_path_for`:

```rust
pub(crate) fn pre_v7_backup_path_for(from: FormatVersion, path: &Path) -> Option<PathBuf> {
    match from {
        FormatVersion::V1 => Some(path.with_extension("v1.backup")),
        FormatVersion::V2 => Some(path.with_extension("v2.backup")),
        FormatVersion::V3 => Some(path.with_extension("v3.backup")),
        FormatVersion::V4 => Some(path.with_extension("v4.backup")),
        FormatVersion::V5 => Some(path.with_extension("v5.backup")),
        FormatVersion::V6 => Some(path.with_extension("v6.backup")),
        _ => None,
    }
}
```

Then **move the backup creation in both orchestrators to fire BEFORE `migrate_v1_to_v2*` runs**, not after the per-step migrations. The full chain becomes:

```rust
// Sync orchestrator (post-fix)
let backup_path = crate::migration::pre_v7_backup_path_for(from, path);
if let Some(backup) = &backup_path {
    std::fs::copy(path, backup)?;  // captures original (V1, V2, V3, V4, V5, V6) state
}

let result = (|| -> PersistenceResult<Vec<u8>> {
    if from == FormatVersion::V1 { migrate_v1_to_v2_sync(path)?; }
    if from <= FormatVersion::V2 { migrate_v2_to_v3_sync(path)?; }
    run_split_and_rename_chain_sync(from, path)
})();

// ...cleanup logic unchanged
```

This creates one outer backup that covers the entire chain. The existing per-step `.v1.backup` mechanism inside `migrate_v1_to_v2_sync` becomes redundant for the chained-through case and should be removed to avoid double-backups. Two options:

- **(A1)** Remove the per-step backup from `migrate_v1_to_v2*` entirely. Cleaner. Breaks any caller that invokes `migrate_v1_to_v2` directly (if any exist; check the call graph).
- **(A2)** Keep the per-step backup but suppress it when called from the orchestrator (e.g., add a `skip_backup: bool` parameter). Less invasive but adds a parameter to a per-step function.

Recommend **(A1)** if `migrate_v1_to_v2*` has no direct callers outside the orchestrator (grep to verify before deciding).

### Option B: per-step backups for V2 too

Don't change the outer policy. Instead, give `migrate_v2_to_v3*` its own `.v2.backup` similar to V1→V2. Each per-step migration manages its own backup, with the same "remove on success" semantics.

This makes both V1 and V2 gaps disappear from the orchestrator's perspective. But it still leaves the asymmetric "remove on success" behavior: a V1→V7 chain still loses the V1 backup once V1→V2 succeeds. Each per-step protects only itself.

To make Option B truly equivalent in protection, you'd also need to NOT remove the per-step backup until the entire orchestrator returns Ok. That's effectively the outer wrap from Option A wearing per-step clothing.

### Recommendation

**Option A with A1.** Single outer backup covering the entire chain, with the per-step `.v1.backup` mechanism removed (assuming no external callers). Clean, one mental model, matches what the user expects.

## Offender examples

```
# V2 source — today
$ ls /tmp/board.*
/tmp/board.json    # was V2, now a partially-transformed V3 or V6 file
# No .v2.backup. No way back to the V2 original.

# V1 source — today
$ # Mid-chain failure scenario:
$ # 1. .v1.backup written by migrate_v1_to_v2
$ # 2. V1→V2 success, .v1.backup removed
$ # 3. V2→V3 success
$ # 4. split_graph fails
$ ls /tmp/board.*
/tmp/board.json    # now a partially-transformed V3 file
# Neither .v1.backup nor any other backup exists.
```

## Solution example

```
# After fix: V1 or V2 source with mid-chain failure
$ ls /tmp/board.*
/tmp/board.json        # broken partial migration
/tmp/board.v1.backup   # OR /tmp/board.v2.backup — original content, preserved
```

## Acceptance criteria

- `pre_v7_backup_path_for` returns `Some(path.v1.backup)` for V1 and `Some(path.v2.backup)` for V2 (in addition to existing V3/V4/V5/V6).
- Both orchestrators write the outer backup **before** any per-step migration runs (V1→V2, V2→V3, split_graph, v6_to_v7_rename).
- The per-step backup inside `migrate_v1_to_v2_sync` and `Migrator::migrate_v1_to_v2` is removed (assuming no external direct callers; verify by grep first).
- A test pins V1 → V7 success: backup is cleaned up after the full chain succeeds.
- A test pins V1 → V7 mid-chain failure (artificial poison after V2→V3 or during split_graph): `.v1.backup` is preserved.
- A test pins V2 → V7 success: backup is cleaned up.
- A test pins V2 → V7 failure: `.v2.backup` is preserved.
- The existing V3/V4/V5/V6 tests from KAN-650 continue to pass unchanged.
- Doc-comment on `pre_v7_backup_path_for` no longer claims "V1/V2 manage their own backups" or "V2 is shape-stable" — that reasoning was only ever true for the per-step transitions in isolation.

## Out of scope

- Adding fsync to the backup `copy` (covered by KAN-658).
- Restructuring the migration logging / report shape (covered by KAN-661).
cards.title: V1/V2 → V7 migration runs destructive chain without backup (pre-existing async + sync gap)

cards.description:
## Stakeholders

- **CLI users** reading error messages — currently see inconsistent capitalization depending on which code path produced the error.
- **MCP clients** parsing or summarizing error messages — capitalization difference is one more thing they must handle.
- **Future maintainers** adding new `NotFound`-style errors — currently must pick which casing without obvious guidance.

## Where

- `crates/kanban-domain/src/error.rs:86-87` — `NotFound { entity: &'static str, id: Uuid }` Display: `"{entity} {id} not found"`.
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/error.rs:91-96` — `NotFoundByName { entity: &'static str, name: String, available: Vec<String> }` Display: `"{entity} '{name}' not found..."`.
- All `KanbanError::not_found("...", id)` call sites — lowercase entity tags (e.g. `"sprint"`, `"card"`, `"column"`).
- All `KanbanError::not_found_by_name("...", name, available)` call sites — capitalized entity tags (e.g. `"Sprint"`, `"Card"`, `"Column"`).
- Pinned by KAN-655's new CLI tests at `crates/kanban-cli/tests/cli_integration.rs:1859-1916` — one test asserts `"Sprint"`, the other asserts `"sprint"`, deliberately matching the asymmetric paths.

## Why

A user sees `"sprint <uuid> not found"` when they pass an unknown UUID and `"Sprint 'foo' not found"` when they pass an unknown name. Same fundamental error class, two different shapes for the entity tag. It looks like a bug to anyone reading two consecutive errors from different paths. It also makes downstream tooling (LLM agents, log aggregators) treat the same error as two slightly different things.

The asymmetry is pre-existing — KAN-655 didn't introduce it but is now the first place tests explicitly pin the two casings to prevent regressions until a deliberate normalization happens.

## How

Pick one canonical convention and apply it everywhere.

**Option A — lowercase everywhere.** Match current `NotFound`. `"sprint 'foo' not found"`. Reads like sentence-mid-clause.

**Option B — capitalized everywhere.** Match current `NotFoundByName`. `"Sprint <uuid> not found"`. Reads like a complete sentence.

**Recommend B**: capitalized. Error messages typically render at the start of an error line (whether from `eprintln!`, MCP client display, or TUI banner) where sentence-leading capitalization is more readable. Also matches `kanban_domain::pluralize` and existing `Validation`/`UnsupportedFutureVersion` Display lines which start with capitalized words.

### Mechanical change

1. Update all `KanbanError::not_found("foo", id)` call sites: `"foo"` → `"Foo"`.
2. Update `is_not_found()` and `not_found_by_name` docs/tests if they assert lowercase.
3. Update the KAN-655 CLI test `test_card_create_with_assign_unknown_uuid_fails_with_useful_error` from `"sprint"` → `"Sprint"`. (The name test already uses `"Sprint"`.)
4. Update any TUI banner / CLI stderr golden tests that assert lowercase entity tags.
5. Update any other crate tests across `kanban-service`, `kanban-mcp`, `kanban-tui`, `kanban-cli` that match on lowercase tags.

`grep -rn 'KanbanError::not_found("' crates/` and `grep -rn '"sprint not found\|"card not found\|"column not found' crates/` give a complete call-site inventory.

## Offender example

```
$ kanban card create --board B --column TODO --title X --assign nonexistent
Error: Sprint 'nonexistent' not found

$ kanban card create --board B --column TODO --title X --assign 11111111-2222-3333-4444-555555555555
Error: sprint 11111111-... not found
```

Same logical error, two visible shapes.

## Solution example

```
$ kanban card create --board B --column TODO --title X --assign nonexistent
Error: Sprint 'nonexistent' not found

$ kanban card create --board B --column TODO --title X --assign 11111111-2222-3333-4444-555555555555
Error: Sprint 11111111-... not found
```

Both lead with `Sprint` regardless of the lookup-key shape.

## Acceptance criteria

- One canonical entity-tag convention (recommended: capitalized) applied across every `KanbanError::not_found*` call site.
- KAN-655's two CLI tests use the same casing for both name-path and UUID-path assertions.
- Workspace tests pass after the rename; no regressions in TUI / CLI / MCP error-message contents observed by users.
- Doc comment on `DomainError::NotFound` and `NotFoundByName` documents the convention so future variants pick the right casing without guessing.
- Changeset is `bump: patch` (user-visible message change is cosmetic, no API shape change).
cards.title: Normalize entity-tag casing across NotFound and NotFoundByName variants

cards.description:
Follow-up from KAN-483 (architecture audit). Scopes the decomposition of `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs` (832 lines) by interaction type: create, select, status, move, archive.

## Current structure

| Lines | Cluster | Entity / interaction |
|---|---|---|
| 1-9 | imports | shared |
| 12-26 | `handle_create_card_key`, `get_focused_column_id` (private) | create |
| 28-72 | `handle_toggle_card_completion`, `handle_card_selection_toggle`, `handle_clear_card_selection`, `handle_select_all_cards_in_view` | multi-select / completion entry points |
| 74-81 | `handle_set_selected_cards_priority` | multi-select priority dialog |
| 83-125 | `handle_assign_to_sprint_key` | sprint assignment dialog |
| 127-166 | `handle_order_cards_key`, `handle_toggle_sort_order_key` | sort/order |
| 168-206 | `handle_toggle_hide_assigned`, `handle_toggle_sprint_filter` | filter toggles |
| 208-230 | `handle_edit_card_key` | external editor for description |
| 232-256 | `toggle_card_completion` (private) | single-card completion |
| 258-301 | `toggle_selected_cards_completion` (private) | bulk completion |
| 303-394 | `create_card` (`pub`, called by dialog submit) | create-card logic incl. auto-complete batch |
| 396-538 | `handle_move_card_left/right`, `handle_move_card`, `move_selected_cards` | left/right column movement |
| 540-589 | `handle_archive_card`, `cursor_archive_anchor`, `start_delete_animations_for_selected`, `start_delete_animation` | archive / delete animation |
| 591-615 | `select_card_after_deletion` | post-archive selection |
| 617-657 | `handle_restore_card`, `start_restore_animations_for_selected`, `start_restore_animation` | restore animations |
| 659-697 | `restore_card` | restore-card command |
| 699-739 | `handle_delete_card_permanent`, `start_permanent_delete_animations_for_selected`, `start_permanent_delete_animation` | permanent delete |
| 741-771 | `handle_toggle_archived_cards_view` | view-mode toggle |
| 773-831 | `handle_manage_children_from_list` | relationship dialog setup (list-mode entry) |

## Proposed new files

After the split `card_handlers.rs` is deleted from `handlers/mod.rs`.

| File | Content | Target lines |
|---|---|---|
| `handlers/card_create_handlers.rs` | `handle_create_card_key`, `get_focused_column_id`, `create_card`, `handle_edit_card_key` | ~130 |
| `handlers/card_select_handlers.rs` | multi-select entry points + `handle_set_selected_cards_priority` | ~70 |
| `handlers/card_status_handlers.rs` | `handle_toggle_card_completion` + `toggle_card_completion` + `toggle_selected_cards_completion` | ~90 |
| `handlers/card_move_handlers.rs` | `handle_move_card_left/right`, `handle_move_card`, `move_selected_cards` | ~150 |
| `handlers/card_archive_handlers.rs` | archive + restore + permanent-delete + view-mode toggle | ~240 |

Sort/filter (~80 lines) merged into existing `filter_handlers.rs` (-> ~207 lines, under 300). Sprint assignment (~43 lines) merged into existing `sprint_handlers.rs` (-> ~264 lines). `handle_manage_children_from_list` (lines 773-831) moves into `card_detail_handlers.rs` (sibling card KAN-488).

## Cluster-to-destination

| Cluster | Current lines | Destination |
|---|---|---|
| create + create_card | 12-26, 303-394 | `card_create_handlers.rs` |
| multi-select / selection-mode entry points | 40-81 | `card_select_handlers.rs` |
| toggle completion (entry + private helpers) | 28-38, 232-301 | `card_status_handlers.rs` |
| move left/right (entry + private helpers) | 396-538 | `card_move_handlers.rs` |
| archive / delete animation + cursor anchor + post-archive selection | 540-615 | `card_archive_handlers.rs` |
| restore animation + `restore_card` command | 617-697 | `card_archive_handlers.rs` |
| permanent delete | 699-739 | `card_archive_handlers.rs` |
| `handle_toggle_archived_cards_view` | 741-771 | `card_archive_handlers.rs` |
| sort / filter entry points | 127-206 | existing `filter_handlers.rs` |
| sprint assignment dialog | 83-125 | existing `sprint_handlers.rs` |
| `handle_edit_card_key` | 208-230 | `card_create_handlers.rs` |
| `handle_manage_children_from_list` | 773-831 | `card_detail_handlers.rs` (KAN-488) |

## Public API impact

Methods called from `app/mod.rs`:
- `handle_create_card_key` (mod.rs:736, 904)
- `handle_archive_card` (mod.rs:746, 931)
- `handle_restore_card` (mod.rs:747, 1165)
- `handle_move_card_left/right` (mod.rs:749-750, 985, 989)
- `handle_edit_card_key`, `handle_toggle_card_completion`, etc.

All are inherent on `App`. Moving them to sibling files in the same `handlers/` module preserves call sites verbatim. `start_delete_animation` (`pub`) is called by `detail_view_handlers.rs:1216`; stays public. `select_card_after_deletion` (`pub`) and `restore_card` (`pub`) are called by `dialog_handlers.rs` / animation-tick code; stay public. `create_card` is called from a dialog submit handler; stays public.

## Sequencing (8 commits)

1. Move sort/filter cluster (lines 127-206) into `filter_handlers.rs`. Smallest, no dependencies.
2. Move sprint-assign dialog opener (lines 83-125) into `sprint_handlers.rs`.
3. Extract `card_create_handlers.rs` (lines 12-26, 208-230, 303-394).
4. Extract `card_select_handlers.rs` (lines 40-81).
5. Extract `card_status_handlers.rs` (completion).
6. Extract `card_move_handlers.rs`.
7. Extract `card_archive_handlers.rs` (archive + restore + permanent-delete + view-toggle).
8. Move `handle_manage_children_from_list` into `card_detail_handlers.rs` from KAN-488; collapse with `handle_manage_children` if it becomes one parameterised helper. Delete `card_handlers.rs` from `handlers/mod.rs`.

## Risks

- `get_focused_column_id` is the only private helper exclusively used by `create_card`; keep them together to avoid widening visibility.
- `toggle_card_completion` and `toggle_selected_cards_completion` (private) are only called from `handle_toggle_card_completion`; must move together.
- `handle_move_card` is private and only called from `handle_move_card_left/right`; move all three together. `move_selected_cards` is private and only used by `handle_move_card`.
- `start_delete_animation` is shared with `detail_view_handlers.rs:1216`. Keep `pub` until the bulk helper itself is also moved into `card_archive_handlers.rs`.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every new file is under 300 lines.
- `card_handlers.rs` no longer exists.
- `cargo test --package kanban-tui` passes at every commit boundary.
- All call sites in `app/mod.rs` remain unchanged.

## Related

Parent audit: KAN-483. Sibling cards: KAN-487 (navigation_handlers.rs), KAN-489 (detail_view_handlers.rs).

cards.description:
Follow-up from KAN-483 (architecture audit). Scopes the decomposition of `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs` (1254 lines) along the entity axis: Card vs Board vs Sprint detail. The file dissolves into three new files; nothing stays behind.

## Current structure

| Lines | Cluster | Entity |
|---|---|---|
| 1-14 | imports + viewport const | shared |
| 16-360 | `handle_card_detail_key` (~345 lines, one giant `match`) | Card detail mode |
| 362-634 | `handle_board_detail_key` (~273 lines) | Board detail mode |
| 636-985 | `handle_sprint_detail_key` (~350 lines), includes `CardListAction` matcher (~250 lines on its own) | Sprint detail mode |
| 987-1101 | `handle_manage_parents`, `handle_manage_children` (`pub(crate)`) | Card relationship dialog setup |
| 1103-1119 | `get_current_card_parents`, `get_current_card_children` | Card relationship queries |
| 1121-1212 | `navigate_to_selected_parent`, `navigate_to_selected_child` (private) | Card relationship in-detail navigation |
| 1214-1253 | `start_delete_animations_for_card_ids`, `toggle_completion_for_card_ids` | Bulk card ops (used by sprint detail) |

## Proposed new files

- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_detail_handlers.rs` (~250 lines after dedup of parent/child near-duplicates)
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/board_detail_handlers.rs` (~270 lines; the JSON-edit inline block at 407-459 should be extracted to a private `edit_board_settings_json` helper, knocking ~50 lines off)
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/sprint_detail_handlers.rs` (~280 lines after extracting `apply_sprint_card_action`)

## Cluster-to-destination

| Cluster | Current lines | Destination |
|---|---|---|
| `handle_card_detail_key` body | 16-360 | `card_detail_handlers.rs` |
| section-wrap helpers for j/k in `Parents`/`Children` (inline arms inside 47-204) | 47-204 | extract to `pub(crate) fn navigate_relationship_*` in `card_detail_handlers.rs` |
| `handle_manage_parents` / `_children` (near-duplicate dialog setup) | 987-1101 | `card_detail_handlers.rs` (collapse into one helper with `Direction { Parents, Children }`) |
| `get_current_card_parents` / `_children` | 1103-1119 | `card_detail_handlers.rs` |
| `navigate_to_selected_parent` / `_child` (near-duplicate) | 1121-1212 | `card_detail_handlers.rs` (collapse into one helper) |
| `handle_board_detail_key` body | 362-634 | `board_detail_handlers.rs`; the inline JSON-edit block at 407-459 extracted to private `edit_board_settings_json` helper |
| `handle_sprint_detail_key` body (key match + `CardListAction` match) | 636-985 | `sprint_detail_handlers.rs`; `CardListAction` matcher into private `apply_sprint_card_action` |
| `start_delete_animations_for_card_ids`, `toggle_completion_for_card_ids` | 1214-1253 | move to `card_handlers.rs` (KAN-488 will absorb these in its `card_archive_handlers.rs` / `card_status_handlers.rs` extractions) |

## Public API impact

Methods called from `app/mod.rs`:
- `handle_card_detail_key` (mod.rs:1073)
- `handle_board_detail_key` (mod.rs:1077)
- `handle_sprint_detail_key` (mod.rs:1079)

All three are inherent methods on `App`. Moving them to new sibling modules under `handlers/` preserves the call sites unchanged. `handle_manage_parents`/`_children` (`pub(crate)`) and the `get_current_card_*` getters are called from `card_handlers.rs:773-831` and `navigation_handlers.rs:495-502`; those calls stay verbatim. The `pub fn` accessors on getters (1103, 1112) should be tightened to `pub(crate)` in the move. `RELATIONSHIP_VIEWPORT_RAW` moves into `card_detail_handlers.rs`.

## Sequencing (4 commits)

1. Extract `card_detail_handlers.rs` first (self-contained; touches only `CardFocus`, relationship state, navigation history). Compile, test.
2. Extract `board_detail_handlers.rs`. It calls into `handle_create_sprint_key`/`handle_create_column_key`/`handle_*_column_*` (all still public on `App` in their original files). Compile, test.
3. Extract `sprint_detail_handlers.rs` last; it depends on `handle_activate_sprint_key`/`handle_complete_sprint_key`/`handle_carry_over_for_sprint` already in `sprint_handlers.rs`, plus the bulk helpers (move those to `card_handlers.rs` in this same step or just before).
4. Delete `detail_view_handlers.rs` and update `handlers/mod.rs`.

## Risks

- The j/k navigation in `handle_card_detail_key` (lines 47-204) uses many local closures over `self.relationship.*_list`. Splitting into helpers requires care with the `&mut self` borrow over `self.relationship.parents_list.update_item_count(...)`; the existing pattern (collect counts first, then mutate) is fine to replicate.
- `apply_sprint_card_action` extraction (sprint detail) must avoid double borrow: the original keeps `match self.sprint_view.panel { ... &mut self.sprint_view.* }` inline; the helper needs an explicit `panel: SprintTaskPanel` parameter rather than re-reading `self.sprint_view.panel`.
- `RELATIONSHIP_VIEWPORT_RAW = 5` must keep its current value (matches `ui.rs`); do not leave a copy behind.

## Acceptance criteria

- Each new file is under 300 lines.
- `detail_view_handlers.rs` no longer exists.
- `cargo test --package kanban-tui` passes at every commit boundary.

## Related

Parent audit: KAN-483. Sibling cards: KAN-487 (navigation), KAN-488 (card_handlers).

cards.description:
## Stakeholders

- **Release manager (Max)** — owns recovery when a publish step half-completes.
- **crates.io / Chocolatey / Homebrew downstream consumers** — see torn states (binary published one place, missing another) with no clear "is this the real 0.7.0?" answer.

## Where

- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — the `release` job (lines 14-220), the dependent `build-windows` job (lines ~230-300), and the `publish-chocolatey` job (lines ~310-400).

## Why

The `release` job is triggered by `pull_request: closed + merged == true`. Because a PR can only be merged once, **a failed step has no automatic re-trigger path** — and the workflow has no idempotency guards. Partial-completion failure modes:

| Failed step | Resulting state | Recovery |
|---|---|---|
| `Publish to crates.io` (line 100) | `master` has release commit + bumped Cargo.toml; no tag; no crates published | Manual: re-run job from GitHub UI, hope nothing was retried twice |
| `Tag version` (line 110) | crates.io published v0.7.0; no `v0.7.0` git tag; no GitHub Release | Manual tag + release create; any re-run of `Publish to crates.io` fails with "already exists" |
| `Create GitHub Release` (line 117) | Tagged + crates.io done; no Release object | Manual `gh release create v0.7.0` |
| `build-windows` | Release exists, Windows assets missing | Re-run that job only |
| `publish-chocolatey` (line 380) | Everything published except Chocolatey | Manual `choco push` from local; `choco push` rejects same-version forever, so a re-run after a real publish requires a `pkgrel`-style bump (which Chocolatey doesn't have) — practically you'd have to ship 0.7.1 |

The current workflow assumes every step succeeds on the first try. Reality says it won't.

## How

Two complementary changes:

**1. Make the `release` job idempotent — skip-if-tag-exists guard at the top.**

```yaml
- name: Skip if tag already published
  if: steps.check.outputs.has_changesets == 'true'
  id: tag_check
  run: |
    VERSION=$(grep -m1 'version = ' Cargo.toml | cut -d'"' -f2)
    if git rev-parse "v${VERSION}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "Tag v${VERSION} exists — release already published, skipping"
      echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
    else
      echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
    fi
```

Then gate every subsequent step on `steps.tag_check.outputs.skip != 'true'`. This means re-running the workflow on the merged PR (via the GH UI) is safe.

**2. Make per-publisher steps re-entrant where the registry permits it.**

- **crates.io**: wrap `cargo publish` in a per-crate "skip if already published" check. `cargo search <crate> --limit 1` returns the latest published version; compare to the target.
- **AUR PKGBUILD bump (line ~135)**: already idempotent (sha256 will be identical on re-run).
- **Homebrew tap bump (line ~180)**: check if the formula already has the target sha256 before pushing.
- **Chocolatey** (line ~380): truly non-idempotent — document that recovery requires a 0.7.1 patch release. See sibling card on chocolatey-specific guard.

**3. Add `continue-on-error: true` on `publish-chocolatey`** so the *GitHub Release* is not blocked by Chocolatey moderation queues or transient failures.

## Offender example

Today, if `publish-chocolatey` fails:
- `master` has the release commit.
- `v0.7.0` tag exists.
- crates.io has 0.7.0.
- AUR is bumped.
- Homebrew is bumped.
- Chocolatey is **not** updated.
- Re-running the workflow does nothing useful: `cargo publish` errors with "crate version `0.7.0` is already uploaded", aborting the job before reaching the Chocolatey step.

The only path forward is to manually `choco push` from a developer machine with the API key (security risk: that key normally only lives in GHA secrets).

## Solution example (compact)

```yaml
# Top of release job, after checkout
- id: tag_check
  run: |
    V=$(grep -m1 'version = ' Cargo.toml | cut -d'"' -f2)
    git rev-parse "v${V}" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
      && echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT" \
      || echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

# crates.io step, swap for:
- name: Publish to crates.io (idempotent)
  if: steps.tag_check.outputs.skip != 'true'
  run: nix run .#publish-crates -- --skip-already-published

# Chocolatey job
publish-chocolatey:
  needs: [release, build-windows]
  continue-on-error: true   # NEW
  ...
```

## Acceptance criteria

- Re-running the `release` workflow on an already-published version exits cleanly with no side effects.
- A simulated failure of `publish-chocolatey` (mock `choco push` to exit 1) still produces a complete crates.io + Homebrew + AUR + GitHub Release.
- A runbook entry exists at `docs/release-recovery.md` enumerating each step's recovery procedure.
cards.title: Make release.yml idempotent on partial-failure re-runs

cards.description:
Follow-up from KAN-483 (architecture audit). Scopes the decomposition of `crates/kanban-domain/src/in_memory_store.rs` (1391 lines). This file is the reference implementation of `DataStore` (40 methods) and `CommandStore` (4 methods), used by the service crate for testing and by the TUI for ephemeral state. 905 of the 1391 lines are tests.

## Current structure

| Block | Lines |
|---|---|
| `StoreState` struct + index helpers | 13-67 |
| `InMemoryStore` struct + lock helpers | 69-111 |
| `DataStore` impl (single 348-line block) split internally into Board / Column / Card / ArchivedCard / Sprint / Graph / Snapshot sections | 113-461 |
| `CommandStore` impl | 463-485 |
| Test module (~30 tests, 905 lines) | 487-1391 |

## Proposed module structure

Convert into a directory module `in_memory_store/`.

| File | Purpose | Target lines |
|---|---|---|
| `in_memory_store/mod.rs` | `InMemoryStore` struct, `new`, `Default`, `read_state`/`write_state`/`read_log`/`write_log`, the empty trait `impl` headers wired up via sibling `impl` blocks | ~80 |
| `in_memory_store/state.rs` | `pub(crate) struct StoreState` + index helpers (`add_card_to_column_index`, `remove_card_from_column_index`, `rebuild_card_column_index`) | ~70 |
| `in_memory_store/data_store_boards.rs` | `impl DataStore for InMemoryStore` for Board + Column methods | ~80 |
| `in_memory_store/data_store_cards.rs` | `impl DataStore for InMemoryStore` for Card + ArchivedCard methods (including `clear_sprint_from_*`) | ~150 |
| `in_memory_store/data_store_sprints_graph.rs` | `impl DataStore` for Sprint, Graph, Snapshot methods | ~110 |
| `in_memory_store/command_store_impl.rs` | `impl CommandStore for InMemoryStore` | ~30 |
| `in_memory_store/tests/state_index.rs` | `cards_by_column` index tests | ~180 |
| `in_memory_store/tests/data_store.rs` | Board/Column/Card/Archived/Sprint CRUD + sort + sprint clearing | ~260 |
| `in_memory_store/tests/snapshot_and_graph.rs` | Snapshot roundtrip, graph rollback, lock-safety, concurrency | ~220 |
| `in_memory_store/tests/command_store.rs` | `CommandStore` tests | ~60 |

Rust allows splitting a single `impl Trait for T` across multiple files within a crate; three separate `impl DataStore for InMemoryStore { ... }` blocks compile fine. Test sub-files are pulled in by a `#[cfg(test)] mod tests;` in `mod.rs`.

## Cluster-to-destination

| Cluster | Current lines | Destination |
|---|---|---|
| StoreState + index helpers | 13-67 | `state.rs` |
| `InMemoryStore` + lock guards | 69-111 | `mod.rs` |
| Board + Column DataStore methods (8) | 113-182 | `data_store_boards.rs` |
| Card + ArchivedCard DataStore methods (13) + sprint clearing | 183-349 | `data_store_cards.rs` |
| Sprint + Graph + Snapshot (11 methods) | 351-460 | `data_store_sprints_graph.rs` |
| CommandStore impl (4) | 463-485 | `command_store_impl.rs` |
| Index-maintenance tests (8) | 683-845 | `tests/state_index.rs` |
| CRUD tests (board/column/card/archived/sprint) | 504-682, 920-1125 | `tests/data_store.rs` |
| Snapshot/graph/concurrency tests (6) | 1127-1371 | `tests/snapshot_and_graph.rs` |
| CommandStore tests (2) | 1313-1390 | `tests/command_store.rs` |

## Public API impact

`lib.rs:64` re-exports `InMemoryStore`; preserved by `pub use in_memory_store::InMemoryStore` (path unchanged). Internal `StoreState` becomes `pub(crate)` in `state.rs`, only used by sibling `impl` files; no external surface change.

## Tests

~30 inline tests move to `in_memory_store/tests/*.rs` declared via `#[cfg(test)] mod tests;` from `mod.rs`. Helper functions `make_board`/`make_column`/`make_card` (492-502) move to `tests/common.rs`.

## Sequencing (4 commits)

1. `refactor(domain): extract StoreState into in_memory_store::state` (move struct + index helpers, no behavior change).
2. `refactor(domain): split InMemoryStore DataStore impl by entity kind` (three impl files for boards/cards/sprints+graph).
3. `refactor(domain): extract InMemoryStore CommandStore impl into sibling file`.
4. `test(domain): split InMemoryStore tests by concern` (four test sub-files + common helpers).

## Risks

- Three separate `impl DataStore` blocks must collectively cover every trait method. The compiler fails loudly if one is missing.
- `StoreState::rebuild_card_column_index` is called from `apply_snapshot` in `data_store_sprints_graph.rs`; needs `pub(crate)` visibility, not `pub`.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every file in `crates/kanban-domain/src/in_memory_store/` is under 300 lines.
- `cargo test --package kanban-domain` passes at every commit boundary.
- Public API at `kanban_domain::InMemoryStore` unchanged.

## Related

Parent audit: KAN-483.
cards.title: Decompose kanban-domain/src/in_memory_store.rs (1391 lines) into in_memory_store/ submodule

cards.description:
## Stakeholders

- **Release manager (Max)** — relies on `validate-release.sh` Step 5 to catch malformed manifests, missing license fields, oversized packages, and other crates.io packaging issues *before* the real `cargo publish` runs.
- **Downstream crates.io consumers** — would see torn workspace state (some crates at new version, others stuck at old) if the first real publish discovers a manifest defect midway through the topological order.

## Where

- `scripts/validate-release.sh:62-75` — Step 5 ("Validating individual crate dry-run publishes")
- Called from `scripts/publish-crates.sh:22` (`validate-release` runs as a pre-publish gate)
- Also called from `.github/workflows/release.yml:93-96` (`Validate release` step in the merge-triggered release job)

## Why

Step 5 of `validate-release.sh` is **a silent no-op today**. The script invokes:

```bash
cargo publish --dry-run --no-verify --offline --quiet --allow-dirty 2>&1 | grep -v "warning:" || true
```

`--offline` blocks the network fetches that `cargo publish --dry-run` needs (to resolve sibling crates against their published crates.io versions). Every crate's dry-run fails with:

```
error: attempting to make an HTTP request, but --offline was specified
```

The `|| true` then swallows the failure. The script reports:

```
✓ All crates passed dry-run validation
✅ Release validation complete - ready to publish!
```

**Smoke-tested locally on 2026-06-08 — confirmed all 9 crates errored out, all 9 were reported as passing.**

This directly undermines the release pipeline's correctness assumption. The workflow proceeds straight from "validation passed" to real `cargo publish`. If a crate has a malformed manifest, the first real publish discovers it — possibly after some sibling crates have already published, leaving the workspace in a torn state (kanban-core@0.7.0 published, kanban-domain still 0.6.0 because its publish failed).

## How

Three reasonable fixes, in increasing rigour. Pick one at implementation time.

### Option A — drop `--offline` (minimal change)

The release workflow runs in a CI runner with network access. There's no reason to constrain Step 5 to offline mode.

```bash
cargo publish --dry-run --no-verify --quiet --allow-dirty 2>&1 | grep -v "warning:" || true
```

But the `|| true` still swallows real failures. Need to drop that too:

```bash
if ! cargo publish --dry-run --no-verify --quiet --allow-dirty 2>&1 | grep -v "warning:"; then
  echo "❌ Dry-run publish failed for $crate"
  exit 1
fi
```

**Risk:** dry-run with network may resolve sibling crates against their *currently published* versions, which can be incompatible with the local source if an API change is in flight (see the existing comment at lines 62-67 explaining why `--offline` was originally chosen). This is the same problem `--no-verify` exists to avoid for the compile step. The fix needs to verify only the manifest/package shape, not the compile.

### Option B — replace dry-run with `cargo package`

`cargo package` produces the same `.crate` tarball that publish would upload, validates the manifest, and runs the same exclusion/inclusion rules — all without touching the network and without depending on sibling-version resolution.

```bash
cargo package --no-verify --allow-dirty --quiet 2>&1 || {
  echo "❌ cargo package failed for $crate"
  exit 1
}
```

This catches: malformed `Cargo.toml`, missing required fields (`license` / `description` / `repository`), oversized package (>10MB), excluded-file regressions, invalid `readme`/`license-file` paths. It does NOT catch: dependency-version resolution issues that only surface against the live crates.io index.

**Recommended.** This is what most workspace projects use for pre-publish manifest validation.

### Option C — split validation into two layers

- Network-free `cargo package --no-verify` per crate (catches manifest defects).
- Network-aware `cargo publish --dry-run --no-verify` for *only the first crate in topological order* (the one with no internal deps). This crate's dry-run succeeds against the live index, validates that the registry accepts the package format, and surfaces auth issues — without the sibling-version resolution problem.

Overkill for now; mention as a future enhancement.

## Risks

- **Pre-existing silent failure** — has been masking real packaging defects since whenever `--offline` was added. Could already have unreleased latent bugs hiding. **The fix may surface a real problem in the current 0.7.0 release candidate** that needs addressing before crates.io publish.
- **Step 5 newly being non-silent** means a malformed manifest now fails the merge-triggered workflow before push-to-master. This is the correct behaviour but is a workflow regression for anyone who currently relies on the validation being toothless.
- **`cargo package` is per-crate and sequential** — adds maybe 30s to the validate step. Acceptable.

## Offender example

Today, validate-release.sh on a hypothetically malformed `kanban-domain/Cargo.toml` (missing `license` field):

```
Step 5: Validating individual crate dry-run publishes...
  Validating crates/kanban-core...
error: attempting to make an HTTP request, but --offline was specified
  Validating crates/kanban-domain...
error: attempting to make an HTTP request, but --offline was specified
  ...
✓ All crates passed dry-run validation
✅ Release validation complete - ready to publish!
```

Workflow proceeds to `nix run .#publish-crates`. First publish (`kanban-core`) succeeds. Second publish (`kanban-domain`) fails:

```
error: missing field `license` in [package]
```

Workspace is now torn: kanban-core@0.7.0 published, kanban-domain stuck at 0.6.0. Recovery requires a 0.7.1 patch release (crates.io versions are immutable).

## Solution example

With Option B (`cargo package`):

```
Step 5: Validating individual crate packages...
  Validating crates/kanban-core...
  ✓ packaged ok
  Validating crates/kanban-domain...
error: missing field `license` in [package]
❌ cargo package failed for crates/kanban-domain
```

Workflow fails *before* the release commit is pushed. Maintainer fixes the manifest, re-merges the release PR, and the publish proceeds atomically.

## Acceptance criteria

- Step 5 of `scripts/validate-release.sh` actually fails when a crate has a packaging defect (verifiable via deliberate-failure test: temporarily remove `license` from one crate's Cargo.toml and confirm the script exits non-zero).
- The script no longer reports "All crates passed dry-run validation" when every dry-run errored.
- Workflow's `Validate release` step (release.yml:93-96) fails the job if any crate fails packaging.
- No regression in the existing 4 steps (workspace structure, version consistency, cross-crate deps, cargo check).
- One smoke-test invocation against the current `develop` HEAD confirms all 9 crates pass.

## Out of scope

- Adding Option C's two-layer split — file separately if Option B proves insufficient.
- Pre-flight check for crates.io registry availability (auth, rate-limits) — that surfaces naturally on first real publish.
- Migration of `validate-release.sh` to Rust or to a Cargo plugin — separate refactor.

## Sizing

~1pt. Single script change, single deliberate-failure test, no workflow surgery needed.

## Why pre-0.7.0

- The pipeline assumes Step 5 validates manifests. Today it doesn't. Releasing 0.7.0 with this defect in place means **any manifest regression introduced between 0.6.0 and 0.7.0 will surface mid-publish**, after some crates have shipped.
- Sprint 18's goal is exactly this: catch release-pipeline risks before the 0.7.0 cut.
- The fix is small (one script, one test).

## References

- KAN-649 ([PR #324](https://github.com/fulsomenko/kanban/pull/324)) — smoke-testing performed while reviewing the tag-guard / continue-on-error / runbook changes surfaced this defect.
- `scripts/validate-release.sh:62-67` — the existing comment explains why `--offline` and `--no-verify` were chosen for the compile-skip concern, but does not address the silent-failure problem.
cards.title: Fix validate-release.sh Step 5 silently passing when every dry-run fails

cards.description:
Follow-up from KAN-483 (architecture audit). Scopes the decomposition of `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/card_commands.rs` (1195 lines). The file owns the `CardCommand` enum and 10 forward+inverse command structs.

## Current structure

| Block | Lines |
|---|---|
| `CardCommand` enum + delegate impl | 10-77 |
| `RestoreCardSprintAttachment` (synthetic) | 84-112 |
| `UpdateCard` (heavy `capture_inverse`) | 114-186 |
| `CreateCard` | 188-277 |
| `MoveCard` | 279-316 |
| `RestoreCard` | 318-365 |
| `DeleteCard` (heavy `capture_inverse` reaching into dependency graph) | 367-438 |
| `ArchiveCards` | 440-497 |
| `AssignCardsToSprint` | 499-568 |
| `UnassignCardFromSprint` | 570-617 |
| `ApplyCardMetadata` | 619-672 |
| `CompactColumnPositions` | 674-712 |
| Tests (~22 tests) | 714-1195 |

## Proposed module structure

Convert into directory `commands/card_commands/`.

| File | Purpose | Target lines |
|---|---|---|
| `card_commands/mod.rs` | `CardCommand` enum + 3 delegate impls (`execute`/`description`/`capture_inverse`); re-exports each variant struct | ~90 |
| `card_commands/create.rs` | `CreateCard` struct + impl | ~95 |
| `card_commands/update.rs` | `UpdateCard` + impl (field-by-field inverse) | ~80 |
| `card_commands/move_card.rs` | `MoveCard` + impl | ~45 |
| `card_commands/lifecycle.rs` | `DeleteCard`, `RestoreCard`, `ArchiveCards` (archive <-> restore pair, delete inverses to import+graph) | ~190 |
| `card_commands/sprint.rs` | `AssignCardsToSprint`, `UnassignCardFromSprint`, `RestoreCardSprintAttachment` (synthetic) | ~150 |
| `card_commands/metadata.rs` | `ApplyCardMetadata` + impl | ~65 |
| `card_commands/compact.rs` | `CompactColumnPositions` + impl | ~45 |

## Cluster-to-destination

| Cluster | Current lines | Destination |
|---|---|---|
| `CardCommand` enum + delegates | 10-77 | `mod.rs` |
| `RestoreCardSprintAttachment` synthetic | 79-112 | `sprint.rs` |
| `UpdateCard` | 114-186 | `update.rs` |

## Public API impact

`commands/mod.rs:14` uses `pub use card_commands::*;`. That glob continues to work, but `mod.rs` of `card_commands/` must explicitly `pub use {create::CreateCard, update::UpdateCard, ...};` so each struct is re-exported at the same path. External surface `kanban_domain::commands::CreateCard`, `kanban_domain::commands::CardCommand` etc. stays unchanged.

## Tests

22 inline tests; all migrate into `card_commands/tests/*.rs`. Each test file imports `super::super::test_helpers::TestContext` (helper module lives in `commands/mod.rs` lines 359-378 and stays put; new path becomes `super::super::super::test_helpers::TestContext`).

## Sequencing (5 commits)

1. `refactor(domain): convert card_commands to a directory module, split CreateCard/UpdateCard/MoveCard`.
2. `refactor(domain): move archive/restore/delete card commands into lifecycle submodule`.
3. `refactor(domain): move sprint-related card commands into sprint submodule`.
4. `refactor(domain): move ApplyCardMetadata and CompactColumnPositions into dedicated submodules`.
5. `test(domain): split card_commands tests by concern`.

## Risks

- `DeleteCard::capture_inverse` (395-437) constructs `Command::Board(BoardCommand::Import(...))` and `Command::Dependency(...)` variants. Their `use super::*` paths need to become `use super::super::*;` (or explicit `crate::commands::*`). Recommend switching to explicit `crate::commands::*` at split time.
- `RestoreCardSprintAttachment` lives at top of file but is constructed by both `AssignCardsToSprint` and `UnassignCardFromSprint`; co-locating with `sprint.rs` is natural.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every file in `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/card_commands/` is under 300 lines.
- `cargo test --package kanban-domain` passes at every commit boundary.

Follow-up from KAN-483 (architecture audit). Scopes the decomposition of `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/sprint_commands.rs` (995 lines). Companion card to KAN-491 (card_commands). Same structural shape, different aggregate.

## Current structure

| Cluster | Current lines | Destination |

After the move `super` becomes `super::super`; quickest fix is to switch to explicit `crate::commands::*` paths.
