cards.title: Decompose kanban-domain/src/commands/sprint_commands.rs (995 lines) into sprint_commands/ submodule

cards.description:
Follow-up from KAN-483 (architecture audit). Scopes the decomposition of `crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs` (1317 lines). `KanbanContext` currently owns the backend, undo stack, dirty/conflict flags, config, accessors for every aggregate, persistence lifecycle, undo/redo control, batch operations with detailed reporting, private compute helpers for status/column invariants, and the full 45-method `KanbanOperations` impl.

This split is the precondition for the eventual `KanbanOperations` god-trait decomposition; once `ops_read.rs`/`ops_write.rs` exist with free-function read methods, splitting the trait into per-aggregate sub-traits becomes mechanical rather than a method rewrite.

## Diverging from the audit's initial proposal

KAN-483 suggested three new objects: `MutationDispatcher`, `SessionState`, and an `ops` free-function module. Refining that:

- `SessionState` as its own struct is right, but small (2 bools + undo stack); fold into a dedicated `session.rs` for readability.
- `MutationDispatcher` as a separate **object** is the wrong shape. `execute/undo/redo` are tightly coupled to the undo stack and dirty flag and call `Arc::clone(&self.backend)` plus `with_transaction`. Extracting them into a separate struct creates a triangle (`KanbanContext` -> `MutationDispatcher` -> `&mut Session`) that doesn't reduce coupling. Better: keep them as inherent methods on `KanbanContext` but move them into a separate `impl` block in `mutation.rs`. Rust's split-`impl` pattern is the right tool.
- `KanbanOperations` impl as free functions over `&dyn KanbanBackend` is right but **only for read-only methods**. The mutating trait methods all funnel through `execute(...)`, which needs `&mut self`. The clean split is `ops_read.rs` (free functions over `&dyn KanbanBackend`) and `ops_write.rs` (still an `impl` block on `KanbanContext`).

## Current structure

| Block | Lines |
|---|---|
| `BatchOperationResult`, `BatchOperationFailure` | 17-27 |
| `struct KanbanContext` + doc | 29-54 |
| Constructors (`open_deferred`, `open`) | 56-77 |
| Accessors (config, backend, data_store, replace_backend, list aggregates, graph, snapshot, apply_snapshot) | 79-131 |
| Data migration (`migrate_sprint_logs`) | 133-170 |
| Undo/redo + session flags | 172-296 |
| Persistence lifecycle (`reload`, `save`) | 298-316 |
| Batch helpers (3 `*_detailed` methods) | 318-544 |
| Compute helpers (4 private fns) | 546-696 |
| `impl KanbanOperations for KanbanContext` (51 trait methods) | 699-1317 |

## Proposed module structure

| File | Purpose | Target lines |
|---|---|---|
| `context.rs` | `KanbanContext` struct, fields, `open`/`open_deferred`, `app_config`, `data_store`, `backend`, `replace_backend`, `reload`, `save`, `migrate_sprint_logs`. Re-exports `BatchOperationResult`/`Failure` from `batch.rs`. | ~150 |
| `session.rs` | `SessionState { undo_stack, dirty, conflict_pending }` with all six flag accessors and `Default::default`. | ~80 |
| `mutation.rs` | `impl KanbanContext { execute, undo, redo, clear_history, can_undo/redo, *_depth }`. Transaction-driving code. | ~150 |
| `ops_read.rs` | `pub(crate)` free functions: `list_boards(backend: &dyn KanbanBackend)`, `get_board`, `list_columns`, `get_column`, `get_card`, `list_cards`, `list_archived_cards`, `find_cards_by_identifier`, `list_sprints`, `get_sprint`, `export_board`, `get_card_branch_name`, `get_card_git_checkout`. | ~200 |
| `ops_write.rs` | `impl KanbanOperations for KanbanContext` containing only the mutating half of the trait. Each mutating method is a 3-7 line wrapper that builds a `Command` and calls `self.execute(...)`. Read methods inside this `impl` delegate to `ops_read.rs` free functions. | ~250 |
| `batch.rs` | `BatchOperationResult`, `BatchOperationFailure`, the three `*_detailed` methods (as `impl KanbanContext`), and the four compute helpers (as free functions over `&dyn KanbanBackend` since they are read-only). | ~290 |

Total ~1120 lines (a bit lower after removing duplicated `use` blocks). Every file under 300.

## Cluster-to-destination

| Cluster | Current lines | Destination |
|---|---|---|
| `BatchOperationResult` / `Failure` types | 17-27 | `batch.rs` |
| `KanbanContext` struct + doc | 29-54 | `context.rs` |
| Constructors | 56-77 | `context.rs` |
| Cheap getters | 79-99 | `context.rs` |
| Aggregate list accessors | 101-131 | `context.rs` (facade calling `ops_read.rs` free fns) |
| `migrate_sprint_logs` | 133-170 | `context.rs` |
| `execute`/`undo`/`redo` | 172-245 | `mutation.rs` |
| Undo cursor + history accessors | 247-268 | `mutation.rs` |
| Dirty / conflict flags (8 methods) | 270-296 | `session.rs` (`SessionState`, surfaced through facade) |
| `reload`/`save` | 298-316 | `context.rs` |
| `archive_cards_detailed` | 320-374 | `batch.rs` |
| `move_cards_detailed` | 376-454 | `batch.rs` |
| `assign_cards_to_sprint_detailed` | 456-544 | `batch.rs` |
| Compute helpers (4 private fns) | 546-696 | `batch.rs` (as free fns over `&dyn KanbanBackend`) |
| `KanbanOperations` read methods | scattered 701-1281 | `ops_read.rs` |
| `KanbanOperations` write methods | scattered 702-1316 | `ops_write.rs` |

## State ownership

- **`backend: Arc<dyn KanbanBackend>`** stays on `KanbanContext`. Cheap to clone via `Arc::clone`; `mutation.rs` and `ops_write.rs` already do this before `with_transaction`. Free functions in `ops_read.rs` take `&dyn KanbanBackend` borrowed from the `Arc`.
- **`undo_stack: UndoStack`** moves into `SessionState` inside `session.rs`. `mutation.rs::execute/undo/redo` reach it via `self.session.undo_stack`.
- **`dirty: bool`** moves into `SessionState`. Set by `execute`/`undo`/`redo`/`migrate_sprint_logs`/`import_board`; cleared by `reload`/`mark_clean`/`replace_backend`. Facade methods on `KanbanContext` (`is_dirty`, `mark_dirty`, `mark_clean`) delegate.
- **`conflict_pending: bool`** moves into `SessionState`. Pure UI signal, four trivial accessors.
- **`app_config: AppConfig`** stays on `KanbanContext`. Only used in three places: `effective_default_card_prefix` in branch-name builders and `effective_default_sprint_prefix` in `create_sprint`. `ops_read::get_card_branch_name` takes `app_config: &AppConfig` as parameter; `ops_write::create_sprint` reads from `&self.app_config`.

## Public API impact

`KanbanContext`'s public surface is preserved 100%. Every method that callers (`tui_context.rs`, `cli/context.rs`, `mcp/context.rs`, `app/mod.rs`) reach for stays on `KanbanContext` as either:

- a direct inherent method (still in `context.rs` or moved-but-still-`impl KanbanContext` via `mutation.rs` / `batch.rs` split-impl), or
- a `KanbanOperations` trait method (impl block lives in `ops_write.rs` but the trait is still implemented for `KanbanContext`).

Free functions in `ops_read.rs` and compute helpers in `batch.rs` are `pub(crate)` only. `BatchOperationResult` / `BatchOperationFailure` continue to be re-exported from `lib.rs:26`. **No consumer changes needed.**

## Tests

`context.rs` has no inline `#[cfg(test)]` block today. All `KanbanContext` tests live in `crates/kanban-service/tests/`. Nothing moves.

Free functions in `ops_read.rs` and compute helpers in `batch.rs` become inline-testable for the first time. Recommend adding `#[cfg(test)]` blocks during the refactor for `compute_target_status_for_move`, `compute_target_column_for_status`, and `build_move_cards_batch`. Stub `KanbanBackend` via a small test double (or `mockall` on the existing trait).

## Sequencing (5 commits)

Each commit a Red-Green-Refactor cycle.

1. `refactor(service): extract SessionState into session.rs` - move `undo_stack`, `dirty`, `conflict_pending` into `SessionState`; `KanbanContext` keeps the same six accessor methods as 1-line delegations.
2. `refactor(service): extract batch helpers and detailed ops into batch.rs` - move `BatchOperationResult`/`Failure`, the three `*_detailed` methods, and the four compute helpers. Add inline unit tests for compute helpers.
3. `refactor(service): split read methods of KanbanOperations into ops_read.rs` - add `pub(crate)` free functions in `ops_read.rs`; the trait `impl` block delegates read methods to them.
4. `refactor(service): move write methods of KanbanOperations into ops_write.rs` - move the mutating half of the trait `impl` to its own file.
5. `refactor(service): move execute/undo/redo into mutation.rs` - final extraction. `context.rs` ends at ~150 lines.

## Risks

- **`&mut self` vs `&self` boundary on `KanbanOperations`**: the trait mixes read methods (`&self`) and write methods (`&mut self`). Rust requires a single `impl Trait for T` block, so reads and writes cannot live in different files as separate `impl` blocks. Resolution: keep the single `impl` block in `ops_write.rs`; each read method body delegates one-line to a free function in `ops_read.rs`. The file boundary is logical, not syntactic.
- **`execute` borrows the backend via `Arc::clone` and uses interior closures over `&dyn DataStore`**: moving to `mutation.rs` keeps the same borrow shape.
- **`with_transaction` takes `&mut dyn FnMut()`**: the closure captures `cmds`, `per_cmd_inverses`, `backend` via shared refs. The current borrow shape is sensitive (`&mut ||` at line 186). When moving, preserve the closure capture exactly.
- **Async edges**: only `open`, `reload`, `save` are `async`. Keep them in `context.rs`. `execute`/`undo`/`redo` stay sync.
- **`replace_backend` clears `undo_stack` and `dirty`**: once those move to `SessionState`, `replace_backend` calls `self.session.clear_for_new_backend()`. Do not lose the `dirty=false` reset.
- **`migrate_sprint_logs` clears the undo stack mid-method** (line 160). Same delegation.

## Order vs other cards

This card runs **before** the `KanbanOperations` god-trait decomposition (a separate audit follow-up). With `ops_read.rs`/`ops_write.rs` in place, splitting the trait into per-aggregate sub-traits is a re-shuffle plus an `impl` split per file, not a method rewrite. Doing it in the other order forces the file split to be redone against a moving target.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every file in `crates/kanban-service/src/` (the post-split set: `context.rs`, `session.rs`, `mutation.rs`, `ops_read.rs`, `ops_write.rs`, `batch.rs`) is under 300 lines.
- `cargo test --package kanban-service` passes at every commit boundary.
- Public surface of `KanbanContext` (every method called from `tui_context.rs`/`cli/context.rs`/`mcp/context.rs`) is unchanged.

## Related

Parent audit: KAN-483. Sibling: KAN-484 (config.rs).
cards.title: Decompose kanban-service/src/context.rs (1317 lines) into focused service modules

cards.description:
Follow-up from KAN-483 (architecture audit). Scopes the decomposition of `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs` (1593 lines). The file holds `KanbanMcpServer`, request schemas for every tool, and a `#[tool_router]`-decorated `impl` containing roughly 39 tool methods.

## Current structure

| Band | Lines | Content |
|---|---|---|
| Preamble & imports | 1-25 | module decls, `pub use server::McpServer`, imports |
| Helpers band | 27-261 | JSON/error shims, parsers (`parse_priority`, `parse_status`, `parse_datetime`), locking primitives, `McpResolve` shim trait, `card_board` helper, macros `mutating_op!`, `read_op!` |
| Schema structs | 263-633 | 32 `#[derive(JsonSchema, Deserialize)]` request structs, currently `pub` |
| Server struct & ctor | 639-658 | `KanbanMcpServer`, `new` |
| Tool router impl | 664-1386 | `#[tool_router] impl KanbanMcpServer { ... }` with ~39 `#[tool]` methods |
| `ServerHandler` impl | 1392-1406 | `#[tool_handler]` |
| Inline tests | 1408-1593 | Helpers only (parsers, error shims) |

## Tool-method clustering

- Boards (5): 666-739
- Columns (6): 741-833
- Cards (9): 835-1050
- Card-sprint (2): 1052-1082
- Card utilities (2): 1084-1110
- Bulk-card (3): 1112-1162
- Sprints (8): 1164-1304
- Sprint carry-over (1): 1306-1327
- Import/Export (2): 1329-1355
- Undo/Redo (2): 1357-1385

Total: 39 `#[tool]` methods. Each request struct is 1:1 with a tool method (no shared schemas).

## Proposed module structure

```
crates/kanban-mcp/src/
  lib.rs                       (~30 lines: pub mods + re-exports)
  context.rs                   (unchanged, 307 lines)
  server.rs                    (unchanged)
  helpers.rs                   (~110 lines)
  tools/
    mod.rs                     (~70 lines, router stub)
    boards.rs                  (~110 lines)
    columns.rs                 (~140 lines)
    cards.rs                   (~250 lines)
    card_sprint.rs             (~60 lines)
    card_utilities.rs          (~60 lines)
    bulk_cards.rs              (~90 lines)
    sprints.rs                 (~230 lines)
    sprint_carry_over.rs       (~50 lines)
    import_export.rs           (~50 lines)
    undo_redo.rs               (~50 lines)
```

## rmcp macro constraint (critical)

`rmcp`'s `#[tool_router]` proc-macro walks a **single** `impl` block, picks every `fn` it sees tagged with `#[tool(...)]`, and generates the static `Self::tool_router()` registration table that `KanbanMcpServer::new` calls at line 655. Splitting `#[tool]` methods across multiple `impl` blocks in different files is **not supported** by `rmcp`'s macro.

Two viable options:

### Option A (recommended)

Keep one `#[tool_router] impl` block in `tools/mod.rs`, but make each method a one-line delegate to a free `pub(crate)` async function in the per-entity file:

```rust
#[tool_router]
impl KanbanMcpServer {
    #[tool(description = "Create a new kanban board")]
    pub async fn tool_create_board(&self, p: Parameters<boards::CreateBoardRequest>)
        -> Result<CallToolResult, McpError> { boards::create_board(&self.ctx, p.0).await }
    // ... 38 more one-line shims
}
```

Each per-entity file owns a `pub(crate) async fn create_board(ctx: &Arc<Mutex<McpContext>>, req: CreateBoardRequest) -> Result<CallToolResult, McpError>` holding the real body. `tools/mod.rs` ends up ~250 lines of mechanical shims (under 300), and the body logic lives in small focused files.

### Option B

Check if the installed `rmcp` exposes `ToolRouter::merge` (newer releases support it). If present, each per-entity file owns its own `#[tool_router] impl`, and `mod.rs` calls `ToolRouter::merge(boards::tool_router(), columns::tool_router(), ...)`. Cleaner but version-dependent.

**Start with A** (works on any rmcp version); upgrade to B in a follow-up if `merge` is available.

## Content allocation

- **`lib.rs`**: `pub mod context; pub mod server; mod helpers; mod tools;` plus `pub use server::McpServer;` and `pub use tools::KanbanMcpServer;`. Schema structs become `pub(crate)` and live with their tool file.
- **`helpers.rs`**: lifts `lib.rs:27-261` verbatim - error shims, parsers, `locked_read`/`locked_write`, `McpResolve` trait + impl, `card_board`, and macros. Convert macros to async functions taking a closure (similar to `locked_write`) for cleaner re-export.
- **`tools/mod.rs`**: declares per-entity sub-modules; holds `KanbanMcpServer` struct (639-658), the single `#[tool_router] impl KanbanMcpServer { ... }` stub, and `#[tool_handler] impl ServerHandler` (1392-1406).
- **`tools/<entity>.rs`**: holds the cluster's request structs (as `pub(crate)`) and plain `pub(crate) async fn` bodies.

Target sizes stay under 300 lines; the largest (`cards.rs` ~250, `sprints.rs` ~230) include both schemas and method bodies.

## Public API impact

External consumers: `kanban-mcp` binary (via `server.rs::McpServer`) and integration tests. The only public surface that matters today is `pub use server::McpServer;` and `pub struct KanbanMcpServer`. Request structs are `pub` but not part of the external contract (MCP clients deserialize by JSON shape, not by import).

After the split:
- Keep `pub`: `McpServer` (via `pub use`), `KanbanMcpServer`, `context::McpContext`.
- Demote to `pub(crate)`: every request struct, every helper fn, `McpResolve` trait, `card_board`, both macros (or their async-fn replacements), per-entity tool functions.

This aligns with the project's "prefer `pub(crate)` over `pub`" rule.

## Tests

Inline tests at lines 1408-1593 cover only helper functions. Move them verbatim into `helpers.rs` as its `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`. They exercise items that all live in that file after the move, so no `super::` paths break.

## Sequencing (5-6 commits)

1. `refactor(mcp): extract helpers module` - create `helpers.rs`, move 27-261 plus inline tests. Verify `cargo test -p kanban-mcp` green.
2. `refactor(mcp): introduce tools module with single router stub` - create `tools/mod.rs`, move the `KanbanMcpServer` struct, `#[tool_router] impl`, and `#[tool_handler] impl ServerHandler`. `lib.rs` becomes a slim re-exporter.
3. `refactor(mcp): split board, column, sprint tools into per-entity files` - move the three largest clusters using Option A pattern.
4. `refactor(mcp): split card, card-sprint, card-utilities, bulk-card tools` - move the card family.
5. `refactor(mcp): split carry-over, import/export, undo/redo tools` - move the small remaining clusters; verify every file is under 300 lines.
6. `refactor(mcp): tighten visibility to pub(crate)` - sweep request structs and helpers to `pub(crate)`.

## Risks

- **rmcp macro single-impl constraint** (see above): dominant risk. Validate Option A with a one-method experiment **before** committing step 2.
- **Macro re-export**: `mutating_op!` and `read_op!` are `macro_rules!`. Re-exporting needs `#[macro_export]` plus `pub(crate) use crate::mutating_op;`, or converting them into async functions taking a closure. Converting is cleaner; fold into commit 1.
- **`McpResolve` trait reach**: defined and `impl`'d in the same file. Moving to `helpers.rs` while `McpContext` lives in `context.rs` is fine (orphan rules permit; trait is local).
- **`#[tool(description=...)]` attribute drift**: tool descriptions are user-visible. When moving to one-line shims, copy descriptions verbatim. Diff-only review pass at the end of step 5.
- **Public API breakage**: external `pub use` users of request structs would break in step 6. Quick grep: `rg 'kanban_mcp::(Create|Get|Update|Delete|List|Move|Assign|Unassign|Archive|Restore|Carry|Export|Import)' --type rust crates/`. If hits surface, leave those specific structs `pub`.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every file under `crates/kanban-mcp/src/` is under 300 lines (post-split).
- `cargo test --package kanban-mcp` passes at every commit boundary.
- `kanban-mcp` binary still starts and serves every tool (manual smoke).
- Tool `description` strings byte-identical before and after.

## Related

Parent audit: KAN-483.
cards.title: Decompose kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs (1593 lines) into per-entity tools submodules

cards.description:
Follow-up from KAN-483 (architecture audit). Scopes the decomposition of `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs` (1811 lines) into a `sqlite_store/` directory keyed by aggregate. Every new file targets <=300 LOC.

## Current structure

| Range | Content |
|---|---|
| 1-18 | imports + `const SCHEMA: &str = include_str!("schema.sql")` (line 18) |
| 21-25 | `pub struct SqliteStore { pool, path, instance_id }` |
| 27-36 | free `fn run` (blocking helper, runtime-flavor `debug_assert!`) |
| 38-67 | conversion helpers: `db_err`, `ser_err`, `p_uuid`, `p_dt`, `p_enum`, `fmt_dt`, `opt_dt` |
| 69-230 | row parsers: `row_to_board` 71-116, `row_to_column` 118-133, `row_to_card` 135-166, `row_to_sprint` 168-191, `rows_to_graph` 193-215, `row_to_sprint_log` 217-230 |
| 234-1102 | `impl SqliteStore`: schema/open 235-402, command log 404-468, board/column/card/sprint/archived/graph write paths and async query helpers |
| 1104-1577 | `impl DataStore for SqliteStore` (sync wrappers calling `run(...)`) |
| 1579-1625 | `impl PersistenceStore for SqliteStore` |
| 1627-1811 | `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (~184 lines, 7 tests) |

## Aggregate-write clusters

| Aggregate | Row parser | Async write / helper | Sync `DataStore` impl | Delete path |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Board | `row_to_board` 71-116 | `fetch_board_aux` 470-499, `write_board_with_conn` 501-583, `write_board_async` 585-590, `fetch_all_board_aux` 874-910, `list_boards_async` 912-935 | `get_board` 1107-1131, `list_boards` 1133-1135, `upsert_board` 1137-1139 | `delete_board` 1141-1150 |
| Column | `row_to_column` 118-133 | `write_column_with_conn` 1008-1031, `write_column_async` 1033-1035, `list_all_columns_async` 937-946 | 1154-1188 | `delete_column` 1190-1199, `delete_columns_by_board` 1201-1210 |
| Card | `row_to_card` 135-166, `row_to_sprint_log` 217-230 | `fetch_sprint_logs_for_card` 592-602, `write_card_with_conn` 604-664, `write_card_async` 666-671, `fetch_sprint_logs_batch` 673-697, `fetch_cards_with_filter` 699-730 | 1214-1357 | `delete_card` 1304-1316, `delete_cards_by_columns` 1318-1336 |
| Sprint | `row_to_sprint` 168-191 | `write_sprint_with_conn` 1037-1067, `write_sprint_async` 1069-1071, `list_all_sprints_async` 948-958 | 1494-1530 | `delete_sprint` 1532-1541, `delete_sprints_by_board` 1543-1552 |
| Archived | (reuses `row_to_card`) | `write_archived_card_with_conn` 1073-1094, `write_archived_card_async` 1096-1101, `list_archived_cards_async` 960-995 | 1361-1490 | `delete_archived_card` 1404-1419 |
| Graph | `rows_to_graph` 193-215 | `get_graph_with_conn` 732-743, `modify_graph_async` 745-752, `write_graph_with_conn` 754-782, `write_graph_async` 784-789, `get_graph_async` 997-1006 | 1556-1566 | (truncates inside `write_graph_with_conn`) |
| Command log | (no row parser) | `append_command_batch` 408-423, `load_all_command_batches` 425-437, `truncate_command_log_after` 439-447, `shift_command_log` 449-468 | none (unwired) | (truncate/shift are the delete paths) |
| Snapshot | - | `snapshot_async` 791-806, `apply_snapshot_async` 808-872 | `snapshot` 1570-1572, `apply_snapshot` 1574-1576 | - |

## Proposed module structure

Convert to `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store/`:

| File | Purpose | Target LOC |
|---|---|---|
| `mod.rs` | `SqliteStore` struct + imports + `impl DataStore` (thin `run(...)` wrappers only). If approaching 300, split into `data_store_impl.rs`. | ~260 |
| `runtime.rs` | `pub(crate) fn run`, `db_err`, `ser_err`, `p_uuid`, `p_dt`, `p_enum`, `fmt_dt`, `opt_dt`. Pure helpers, no `&self`. | ~50 |
| `schema.rs` | `pub(crate) const SCHEMA`, `pub(crate) async fn migrate(&Pool)`, `pub(crate) async fn drop_legacy_command_log_if_present(&Pool)`, `pub(crate) async fn load_or_create_instance_id(&Pool)`. | ~110 |
| `open.rs` | `impl SqliteStore { pub async fn open(...), pub fn pool(), pub async fn checkpoint() }`. Calls `schema.rs`. | ~70 |
| `board.rs` | `row_to_board`, `fetch_board_aux`, `fetch_all_board_aux`, `list_boards_async`, `write_board_with_conn`, `write_board_async`, and the board chunk of `impl DataStore` (4 fns). | ~250 |
| `column.rs` | `row_to_column`, column writers, column `DataStore` methods (6 fns). | ~140 |
| `card.rs` | `row_to_card`, `row_to_sprint_log`, `fetch_sprint_logs_for_card`, `fetch_sprint_logs_batch`, `fetch_cards_with_filter`, `write_card_with_conn`, `write_card_async`, card `DataStore` methods (10 fns incl. counts and `clear_sprint_from_cards`). | ~280 |
| `sprint.rs` | `row_to_sprint`, sprint writers, sprint `DataStore` methods (6 fns). | ~170 |
| `archived.rs` | `write_archived_card_with_conn`, `write_archived_card_async`, `list_archived_cards_async`, archived `DataStore` methods (6 fns). | ~250 |
| `graph.rs` | `rows_to_graph`, graph helpers, graph `DataStore` methods (3 fns). | ~130 |
| `snapshot.rs` | `snapshot_async`, `apply_snapshot_async`, snapshot `DataStore` methods (2 fns), and `impl PersistenceStore`. | ~150 |
| `command_log.rs` (feature-gated; see below) | The 4 command-log methods. | ~70 |
| Integration tests | Move `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` to `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/tests/sqlite_store.rs`. | ~190 |

`schema.sql` itself stays at `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/schema.sql`; `include_str!` path adjusts to `"../schema.sql"`.

## Connection / pool plumbing

- Pool owned by `SqliteStore { pool: Pool<Sqlite>, ... }` (line 22). Stays a field on the struct in `mod.rs`. Each per-aggregate module uses `&self.pool` via methods on `impl SqliteStore { ... }` defined in that file (Rust permits split `impl` blocks across the module's sub-files).
- `run` blocker (27-36) moves to `runtime.rs` as `pub(crate) fn run`. The `tokio::runtime::Handle::current().runtime_flavor()` check stays in `open()` (in `open.rs`) where it produces a real `KanbanError` instead of `debug_assert!`.
- `pool()` (392-394) and `checkpoint()` (396-402) stay in `open.rs` alongside `open()`. `pool()` is the only API surface `tests/data_store.rs` uses for ad-hoc SQL probing.

## `pub` audit

| Item | Location | Callers | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `pub struct SqliteStore` | 21 | every backend wire-up, tests | keep `pub` |
| `pub async fn SqliteStore::open` | 235 | `store_manager.rs`, `sqlite_backend.rs`, tests | keep `pub` |
| `pub fn SqliteStore::pool` | 392 | `tests/data_store.rs` only | keep `pub` with `#[doc(hidden)]`; legitimate escape hatch for integration tests |
| `pub async fn SqliteStore::checkpoint` | 396 | `sqlite_backend.rs:196` | keep `pub` |
| `pub async fn append_command_batch`, `load_all_command_batches`, `truncate_command_log_after`, `shift_command_log` | 408-468 | none | downgrade to `pub(crate)` (see "Command log") |

All currently-free helpers and row parsers are already module-private; after the split they should be `pub(crate)` so sibling sub-modules can call them.

## Command log methods

The four methods at 408-468 are unused outside this file. The doc-comment on line 404 says "not yet wired through SqliteBackend".

Recommendation: isolate in `sqlite_store/command_log.rs` behind `pub(crate)` visibility, gated behind `#[allow(dead_code)]` (or a Cargo feature if desired). Preserves the work for the imminent wiring task at ~70 LOC.

Source comments at 260-264, 307-309, 328-329, 339-342 mention `KAN-405` and `KAN-191`. Per CLAUDE.md (no task-ID references), strip the kanban-ticket prose during the schema-module move. Keep one sentence stating *what* the legacy drop does, not *which ticket* mandated it.

## Sequencing (8 commits, each Red-Green-Refactor)

1. `refactor(persistence-sqlite): move tests out of sqlite_store.rs` - relocate `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` to `tests/sqlite_store.rs`. Pure file move. Shrinks the file to ~1630 LOC; decouples tests from internals.
2. `refactor(persistence-sqlite): split sqlite_store.rs into a directory` - create `sqlite_store/mod.rs` containing the entire current body verbatim; delete `sqlite_store.rs`. Adjust `include_str!("../schema.sql")`. Mechanical step.
3. `refactor(persistence-sqlite): extract runtime helpers` - pull `run`, `db_err`, etc. into `runtime.rs` as `pub(crate)`.
4. `refactor(persistence-sqlite): extract schema and migration into schema.rs` - move `SCHEMA`, `drop_legacy_command_log_if_present`, `migrate`, `load_or_create_instance_id`. Drop kanban ticket IDs from comments.
5. `refactor(persistence-sqlite): extract graph aggregate` - smallest (~130 LOC). Validates the split pattern with the smallest blast radius.
6. `refactor(persistence-sqlite): extract column and sprint aggregates` - structurally simple, no shared private helpers; one commit.
7. `refactor(persistence-sqlite): extract board, card, archived, snapshot aggregates` - the heavy ones. Order: card-related shared helpers settle in `card.rs` before `archived.rs` and `snapshot.rs` import them.
8. `refactor(persistence-sqlite): isolate command_log behind pub(crate)` - move the four methods to `command_log.rs` and tighten visibility.

## Risks

- **Shared private helpers across aggregates**: `row_to_card` is used by `card.rs`, `archived.rs`, and `snapshot.rs` (indirectly via `fetch_cards_with_filter`). `fetch_sprint_logs_batch` is used by `fetch_cards_with_filter` and `list_archived_cards_async` and `list_archived_cards_by_columns`. Mitigation: `card.rs` owns both; siblings import via `use super::card::{row_to_card, fetch_sprint_logs_batch}`.
- **Transactions that span aggregates**: `apply_snapshot_async` (808-872) opens a single transaction and calls every `write_*_with_conn` against the same `&mut SqliteConnection`. Mitigation: every `*_with_conn` helper exposed as `pub(crate)` from its module with stable signature `(conn: &mut sqlx::SqliteConnection, &T) -> KanbanResult<()>`.
- **`Pool<Sqlite>` ownership**: per-aggregate methods need `&self.pool` (or `self.pool.begin()`); cannot live as free `fn`s; must remain `impl SqliteStore` blocks across sub-files. Well-supported in Rust; reviewers must understand the intent.
- **`run` blocker depends on Tokio flavor**: `debug_assert!` at line 30 is non-fatal in `--release`. After the move it stays in `runtime.rs`. The hard check stays in `open()` (237-244). Keep both.
- **`tests/data_store.rs` uses `store.pool()`**: keep `pool()` `pub` with `#[doc(hidden)]`.
- **`KAN-405` / `KAN-191` comments**: per CLAUDE.md, source comments referencing task IDs should not appear. Step 4 strips them; reviewers should not interpret the strip as a behaviour change.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every file in `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store/` is under 300 lines.
- `cargo test --package kanban-persistence-sqlite` passes at every commit boundary.
- No KAN-* task IDs remain in source comments or tracing strings under the new directory.
- The `command_log.rs` module is either correctly gated and unwired, or has a follow-up card to wire it.

## Related

Parent audit: KAN-483.
cards.title: Decompose kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs (1811 lines) into sqlite_store/ by aggregate

cards.description:
Umbrella card for decomposing `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs` (2975 lines). Broken down into seven sub-cards, each independently mergeable. Land in roughly this order; KAN-503 must land last.

## Sub-cards

| Card | Scope | Risk |
|---|---|---|
| **KAN-497** | Extract trivial helpers: `terminal`, `clipboard_actions` | none |
| **KAN-498** | Fold App methods into existing sub-modules (`mode`, `ui_state`, `animation`, `sprint_view`, `persistence`) plus new `error_log_view` | very low |
| **KAN-499** | Extract `construct` and `lifecycle` (App::new family, quit, save-worker) | low |
| **KAN-500** | Extract startup storage-file dialog (`storage_dialog`); depends on KAN-499 for `App::test_default` | low |
| **KAN-501** | Extract read/command/io/edit clusters (`queries`, `commands`, `io`, `edit_field`) | low |
| **KAN-502** | Extract `key_dispatch/` with per-mode split (the hot path) | medium |
| **KAN-503** | Extract `run/` with tokio::select arm split, then final `mod.rs` trim and clippy audit | high (lands last) |

After all seven sub-cards merge, the entire `app/` directory should respect the <300-line guideline (with the possible exception of `run/mod.rs` if the arm split leaves it at ~220 lines, which is acceptable).

## Sub-card dependencies

- KAN-500 depends on KAN-499 (uses `App::test_default`).
- KAN-503 depends on every other sub-card landing first; it consumes the new methods that earlier cards relocate.
- All other sub-cards are independent of each other.

## Original analysis

The detailed file-by-file analysis below was the source material for the seven sub-cards. Kept for reference and as ground truth if any sub-card needs to re-derive its scope.

---

Follow-up from KAN-483 (architecture audit). Scopes the decomposition of `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs` (2975 lines). This is the **largest single file in the workspace**. The `app/` directory was created in KAN-224 by splitting an earlier `app.rs`; the `App` struct and a large `impl App` block remain in `mod.rs` and have not been distributed across the existing sub-modules.

## Current structure

| Range | Content |
|---|---|
| 1-37 | sub-module declarations + re-exports |
| 39-67 | external `use` block |
| 69-74 | free fn `default_store_manager` |
| 76-113 | `pub struct App` (37 public fields) |
| 115-149 | `enum StorageBackendChoice` + `impl` + free fn `swap_known_extension` |
| 151-200 | `ExportStep`, `ExportFormat`, `ExportDialogState` + `impl` |
| 202-219 | `enum MigrationState`, `enum CardField`, `enum BoardField` |
| 221-2388 | first `impl App` block (giant) |
| 2390-2405 | free fns `setup_terminal`, `restore_terminal` |
| 2407-2411 | `impl Default for App` |
| 2413-2462 | second `impl App` with `test_default` |
| 2464-2974 | `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (12 tests) |

Types defined: `App`, `StorageBackendChoice`, `ExportStep`, `ExportFormat`, `ExportDialogState`, `MigrationState`, `CardField`, `BoardField`. No traits implemented on `App` directly in this file (only `Default`).

## Method-cluster table (first `impl App`, lines 221-2388)

| Cluster | Summary | Lines | Destination |
|---|---|---|---|
| Construction | `new`, `new_with_store` | 221-338 | new `app/construct.rs` |
| Quit / lifecycle | `quit`, `handle_quit_key`, `spawn_save_worker` | 340-456 | new `app/lifecycle.rs` |
| Mode stack | `push_mode`, `pop_mode`, `is_dialog_mode`, `get_base_mode`, `open_dialog` | 458-477, 525-527 | existing `app/mode.rs` (extend with `impl App` methods) |
| Error log accessors | `with_error_log`, `with_error_log_mut`, `open_error_log`, `set_error_log`, `error_log_arc`, `handle_error_log_mode` | 479-523 | new `app/error_log_view.rs` |
| Banner | `set_error`, `set_success`, `clear_banner` | 658-668 | existing `app/ui_state.rs` |
| Startup file dialog | `maybe_push_startup_file_dialog`, `handle_choose_storage_file_dialog`, `adopt_storage_file`, types `StorageBackendChoice` + `swap_known_extension` | 115-149, 529-656 | new `app/storage_dialog.rs` |
| Key dispatch | `keycode_matches_binding_key`, `execute_action`, `handle_key_event`, `handle_search_mode`, `handle_archived_cards_view_mode`, `scroll_help_into_view`, `handle_help_mode` | 670-1260 | new `app/key_dispatch.rs` (still likely > 300 lines; may need intra-fn extraction) |
| Animation tick | `handle_animation_tick`, `complete_restore_animation` | 1262-1361 | existing `app/animation.rs` |
| Board/card lookups | `get_board_card_count`, `get_sorted_board_cards`, `get_selected_card_in_context`, `get_selected_card_id`, `select_card_by_id`, `get_card_by_id`, `get_card_for_detail_view` | 1363-1466 | new `app/queries.rs` |
| Sprint task lists | `populate_sprint_task_lists`, `apply_sort_to_sprint_lists` | 1468-1544 | existing `app/sprint_view.rs` |
| Commands / undo / view | `execute_command`, `execute_commands_batch`, `prepare_frame`, `undo`, `redo`, `sync_card_list_component`, `switch_view_strategy` | 1546-1640 | new `app/commands.rs` |
| Export / auto-save | `export_board_with_filename`, `export_all_boards_with_filename`, `auto_save`, `check_ended_sprints` | 1642-1715 | new `app/io.rs` |
| External-editor edit_* | `edit_board_field`, `edit_card_field`, `edit_entity_json_impl`, `edit_entity_impl` | 1717-1889 | new `app/edit_field.rs` |
| `run` / `load_initial_state` | top-level run loop, init | 1891-2212 | new `app/run.rs` |
| Import | `import_board_from_file` | 2214-2269 | `app/io.rs` |
| External reload | `auto_reload_from_external_change`, `migrate_sprint_logs` | 2271-2290 | existing `app/persistence.rs` |
| Clipboard copy | `copy_card_output`, `copy_branch_name`, `copy_git_checkout_command` | 2292-2331 | new `app/clipboard_actions.rs` |
| Selection-index getters | `get_current_priority_selection_index`, `get_current_sprint_selection_index`, `get_current_sort_field_selection_index` | 2333-2387 | `app/queries.rs` |
| Terminal setup | free fns `setup_terminal`, `restore_terminal` | 2390-2405 | new `app/terminal.rs` |
| `Default` + `test_default` | `impl Default`, `App::test_default` | 2407-2462 | `app/construct.rs` |

## Proposed final shape of `app/mod.rs` (~150-200 lines)

- All `pub mod`/`pub use` lines plus new sub-module declarations.
- `pub struct App { ... }` (38 lines).
- Field-level type definitions (`ExportStep`, `ExportFormat`, `ExportDialogState`, `MigrationState`, `CardField`, `BoardField`).
- Target: <= 220 lines.

## New sub-modules

All sibling under `app/` as `pub(crate) mod foo;`. None need re-exports for `impl App` methods (Rust merges all `impl App` across the crate).

| File | Purpose | Target lines |
|---|---|---|
| `app/construct.rs` | `new`, `new_with_store`, `default_store_manager`, `impl Default`, `test_default` | ~200 |
| `app/lifecycle.rs` | `quit`, `handle_quit_key`, `spawn_save_worker` | ~130 |
| `app/storage_dialog.rs` | `StorageBackendChoice` + impl, `swap_known_extension`, dialog handlers, `adopt_storage_file` | ~180 |
| `app/key_dispatch.rs` | key routing + help/search modes | ~480 (still > 300; see Risks) |
| `app/queries.rs` | pure read-side helpers | ~200 |
| `app/commands.rs` | command execution / undo / frame prep | ~110 |
| `app/io.rs` | import/export/auto-save | ~170 |
| `app/edit_field.rs` | external-editor field edits | ~180 |
| `app/run.rs` | top-level run loop + load_initial_state | ~320 (likely needs further intra-fn extraction) |
| `app/error_log_view.rs` | error-log dialog wiring | ~50 |
| `app/clipboard_actions.rs` | clipboard helpers | ~50 |
| `app/terminal.rs` | crossterm setup/teardown | ~25 |

Existing sub-modules absorb additional `impl App` blocks: `mode.rs`, `ui_state.rs`, `animation.rs`, `sprint_view.rs`, `persistence.rs`.

## Public API impact

`kanban_tui::app::*` currently exposes types defined in `app/mod.rs`: `App`, `ExportStep`, `ExportFormat`, `ExportDialogState`, `MigrationState`, `CardField`, `BoardField`. `lib.rs` only re-exports `App`.

External call sites (`handlers/*.rs`, tests) consume: `App, AppMode, BoardFocus, CardField, DialogMode, ExportDialogState, Focus, MigrationState, SettingsFocus, CardAnimation`, plus `StorageBackendChoice` (test-only) and `swap_known_extension` (test-only).

Preserving the surface is mechanical: every type that moves should be re-exported from `app/mod.rs` (`pub use storage_dialog::StorageBackendChoice;`, `pub(crate) use storage_dialog::swap_known_extension;` etc.). Methods stay on `App` because each new sub-module declares its own `impl App { ... }`; call sites need no changes.

## Tests

12 tests in inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (2464-2974). Recommended moves (each test stays inline in its new module to retain access to private items):
- `test_save_worker_does_not_send_completion_on_conflict` (2474) -> `app/lifecycle.rs`
- `test_scroll_help_into_view_scrolls_deep_item` (2586) -> `app/key_dispatch.rs`
- 9 storage-dialog tests + `swap_known_extension` test (2604-2944) -> `app/storage_dialog.rs`

## Sequencing (6 commits, each green)

1. `refactor(tui): extract terminal setup and clipboard helpers` - `app/terminal.rs` + `app/clipboard_actions.rs`. Smallest, no risk.
2. `refactor(tui): extract construct, lifecycle, and storage-dialog modules` - `app/construct.rs` (with `test_default`, `impl Default`), `app/lifecycle.rs`, `app/storage_dialog.rs` (with tests). Verifies `pub use` plumbing.
3. `refactor(tui): extract queries, commands, io, and edit-field modules` - read-mostly methods with low coupling.
4. `refactor(tui): fold small clusters into existing sub-modules` - add `impl App` blocks to `mode.rs`, `ui_state.rs`, `animation.rs`, `sprint_view.rs`, `persistence.rs`. Plus create `app/error_log_view.rs`.
5. `refactor(tui): extract key dispatch and run loop` - the big one. Move key/help/search cluster (670-1260) to `key_dispatch.rs` plus `scroll_help_into_view`, then `load_initial_state` + `run` to `run.rs`. Both files likely exceed 300; follow-up pass to break `handle_key_event` arms by mode into `key_dispatch/normal.rs`, `key_dispatch/dialog.rs`, etc.; same for `run`'s `tokio::select!` arms.
6. `refactor(tui): inline-test cleanup and final mod.rs trim` - verify `mod.rs` <= 220 lines; rerun `cargo clippy --all-targets`.

## Risks

- **`handle_key_event` is 339 lines** (798-1136). Even after extraction it breaches the 300-line guideline. Plan to split match arms by mode into `key_dispatch/<mode>.rs` files.
- **`App::run` is 300 lines** (1912-2212) with five `tokio::select!` arms capturing `&mut self` across `.await`s. Extracting each arm into a private async method on `App` is the cleanest reduction, but borrow-checker friction is likely. Mitigation: take the receiver out via `Option::take()` for the duration of the await, then put it back.
- **`#[doc(hidden)] pub async fn load_initial_state`**: confirm none of the `tests/*.rs` files in `kanban-tui/tests/` call it by the public name. Safe to relocate.
- **`fn keycode_matches_binding_key`** is an associated fn. When moved to `key_dispatch.rs`, change call site at 1252 to `Self::` if the impl is still on `App`, or convert to a free fn `pub(super)` and update the caller.
- **Test fixture duplication**: `test_default` (2415-2461) and the inline test save-worker fixture (2525-2562) both spell out the full `App { ... }` literal. After moving `test_default` into `construct.rs`, the save-worker test should call `App::test_default()` and override the fields it needs; saves ~40 lines.
- **No circular deps**: every new sub-module needs `use crate::app::{App, ...};` only. The same pattern is already used by `handlers/*.rs` (12 files).
- **`impl App` reopened many times**: Rust allows this freely across modules within one crate. Just ensure each method is `pub(crate)` (or `pub` if part of the published API: `App::new`, `App::run`, `App::test_default`).
- **`ExportDialogState` has `pub fn new/toggle/select_all/any_selected`** consumed by `handlers/settings_handlers.rs`. Keep these `pub`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `app/mod.rs` is under 230 lines after the split.
- Every new sub-module file is at or under 300 lines, except `key_dispatch.rs` and `run.rs` which may need additional intra-file extraction in a follow-up commit.
- `cargo test --package kanban-tui` passes at every commit boundary.
- Every test that was inline before is now inline in its new home (private internals still accessible).

## Related

Parent audit: KAN-483.
cards.title: Decompose kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs (2975 lines) into focused App sub-modules

cards.description:
Sub-task of KAN-496 (decompose `app/mod.rs`). Two small, zero-risk extractions that validate the `pub(crate) mod` plumbing pattern before touching anything coupled to App state.

## Scope

| Cluster | Current location | New file | Target lines |
|---|---|---|---|
| `setup_terminal`, `restore_terminal` (free fns) | `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:2390-2405` | `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/terminal.rs` | ~25 |
| `copy_card_output`, `copy_branch_name`, `copy_git_checkout_command` | `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:2292-2331` | `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/clipboard_actions.rs` | ~50 |

## Sequencing (2 commits)

1. `refactor(tui): extract terminal setup helpers into app/terminal.rs`. Free fns; no `App` coupling.
2. `refactor(tui): extract clipboard actions into app/clipboard_actions.rs`. `impl App` methods; rely on the inherent-method split-across-files pattern (Rust merges `impl App` blocks crate-wide).

## Public API impact

`setup_terminal`/`restore_terminal` were not previously re-exported; check `kanban-tui/src/lib.rs` and any test files. If they were `pub(crate)`, keep them `pub(crate)` in the new module. Clipboard helpers are `impl App` methods; call sites in `key_dispatch` (still in `app/mod.rs` for now) remain unchanged.

## Tests

No inline tests for either cluster. Nothing to move.

## Risks

None worth listing. This is the lowest-risk extraction in the entire `app/mod.rs` split.

## Acceptance criteria

- `app/terminal.rs` and `app/clipboard_actions.rs` exist.
- `cargo test --package kanban-tui` passes at both commit boundaries.
- `app/mod.rs` shrinks by ~60 lines.

cards.description:
Sub-task of KAN-496 (decompose `app/mod.rs`). Distributes method clusters from the giant `impl App` block in `app/mod.rs` into the existing `app/` sub-modules that already own the underlying state. Net effect: ~60 lines per sub-module gain, ~300 lines drop from `mod.rs`.

## Scope

Each row is a separate commit. All targets are existing sub-modules under `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/`. The technique is the same in every case: open a new `impl App { ... }` block in the destination file and move the method bodies in. Rust merges `impl App` blocks crate-wide; call sites in `app/mod.rs` (which still hosts `handle_key_event` for now) remain unchanged.

| Cluster | Current location | Destination sub-module |
|---|---|---|
| `push_mode`, `pop_mode`, `is_dialog_mode`, `get_base_mode`, `open_dialog` | `app/mod.rs:458-477, 525-527` | existing `app/mode.rs` |
| `set_error`, `set_success`, `clear_banner` | `app/mod.rs:658-668` | existing `app/ui_state.rs` |
| `handle_animation_tick`, `complete_restore_animation` | `app/mod.rs:1262-1361` | existing `app/animation.rs` |
| `populate_sprint_task_lists`, `apply_sort_to_sprint_lists` | `app/mod.rs:1468-1544` | existing `app/sprint_view.rs` |
| `auto_reload_from_external_change`, `migrate_sprint_logs` | `app/mod.rs:2271-2290` | existing `app/persistence.rs` |
| `with_error_log`, `with_error_log_mut`, `open_error_log`, `set_error_log`, `error_log_arc`, `handle_error_log_mode` | `app/mod.rs:479-523` | new `app/error_log_view.rs` |

The first five fold into existing files; the sixth creates a new tiny file (~50 lines) for the error-log dialog wiring because no existing sub-module owns it. Could also fold into `app/ui_state.rs` if the team prefers fewer files.

## Sequencing (6 commits)

1. `refactor(tui): fold mode-stack methods into app/mode.rs`.
2. `refactor(tui): fold banner methods into app/ui_state.rs`.
3. `refactor(tui): fold animation methods into app/animation.rs`.
4. `refactor(tui): fold sprint task list methods into app/sprint_view.rs`.
5. `refactor(tui): fold external-reload methods into app/persistence.rs`.
6. `refactor(tui): extract error_log dialog wiring into app/error_log_view.rs`.

Each commit is a pure relocation. No call site changes.

## Risks

None substantive. The risk model is "did I copy the methods correctly". Verified by `cargo test --package kanban-tui` at every commit boundary.

## Acceptance criteria

- The five fold-ins land; `error_log_view.rs` is created.
- `app/mod.rs` shrinks by ~300 lines.
- Each destination sub-module still under 300 lines after fold-in.
- No public API change.

## Related

cards.description:
Sub-task of KAN-496 (decompose `app/mod.rs`). Pulls the App constructors and the quit/save-worker plumbing out of `mod.rs`.

## Scope

| Cluster | Current location | New file | Target lines |
|---|---|---|---|
| `App::new`, `App::new_with_store`, free fn `default_store_manager`, `impl Default for App`, `App::test_default` | `app/mod.rs:69-74, 221-338, 2407-2462` | `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/construct.rs` | ~200 |
| `App::quit`, `handle_quit_key`, `spawn_save_worker` + the `test_save_worker_does_not_send_completion_on_conflict` test | `app/mod.rs:340-456, 2474-2585` | `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/lifecycle.rs` | ~270 (incl. test) |

## Sequencing (2 commits)

1. `refactor(tui): extract App constructors and test_default into app/construct.rs`. Includes `impl Default for App` and the `default_store_manager` free fn. The inline save-worker test (commit 2 target) should adopt `App::test_default()` to drop its ~40 lines of struct-literal duplication while we are here.
2. `refactor(tui): extract App lifecycle into app/lifecycle.rs`. Move `quit`, `handle_quit_key`, `spawn_save_worker`, plus the `test_save_worker_does_not_send_completion_on_conflict` test (which lives next to `spawn_save_worker` because it exercises private internals).

## Public API impact

- `App::new` and `App::new_with_store` are `pub`; keep them `pub` (used by `kanban-cli`).
- `App::test_default` is `pub` (used by integration tests in `kanban-tui/tests/`); keep `pub`.
- `default_store_manager` is `pub(crate)`; keep `pub(crate)`.
- `quit`, `handle_quit_key`, `spawn_save_worker` are inherent methods on `App` reached by name; the split-`impl`-across-files pattern preserves call sites.

## Tests

`test_save_worker_does_not_send_completion_on_conflict` (currently at `app/mod.rs:2474`) is the only inline test in this scope. Moves to `app/lifecycle.rs` next to `spawn_save_worker`. The `App { ... }` literal inside the test should be replaced with `App::test_default()` plus the field overrides it needs; this is a small cleanup that also exercises the newly-extracted `App::test_default`.

## Risks

- `App::new_with_store` is ~110 lines (lines 231-338); if it lands over 50 lines in `construct.rs`, leave it for a follow-up intra-fn extraction. Do not try to fold that work into this card.
- `spawn_save_worker` spawns a tokio task that captures `&self`-derived clones; the task body stays opaque to the move (single-method relocation).

## Acceptance criteria

- `app/construct.rs` and `app/lifecycle.rs` exist; each under 300 lines.
- `app/mod.rs` shrinks by ~280 lines.
- `cargo test --package kanban-tui` passes at both commit boundaries.
- The save-worker test runs in its new location without modification beyond the `test_default` cleanup.

## Related

Parent umbrella: KAN-496. Audit: KAN-483.

cards.description:
Sub-task of KAN-496 (decompose `app/mod.rs`). The startup "choose storage file" dialog from KAN-330 is the largest cluster of intertwined types, handlers, and tests in `mod.rs`. Pulling it out as one unit is cleaner than separating the types from the handlers from the tests.

## Scope

| Item | Current location |
|---|---|
| `enum StorageBackendChoice` + impl | `app/mod.rs:115-136` |
| Free fn `swap_known_extension` | `app/mod.rs:141-149` |
| `App::maybe_push_startup_file_dialog` | `app/mod.rs:529-...` (within 529-656) |
| `App::handle_choose_storage_file_dialog` | within 529-656 |
| `App::adopt_storage_file` | within 529-656 |
| 9 inline tests covering all of the above | `app/mod.rs:2604-2944` |
| `swap_known_extension` test | `app/mod.rs:2944-...` |

All move into a new `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/storage_dialog.rs` (~180 lines code + tests). The tests stay inline in the new module so they retain access to the (now-private) `adopt_storage_file` and `keycode_matches_binding_key` helpers if any.

## Sequencing (1 commit)

`refactor(tui): extract startup storage-file dialog into app/storage_dialog.rs`.

One commit because the types, handlers, and tests reference each other and cannot land in separate commits without breaking the build.

## Public API impact

- `StorageBackendChoice` is currently `pub` and referenced in tests (test-only consumer). After the move, `app/mod.rs` re-exports it (`pub use storage_dialog::StorageBackendChoice;`) so external test paths keep working.
- `swap_known_extension` is `pub(crate)` and only used by the inline test. After the move it can stay `pub(crate)` and the test imports it locally.
- The three `App` methods (`maybe_push_startup_file_dialog`, `handle_choose_storage_file_dialog`, `adopt_storage_file`) are `pub(crate)` inherent methods; the split-`impl`-across-files pattern preserves the call sites in `key_dispatch` (still in `app/mod.rs` at the time of this card).

## Tests

10 inline tests total (9 dialog + 1 free fn). All move with the production code. Test helpers `test_default()` from `app/construct.rs` (sub-card KAN-499) are imported via `use crate::app::App;`.

## Risks

- `App::adopt_storage_file` swaps the store manager and re-spawns the save worker; the test setup depends on the test harness's `tokio::runtime::Handle::current()`. Moving the test does not change runtime semantics; verify the multi-thread flavor at the test boundary.
- This sub-card depends on **KAN-499** (construct + lifecycle) landing first so that `App::test_default()` exists; otherwise the tests are stuck with full struct-literal init.

## Acceptance criteria

- `app/storage_dialog.rs` exists; under 300 lines (excluding inline tests, which may push it ~50-100 over - acceptable for a self-contained dialog module).
- `app/mod.rs` shrinks by ~440 lines (180 code + 260 tests).
- `cargo test --package kanban-tui` passes.
- All 10 tests run in the new module.

## Related

cards.description:
Sub-task of KAN-496 (decompose `app/mod.rs`). Pulls four medium-coupling extractions out of `mod.rs`. All four are read-mostly or pure-mutation clusters that do not touch the key-dispatch hot path or the run loop.

## Scope

| Cluster | Current location | New file | Target lines |
|---|---|---|---|
| `get_board_card_count`, `get_sorted_board_cards`, `get_selected_card_in_context`, `get_selected_card_id`, `select_card_by_id`, `get_card_by_id`, `get_card_for_detail_view`, `get_current_priority_selection_index`, `get_current_sprint_selection_index`, `get_current_sort_field_selection_index` | `app/mod.rs:1363-1466, 2333-2387` | `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/queries.rs` | ~200 |
| `execute_command`, `execute_commands_batch`, `prepare_frame`, `undo`, `redo`, `sync_card_list_component`, `switch_view_strategy` | `app/mod.rs:1546-1640` | `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/commands.rs` | ~110 |
| `export_board_with_filename`, `export_all_boards_with_filename`, `auto_save`, `check_ended_sprints`, `import_board_from_file` | `app/mod.rs:1642-1715, 2214-2269` | `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/io.rs` | ~170 |
| `edit_board_field`, `edit_card_field`, `edit_entity_json_impl`, `edit_entity_impl` | `app/mod.rs:1717-1889` | `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/edit_field.rs` | ~180 |

## Sequencing (4 commits)

1. `refactor(tui): extract App lookup helpers into app/queries.rs`. Pure read-side; lowest risk in this sub-card.
2. `refactor(tui): extract App command/undo/frame helpers into app/commands.rs`. These wrap calls to `KanbanContext` and `tui_context.execute_command`.
3. `refactor(tui): extract App import/export/auto-save into app/io.rs`. The two export helpers, `auto_save`, `check_ended_sprints`, and `import_board_from_file`.
4. `refactor(tui): extract App external-editor field edits into app/edit_field.rs`. The four `edit_*` methods plus their private `edit_entity_*_impl` helpers.

Each commit lands independently. None block the others.

## Public API impact

All four clusters are `pub(crate)` inherent methods on `App`. The split-`impl`-across-files pattern preserves every call site. The two `export_*_with_filename` methods are `pub` (called by `handlers/`); they stay `pub`.

## Tests

No inline tests for any of these clusters; nothing to move. Integration tests in `kanban-tui/tests/` exercise the export/import paths and continue to work unchanged.

## Risks

- `auto_save` and `check_ended_sprints` both touch `tui_context.save_or_queue(...)`; the closures around `&mut self` are sensitive to borrow ordering. Move whole-method, do not refactor the bodies during the move.
- `edit_entity_json_impl` and `edit_entity_impl` are generic over an entity type and reach into `editor.rs`; the generic bounds must be preserved verbatim in the move.

## Acceptance criteria

- Each new file under 300 lines.
- `app/mod.rs` shrinks by ~600 lines across the four commits.
- `cargo test --package kanban-tui` passes at every commit boundary.

## Related

Parent umbrella: KAN-496. Audit: KAN-483.
cards.title: Extract App read, command, IO, and edit helpers from app/mod.rs (queries, commands, io, edit_field)

cards.description:
Sub-task of KAN-496 (decompose `app/mod.rs`). The key-dispatch cluster is the second-largest piece of `mod.rs` and the single hottest hot path. This card extracts it then sub-splits the giant `handle_key_event` `match` arm-by-arm so no file remains over 300 lines.

## Scope

| Cluster | Current location | Initial new file |
|---|---|---|
| `keycode_matches_binding_key`, `execute_action`, `handle_key_event`, `handle_search_mode`, `handle_archived_cards_view_mode`, `scroll_help_into_view`, `handle_help_mode` + `test_scroll_help_into_view_scrolls_deep_item` | `app/mod.rs:670-1260, 2586-2603` | `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/key_dispatch.rs` (~480 lines + ~20 test) |

The initial extraction lands as a single ~480-line file because the entire cluster moves atomically. The follow-up commit splits `handle_key_event` (currently 339 lines, `mod.rs:798-1136`) by mode into `key_dispatch/<mode>.rs` files; the entry point stays in `key_dispatch/mod.rs`.

## Proposed post-split shape

```
crates/kanban-tui/src/app/key_dispatch/
  mod.rs                       (~140 lines: keycode_matches_binding_key,
                                 execute_action, dispatch entry, helpers)
  normal.rs                    (~70 lines: NormalMode arm)
  dialog.rs                    (~80 lines: dialog mode arms)
  search.rs                    (~50 lines: handle_search_mode + helpers)
  help.rs                      (~60 lines: handle_help_mode, scroll_help_into_view)
  archived.rs                  (~50 lines: handle_archived_cards_view_mode)
```

Exact arm-to-file mapping depends on the structure of `handle_key_event`; the cluster table is provisional and refines during commit 2.

## Sequencing (2-3 commits)

1. `refactor(tui): extract key dispatch into app/key_dispatch.rs as a single file`. Whole-cluster move. File is over the 300-line guideline; commit message acknowledges the follow-up.
2. `refactor(tui): split key_dispatch.rs into per-mode submodules`. Convert to `key_dispatch/mod.rs` directory and distribute the `handle_key_event` match arms into per-mode files. Verify each ends under 200 lines.
3. (Optional) `refactor(tui): fold help-scroll test into key_dispatch/help.rs`. Trivial follow-up if commit 2 left it in `mod.rs`.

## Public API impact

- `handle_key_event` is `pub(crate)` and called from `App::run` (currently `app/mod.rs:1891-2212`, soon to move to `app/run.rs` via the next sub-card). The split-`impl`-across-files pattern preserves the call site.
- `scroll_help_into_view` is private; it must stay accessible to its inline test, so it moves alongside the test into `key_dispatch/help.rs`.
- `keycode_matches_binding_key` is an associated fn (`Self::keycode_matches_binding_key`). One non-`Self` call site at `app/mod.rs:1252` (currently); update or convert to a `pub(super) fn` free helper at split time.

## Risks

- The match arms in `handle_key_event` (339 lines) cover every mode the TUI supports. The split must be exhaustive; the compiler's exhaustiveness check on the `AppMode` enum is the safety net.
- Several arms capture `&mut self` and then call into handlers in `handlers/*.rs`. Borrow ordering is sensitive; moving whole arms preserves the existing ordering. Do not refactor arms during the move.
- After commit 2, each per-mode file ends up with a small focused `impl App` block. Verify the `pub(crate)` visibility on the per-mode methods stays consistent with their callers in `mod.rs` dispatch.
- This sub-card lands **after** KAN-501 (read/command/io/edit) because some of the match arms call into `execute_command` and `undo`/`redo`, which by then live in `app/commands.rs`. If KAN-501 has not landed, leave the arms calling the still-in-`mod.rs` methods; both shapes resolve fine.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every file in `app/key_dispatch/` is under 300 lines after commit 2.
- The inline `test_scroll_help_into_view_scrolls_deep_item` test runs in its new module.
- `cargo test --package kanban-tui` passes at every commit boundary.
- `app/mod.rs` shrinks by ~600 lines across the two commits.

## Related

Parent umbrella: KAN-496. Audit: KAN-483.
cards.title: Extract App key-dispatch into app/key_dispatch/ submodule with per-mode split

cards.description:
Sub-task of KAN-496 (decompose `app/mod.rs`). The final and hardest sub-card: extract the top-level run loop and load-initial-state, sub-split the giant `tokio::select!` block, then perform the closing audit on `app/mod.rs`.

## Scope

| Cluster | Current location | New file |
|---|---|---|
| `load_initial_state`, `run` | `app/mod.rs:1891-2212` | `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/run.rs` (~320 lines initial) |

After the initial extraction the file is over 300 lines because `run` itself is ~300 lines containing five `tokio::select!` arms.

## Proposed post-split shape

```
crates/kanban-tui/src/app/run/
  mod.rs                       (~140 lines: load_initial_state + run dispatcher)
  key_loop.rs                  (~50 lines: key-event arm)
  save_completion.rs           (~50 lines: save-completion arm)
  file_change.rs               (~50 lines: file-change arm + auto-reload integration)
  migration.rs                 (~40 lines: migration-result arm)
  tick.rs                      (~30 lines: animation tick arm)
```

Per-arm files contain `pub(crate) async fn handle_<arm>(&mut self, ...) -> ControlFlow<()>` (or similar control-shape) so `run` itself becomes a thin `tokio::select! { arm_a => ..., arm_b => ..., ... }` block.

## Sequencing (3 commits)

1. `refactor(tui): extract App run loop and load_initial_state into app/run.rs as a single file`. Whole-cluster move. Acknowledges the file is over the 300-line guideline.
2. `refactor(tui): split run.rs tokio::select arms into per-arm submodules`. Convert to `app/run/mod.rs` directory. Each arm becomes a method on `App` (kept on `App` via split `impl` rather than free functions because every arm needs `&mut self`).
3. `refactor(tui): final app/mod.rs trim and clippy audit`. The closing pass. `app/mod.rs` should now hold only struct definitions, module declarations, and `pub use` re-exports. Confirm <= 230 lines. Run `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` and address any newly-surfaced warnings (likely `dead_code` from now-private items, or `unreachable_pub` from items that should be `pub(crate)`).

## Public API impact

- `App::run` is `pub` and is the entry point called from `kanban-cli` (`crates/kanban-cli/src/...`). It stays `pub` and stays callable as `App::run(...)` after the directory split (Rust's inherent-method visibility does not change with file location).
- `load_initial_state` is `pub(crate)` and only called from inside `run`. Keep `pub(crate)`.

## Risks

- **`App::run` is the biggest borrow-checker risk in the entire decomposition.** The five `tokio::select!` arms capture `&mut self` across `.await`s. Splitting each arm into a method means the method holds `&mut self`, which forbids the surrounding `tokio::select!` from holding `&mut self.persistence.file_change_rx` for another arm. Mitigation: take the receiver out via `Option::take()` for the duration of the await, then put it back; or move the rx into a local before the select. This is the technique already used in similar patterns in `kanban-tui`; verify against `app/mod.rs:1912-2212` before committing.
- **Order of arms matters**. The current `tokio::select!` arm ordering controls which event wins on a tie; preserve the order in commit 2.
- **`load_initial_state` is `#[doc(hidden)] pub async fn`**. Confirm no `tests/*.rs` file in `kanban-tui/tests/` calls it by name. If any do, keep `pub`.
- **This card must land last** of the KAN-496 sub-cards. Earlier sub-cards may move methods that `run` calls (e.g., `auto_reload_from_external_change` to `persistence.rs` in KAN-498); the call paths resolve through the split-`impl` pattern, but if any earlier card moved a method whose body changed, this card lands on top.
- Commit 3 (`final mod.rs trim`) may surface previously-hidden `unreachable_pub` lints. Address them by demoting to `pub(crate)`; do not paper over with `#[allow]`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `app/run/` directory exists; every file under 300 lines after commit 2.
- `app/mod.rs` is at or under 230 lines after commit 3.
- `cargo test --package kanban-tui` passes at every commit boundary.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` is clean after commit 3.
- The entire `KAN-496` umbrella can be considered complete: no file under `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/` exceeds the 300-line guideline.

## Related

Parent umbrella: KAN-496. Audit: KAN-483.
cards.title: Extract App run loop into app/run.rs with tokio::select arm split, then final mod.rs trim

cards.description:
## Context

During KAN-504 review, the TUI's `with_flush` override pattern came up. `TuiContext` wraps every successful `GraphOperations` / `KanbanOperations` mutator to call `save_coordinator.queue_flush()` for debounced async save. CLI and MCP don't need this — they save explicitly per command. After PR #287:

- CLI and MCP use `delegate_graph_ops_to_inner!` macro (1 line each).
- TUI hand-writes ~20 LOC of override per trait because it does real work in the wrapper.

## Proposal

Move the post-mutation hook into `KanbanContext` itself as a registerable observer. TUI registers its hook at startup; CLI/MCP register nothing; all three surfaces converge on the macro.

```rust
// crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs
pub struct KanbanContext {
    backend: Arc<dyn KanbanBackend>,
    on_mutation: Vec<Box<dyn Fn() + Send + Sync>>,
    // ... existing fields
}

impl KanbanContext {
    pub fn register_post_mutation_hook(
        &mut self,
        hook: impl Fn() + Send + Sync + 'static,
    ) {
        self.on_mutation.push(Box::new(hook));
    }

    // Inside execute() after a successful commit:
    for hook in &self.on_mutation { hook(); }
}
```

## Use cases beyond TUI debounced save

1. TUI debounced-save coordinator (the originating motivation).
2. Real-time sync layer subscribing to mutations for upstream replication.
3. Audit / event sourcing beyond the existing command log (different event shape — entity-state-change vs command-replay).
4. Metrics emission: mutation counts per session, broken down by command kind.
5. Cache invalidation for derived views (TaskListView, sprint summaries).
6. Test fixtures that want to assert observer firing without coupling to internal state.

## Design questions to resolve before implementing

- **Granularity**: per-`execute()` batch or per-command-within-batch? Recommend per-batch — matches current TUI behavior and avoids fan-out on multi-command undo replays.
- **Payload**: no-payload simplest; payload = command kind + affected entity ids enables richer observers but couples observers to command shape. Recommend: start with no payload; add payload later as `Fn(MutationEvent)` if a second consumer needs it.
- **Sync vs async**: sync fits the existing transactional model. Async would complicate the transaction boundary. Recommend sync.
- **Hook removal**: start with `Vec<Box<...>>` and no removal API. Add `register_named(name, hook)` + `unregister(name)` if it becomes load-bearing (e.g., dynamic plugin loading).
- **Ordering**: registration order. Document explicitly.
- **Error semantics**: hooks return `()` — they cannot fail the transaction. A hook that needs to report failure logs/emits its own way.

## Migration steps once landed

1. Add `register_post_mutation_hook` API to `KanbanContext` with a Vec storage field.
2. Fire hooks inside `execute()` after successful commit (after the existing `audit_log` write).
3. TUI startup site: register `|| save_coordinator.queue_flush()`.
4. Remove `TuiContext::with_flush` helper.
5. Remove all per-method overrides in `TuiContext` that only existed to call `with_flush`.
6. `TuiContext` adopts `delegate_graph_ops_to_inner!` (matching CLI/MCP) and `delegate_kanban_ops_to_inner!` (the equivalent macro for `KanbanOperations`, which needs to be created alongside).

## Acceptance criteria

- New consumer can register a hook and observe firing after each successful `execute()` batch.
- Hook does NOT fire on `execute()` paths that error before commit.
- CLI and MCP behavior unchanged when no hook registered.
- TuiContext loses its per-method override; uses the same macros CLI/MCP do.
- All existing tests pass without modification.
- New tests:
  - Hook fires after successful execute; not after failed execute.
  - Multiple hooks fire in registration order.
  - Hook reads observable state and sees post-commit values (the commit has happened by the time the hook fires).
  - No-hook overhead < 1µs on the mutation path (benchmark or guard test).

## Trade-offs

**Pro:**
- Total alignment across CLI/MCP/TUI surfaces; one mechanism per concern.
- `KanbanContext` gains a genuine extension point useful for future plugins.
- TuiContext shrinks substantially (~60 LOC removed).
- Future plugins compose cleanly without touching the wrappers.

**Con:**
- ~50 LOC of observer infrastructure in `KanbanContext`.
- Slight overhead per mutation (`Vec::is_empty` + iterate-if-nonempty).
- Pattern is YAGNI today — only one current consumer.

## Why deferred

This is a clean improvement but doesn't earn its keep on a single consumer. The current `TuiContext::with_flush` is a deliberate, justified override. Revisit when a second consumer wants post-mutation hooks — at that point the observer pattern is the obviously-right answer rather than a speculation.

## Related

- KAN-504 (the PR where this came up — `delegate_graph_ops_to_inner!` macro was the first half of this story)
- The current `SaveCoordinator` in `crates/kanban-tui/src/state/mod.rs` would become the first registered hook
cards.title: Add post-mutation lifecycle hook observer to KanbanContext

cards.description:
## Context

PR #287 (KAN-504) audit surfaced two distinct error-message inconsistencies for card-identifier-not-found across the CLI. One was scoped and fixed inline (the `identifier resolution failed:` prefix the new `relation` commands had). The other predates this PR and stays out of scope: `kanban card get` swaps the order of "not found" vs the identifier.

## Observed messages

After PR #287 lands:

| Command | Message |
|---|---|
| `kanban card get KAN-99999` | `Card not found: 'KAN-99999'` |
| `kanban card delete KAN-99999` | `Card 'KAN-99999' not found` |
| `kanban card archive KAN-99999` | `Card 'KAN-99999' not found` |
| `kanban card update KAN-99999 ...` | `Card 'KAN-99999' not found` |
| `kanban relation add --parent KAN-99999 ...` | `Card 'KAN-99999' not found` |
| `kanban relation parents KAN-99999` | `Card 'KAN-99999' not found` |

`card get` is the odd one out — `Card not found: 'X'` instead of `Card 'X' not found`.

## Task

Normalise `kanban card get` (and any other card-targeted command found during the sweep) to emit `Card 'X' not found` — matching the established pattern.

## Investigation needed

- Find the error site for `card get` (likely in `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/card.rs` or in the domain's `KanbanOperations::get_card` flow when None is wrapped into an error).
- Identify whether the divergence is at the CLI handler or further down in the domain — if domain, the fix may benefit several callers at once.
- Sweep `kanban card list`, `card move`, `card assign-sprint`, etc. for any other "Card not found: 'X'"-shape messages.

## Acceptance criteria

- All card-targeted commands report identifier-not-found as `Card 'X' not found` (or another single agreed phrasing).
- Existing CLI integration tests that match on the message are updated.
- New tests pin the new convention via a small parameterised loop or explicit per-command assertions.

## Why deferred

The fix is straightforward but cross-cuts multiple handlers and may shake loose pre-existing inconsistencies in `card move`, `assign-sprint`, etc. Scope creep risk is real. Worth its own ticket.

## Related

- KAN-504 (where the inconsistency was first noticed; the `relation` half of the fix landed there)
- The companion card for stripping the same prefix from `KanbanMcpError::Resolution`
cards.title: Normalise 'Card X not found' message across all card-targeted CLI commands

cards.description:
## Context

PR #287 (KAN-504) introduced `KanbanMcpError::Resolution { hint: String }` for MCP-boundary identifier resolution errors. Its `Display` impl is `"identifier resolution failed: {hint}"`, and the same prefix gets stripped from the CLI side in a companion change. For symmetry across surfaces, do the same to the MCP variant.

## Current behaviour

```rust
// crates/kanban-mcp/src/error.rs
#[error("identifier resolution failed: {hint}")]
Resolution { hint: String },
```

When an LLM client calls a tool with an unresolvable card identifier, the rendered `McpError.message` reads:

```
identifier resolution failed: Card 'KAN-99999' not found
```

The error code is already `INVALID_PARAMS` (verified by `test_into_mcp_error_resolution_renders_hint_exactly_once`), which gives the LLM enough categorical signal. The `identifier resolution failed:` prefix is then redundant — the hint already says `Card 'KAN-99999' not found`.

## Proposal

Strip the prefix from `KanbanMcpError::Resolution`'s Display:

```rust
#[error("{hint}")]
Resolution { hint: String },
```

Rendered messages become exactly the hint, matching the CLI side after the companion fix.

## Considerations specific to MCP

LLM clients sometimes parse error messages structurally. The current prefix is a (weak) machine-readable category. Mitigations / non-issues:

- The structured `ErrorCode::INVALID_PARAMS` already encodes the category. LLM tool-use protocols read the code, not the message text.
- The hint itself describes the failure mode ("Card 'X' not found", "Board 'X' not found", etc.) which is more useful than a fixed prefix.
- The existing test (`test_into_mcp_error_resolution_renders_hint_exactly_once`) pins the no-duplicate invariant — it would still pass after stripping the prefix because the hint appears exactly once.

## Acceptance criteria

- `KanbanMcpError::Resolution.to_string()` renders only the hint.
- `McpError.message` produced via `KanbanMcpError → McpError` conversion contains the hint verbatim, no prefix.
- Existing MCP integration tests pass.
- New test asserts the rendered message equals the hint.

## Why deferred

Not strictly necessary — the prefix is informational rather than incorrect. Bundled with the CLI cleanup for symmetric naming across the typed error boundaries. Worth doing soon but didn't warrant blocking this PR.

## Related

- KAN-504 (where `KanbanMcpError` was introduced)
- Companion card for normalising `kanban card get` error message
- The CLI-side fix that already landed in PR #287
cards.title: Strip 'identifier resolution failed:' prefix from KanbanMcpError::Resolution Display

cards.description:
## Context

PR #287 (KAN-504) shipped `kanban relation` for parent/child edges (the `parent_child: DagGraph` sub-graph of `DependencyGraph`). The blocks DAG (`blocks: DagGraph`) is fully wired through `GraphOperations` at the service layer but has zero user-facing surface — `kanban-cli`, `kanban-mcp`, and `kanban-tui` never construct `CardEdgeType::Blocks` calls.

Add a `kanban block` subcommand that mirrors the `kanban relation` interface for the blocks DAG.

## Interface (mirrors `kanban relation`)

```
kanban block add --blocker KAN-5 --blocked KAN-7
kanban block remove --blocker KAN-5 --blocked KAN-7
kanban block blockers KAN-7    # cards that block KAN-7 (incoming Blocks edges)
kanban block blocking KAN-5    # cards that KAN-5 blocks (outgoing Blocks edges)
```

The list verbs (`blockers` / `blocking`) directly map to `Directed::incoming` / `Directed::outgoing` on `DependencyGraph::blocks`. Both should accept `--sort` and `--order` to mirror `kanban relation parents` / `kanban relation children`.

## MCP surface

Four parallel tools, naming-mirroring the existing relation tools:

- `tool_add_card_block` — params: `blocker: String`, `blocked: String`
- `tool_remove_card_block` — same shape
- `tool_list_card_blockers` — params: `card: String`
- `tool_list_card_blocking` — params: `card: String`

All return `Vec<CardSummary>` for listings and the `{blocker, blocked}` envelope for mutations. Error semantics identical to relation (cycle / self-ref / edge-not-found, rendered with input-identifier breadcrumbs).

## TUI surface

A "blocks" relationship popup parallel to the existing parent/child popup. Probably triggered from the card-detail view via a new keybinding (e.g. `B`). Same multi-select interaction as the parent/child popup, but writing to `blocks` not `parent_child`.

## Implementation outline

1. **CLI** (`crates/kanban-cli/src/`):
   - Add `BlockCommand` + `BlockAction` enum in `cli.rs` (mirror `RelationCommand` / `RelationAction`).
   - Add `handlers/block.rs` mirroring `handlers/relation.rs` — same `enrich_add_error` / `enrich_remove_error` shape (cycle + self-ref + edge-not-found, all with input-identifier breadcrumbs).
   - Wire in `app.rs` dispatch.
2. **MCP** (`crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs`):
   - Four new request types (`AddCardBlockRequest`, `RemoveCardBlockRequest`, `ListCardBlockersRequest`, `ListCardBlockingRequest`).
   - Four new `#[tool]` methods using `locked_write` / `locked_read` and `KanbanMcpResult`.
3. **TUI** (`crates/kanban-tui/src/`):
   - Add a blocks-relationship popup state.
   - Add keybinding (probably `B` from card detail view).
   - Reuse the existing relationship-popup component if generalisable; otherwise parallel implementation.
4. **Tests**:
   - CLI integration tests parallel to `relation_tests` (same shape, different verbs).
   - MCP integration tests pinning `INVALID_PARAMS` codes and message breadcrumbs.
   - Domain tests already exist (`add_blocks` / cycle rejection are in `dependency_graph.rs` tests).

## Service / domain layer changes

**None.** Everything needed is already shipped in KAN-504:
- `GraphOperations::{add_card_edge, remove_card_edge, list_card_edges_from, list_card_edges_to}` accepts `CardEdgeType::Blocks`.
- `DependencyGraph::{add_blocks, remove_blocks, blockers, blocks_targets}` are public.
- Cycle / self-ref / edge-not-found errors flow up through the same `KanbanError` path the relation handlers already use.

## Acceptance criteria

- All four CLI subcommands work end-to-end against a real `./kanban.json` (or `~/notes/memes/kanban.json`).
- Cycle, self-reference, and edge-not-found errors echo the input flags the same way `kanban relation` does.
- Error messages have no `validation error:` or `identifier resolution failed:` prefixes (use the `Message` variant of `KanbanCliError`).
- MCP tools return `INVALID_PARAMS` for domain errors.
- Test count grows; existing tests unchanged.

## Companion

KAN-NNN (the `kanban link` card) — same shape for the undirected `RelatesTo` sub-graph.

## Related

- KAN-504 (the PR where the service / domain layer was wired up; this card consumes that surface)
cards.title: Add 'kanban block' subcommand for the Blocks DAG (CLI + MCP + TUI)

cards.description:
## Context

PR #287 (KAN-504) shipped `kanban relation` for parent/child edges. The undirected `relates: UndirectedGraph` sub-graph is fully wired through `GraphOperations` at the service layer but has zero user-facing surface — `kanban-cli`, `kanban-mcp`, and `kanban-tui` never construct `CardEdgeType::RelatesTo` calls.

Add a `kanban link` subcommand that mirrors the `kanban relation` interface shape for the undirected case. The verb "link" was chosen over "relate" for visual non-collision with "relation" and because it's the common term in adjacent PM tools (Jira, Notion).

## Interface (mirrors `kanban relation`, asymmetric because undirected)

```
kanban link add --a KAN-5 --b KAN-7
kanban link remove --a KAN-5 --b KAN-7
kanban link list KAN-5    # cards linked to KAN-5 (single direction — undirected has no in/out split)
```

Note the deliberate divergence from `kanban relation` and `kanban block`:
- **Undirected** means only ONE list verb (`list`), not two (`parents`/`children` or `blockers`/`blocking`). `Undirected::neighbors` returns a single neighbour set; there's no directional asymmetry to expose.
- Flag naming `--a` / `--b` (or `--first` / `--second`) reflects the symmetry. Avoid `--from` / `--to` because those imply direction.

## MCP surface

Three parallel tools (vs four for the directed graphs):

- `tool_add_card_link` — params: `a: String`, `b: String`
- `tool_remove_card_link` — same shape
- `tool_list_card_links` — params: `card: String`
