### `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/column_handlers.rs`

```rust
:74   // Collect and sort column data before mutating  ← describes pattern, not safety
```

This is borrow-checker pattern annotation. If the pattern is non-obvious enough to warrant a comment, extract to a named helper: `let sorted_columns = collect_columns_sorted_by_position(&self.model);` — the function name carries the intent.

---

## Sweep plan

### Step 1 — Inventory

```bash
rg -n "^\s*//[^/]" crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/ \
  | grep -v "^\s*///" \
  | grep -v "TODO\|FIXME\|SAFETY\|NOTE"
```

Categorize each hit:
- **D**elete (narration)
- **E**xtract (the comment hints at a missing helper)
- **R**eword as rustdoc on a type/method (state-machine prose)
- **K**eep (genuine safety/correctness/algorithm)

### Step 2 — Apply

Each category gets a focused commit. The deletes are first (lowest risk). Extracts come second (they reveal logic worth naming). Rewords last (they may grow into proper module docs).

### Step 3 — Validate

```bash
cargo build --workspace
cargo test --package kanban-tui
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
```

Behaviour change should be zero.

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All audit-listed offenders (card_handlers.rs:52,55,144; dialog_handlers.rs:33-34,52-54,445-446; column_handlers.rs:74) addressed
- [ ] Full sweep of `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/**/*.rs` — no narration comments remain
- [ ] State-machine prose, where genuinely valuable, lives in rustdoc on the relevant type/enum
- [ ] No comments referencing PR numbers, card numbers, or removed code
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean

## Scope boundaries

- **In scope**: `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/`
- **Out of scope** (sibling cards): the rest of `crates/kanban-tui/src/`, other crates. Same problem likely exists elsewhere but this card stays focused so the diff is reviewable.

## Why now

The handler tree is already the biggest velocity tax in the TUI. Comment noise compounds the file-size problem (KAN-223). Sweeping once now, then enforcing via review going forward, costs ~2 hours and recovers ~5-10% file size across handlers.
cards.title: refactor(tui): comment hygiene sweep across handlers/

cards.description:
## Where, Why, How

**Where.** Test fixture builders are scattered across crates with overlapping responsibilities and zero shared API:

- `crates/kanban-domain/src/in_memory_store.rs:492` — `make_board`, `make_column`, `make_card` (private to the module)
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/sprint.rs:221` — `make_sprint`
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/helpers.rs:8` — `fully_populated_snapshot` (Snapshot-shaped)
- `crates/kanban-cli/tests/cli_integration.rs:354` — `setup_board` (duplicated again at line 1767 in the **same file**)
- `crates/kanban-service/src/test_helpers/contract/` — `BackendFactory`-based builders

**Why.** Today writing a new test that needs "a board with three columns and a sprint" requires either:

1. Picking the right crate's private fixture (most live in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`, inaccessible)
2. Re-rolling the fixture inline (the most common path — and the source of drift)
3. Threading through `BackendFactory` if the test needs persistence (more setup than the test itself)

The cli_integration.rs **internal duplication** at lines 354 and 1767 is the smoking gun: a 5-page file has two definitions of `setup_board` because copy-paste is faster than finding the existing one.

Symptoms:

- Tests across crates use slightly different defaults (priority, status, position), making cross-crate behavioural comparisons unreliable
- Adding a new required field to `Card` cascades into N test files that each have their own constructor pattern
- New contributors hunt across crates for the right "make a test card" helper

**How.** Extract a single `kanban-test-fixtures` crate (`#[cfg(test)]`-friendly), publish builders for the core domain types with sensible defaults, and progressively migrate existing fixtures to call into it.

---

## Proposed solution

### Step 1 — Create `kanban-test-fixtures` crate

```toml
# crates/kanban-test-fixtures/Cargo.toml
[package]
name = "kanban-test-fixtures"
version.workspace = true
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
kanban-domain = { path = "../kanban-domain" }
uuid = { workspace = true }
chrono = { workspace = true }
```

The crate is workspace-internal — not published. It's a development dependency for every other crate's `dev-dependencies` block.

### Step 2 — Builder pattern over `make_*`

The existing `make_card(column_id, "title")` helpers are positional and brittle. Use builders instead:

```rust
// crates/kanban-test-fixtures/src/lib.rs

pub fn card() -> CardBuilder { CardBuilder::default() }

#[derive(Default)]
pub struct CardBuilder {
    title: Option<String>,
    column_id: Option<ColumnId>,
    priority: Option<CardPriority>,
    // ...
}

impl CardBuilder {
    pub fn title(mut self, t: impl Into<String>) -> Self { self.title = Some(t.into()); self }
    pub fn column(mut self, id: ColumnId) -> Self { self.column_id = Some(id); self }
    pub fn priority(mut self, p: CardPriority) -> Self { self.priority = Some(p); self }
    pub fn build(self) -> Card {
        Card::new(
            /* defaults filled in */
            self.column_id.unwrap_or_else(ColumnId::new),
            self.title.unwrap_or_else(|| "Test Card".into()),
            // ...
        )
    }
}
```

Mirror for `board()`, `column()`, `sprint()`, `tag()`. Add `snapshot()` for the persistence-layer Snapshot type and `populated_context()` for a service-layer KanbanContext pre-seeded.

### Step 3 — Migrate existing fixtures

In priority order (highest duplication first):

1. **`crates/kanban-cli/tests/cli_integration.rs`** — both `setup_board` definitions become `fixtures::board().build()`. Removes the internal duplication immediately.
2. **`crates/kanban-domain/src/*`** — replace private `make_*` helpers with `kanban_test_fixtures::*` calls. Drop the inline definitions.
3. **`crates/kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/helpers.rs`** — `fully_populated_snapshot()` calls into `fixtures::snapshot().fully_populated().build()`.
4. **`crates/kanban-service/src/test_helpers/contract/`** — the `BackendFactory` traits stay (they parameterise tests over JSON vs SQLite), but the data they generate routes through `kanban-test-fixtures`.

Each migration is its own commit.

### Step 4 — Documentation

Add a short README in the new crate explaining the builder pattern and default values. Reference it from CONTRIBUTING.md under "Testing → Writing Tests".

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `crates/kanban-test-fixtures/` crate exists, builds, has unit tests for each builder
- [ ] `cli_integration.rs` no longer defines `setup_board` twice (or at all — calls into fixtures)
- [ ] At least the top-3 most-duplicated fixtures from the audit are migrated
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md "Writing Tests" section mentions the new crate
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace --tests` clean
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes

## Out of scope

- Full migration of every fixture-using test across the workspace (likely 50+ tests). Land the crate + top-3 migrations first; subsequent migrations are sibling cards or opportunistic.
- Property-based test helpers (proptest/quickcheck integration) — that's its own card.

## Why now

Cli_integration.rs literally has two copies of `setup_board`. That alone is a red flag worth ~3 hours to fix. Doing it via a shared crate avoids the failure mode where every crate gets its own better-but-still-isolated fixture module and we end up with the same problem one layer deeper.
cards.title: refactor(tests): extract kanban-test-fixtures crate, unify scattered fixtures

cards.description:
## Where, Why, How

**Where.** `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs` — **2700 lines**, the second-largest single source file in the workspace. Contains the entire SQLite backend in one file: connection pooling, schema management, all 13 tables' CRUD, migration logic, command-log persistence, conflict detection integration.

**Why.** Per CONTRIBUTING.md, files should be < 300 lines. This file is 9× over. The cost:

- Any schema change forces every reader to find the right `row_to_*` and `upsert_*` pair among 13 sets of them
- Migration logic is interleaved with steady-state CRUD — hard to tell at a glance whether a chunk is "load path" or "format-upgrade path"
- CONTRIBUTING.md "Adding a Field to a Domain Model" section calls out `sqlite_store.rs` as the file most likely to break compilation — and it's the hardest one to navigate to find the right spot

**How.** Split by table-group. Each domain entity (board, column, card, sprint, archived_card, edges, metadata, command_log) becomes its own file under `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/tables/`. The top-level `sqlite_store.rs` shrinks to connection management + dispatch.

---

## Proposed solution

### Step 1 — Catalogue the file

Approximate breakdown from a scan of `sqlite_store.rs`:

| Concern | Approx. lines | Goes to |
|---|---|---|
| Connection pool / open / WAL setup | ~150 | `sqlite_store.rs` (top-level, shrunk) |
| Schema bootstrap, migration step on open | ~200 | `tables/schema.rs` |
| Metadata table | ~100 | `tables/metadata.rs` |
| Board CRUD (+ sprint_names, sprint_counters helpers) | ~250 | `tables/board.rs` |
| Column CRUD | ~150 | `tables/column.rs` |
| Card CRUD | ~300 | `tables/card.rs` |
| Sprint CRUD + sprint_logs | ~250 | `tables/sprint.rs` |
| Archived card CRUD | ~150 | `tables/archived_card.rs` |
| Edge tables (spawns/blocks/relates) | ~400 | `tables/edges.rs` |
| Command log CRUD (append/truncate/list) | ~250 | `tables/command_log.rs` |
| `PersistenceStore` trait impl (delegates to above) | ~200 | `sqlite_store.rs` |
| Tests | ~300 | per-table test files |

After split: top-level file ~400 lines (still over target, but acceptable for orchestration); each table file 100-400 lines (most under target).

### Step 2 — File layout

```
crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/
├── lib.rs            # re-exports
├── sqlite_store.rs   # SqliteStore struct + PersistenceStore impl (delegates)
├── factory.rs        # (existing) SqliteStoreFactory
├── schema.sql        # (existing) DDL
└── tables/
    ├── mod.rs
    ├── schema.rs        # CREATE TABLE driver + legacy-drop bootstrap
    ├── metadata.rs
    ├── board.rs
    ├── column.rs
    ├── card.rs
    ├── sprint.rs
    ├── archived_card.rs
    ├── edges.rs
    └── command_log.rs
```

### Step 3 — Shared helpers

Several patterns repeat across tables:

- `i32::try_from(usize)` for position fields (KAN-251 already flags this; that card's fix lands cleanly into one shared helper)
- Edge-weight `f64`-to-`f32` cast (KAN-255)
- `chunk_for_variable_limit` for batch ops (KAN-253)

Each gets a single home in `tables/mod.rs` or a `helpers.rs`. The split surfaces these as the obvious extraction points they should have been.

### Step 4 — Tests

Inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` blocks move with their code. The crate's `tests/roundtrip.rs` integration tests don't change.

### Step 5 — Verify

```bash
cargo test --package kanban-persistence-sqlite
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
```

Behaviour change: none. This is purely a file reorganization.

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `sqlite_store.rs` < 500 lines (struct + delegates only)
- [ ] No `tables/*.rs` file > 450 lines
- [ ] `cargo test --package kanban-persistence-sqlite` passes (full roundtrip + concurrency tests)
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md "Adding a Field to a Domain Model" section updated to reference the per-entity table files instead of the monolith
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean

## Out of scope

- Schema redesign — no DDL changes in this card
- Migration logic refactor — only relocation
- Sibling cards (KAN-251, KAN-253, KAN-255) — those land naturally once shared helpers exist; mention but don't bundle

## Why this is lower priority than handler splits

Persistence files don't see the daily merge-conflict traffic that TUI handlers do. The split is still valuable — the file violates the rule and the documented "how to add a field" workflow points readers into a 2700-line file — but the velocity tax is lower per change. Schedule when there's a quiet window for the persistence layer.
cards.title: refactor(persistence-sqlite): decompose sqlite_store.rs into per-table modules

cards.description:
## Where, Why, How

**Where.** `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs` — **1904 lines**, contains every MCP tool definition, all tool handlers, the JSON-schema annotations, the McpContext wrapper, and the error-enrichment helpers.

**Why.** Per CONTRIBUTING.md, files should be < 300 lines. This is 6× over. Each new MCP tool today lands in the same file, growing it unboundedly. The structure-of-record problem:

- A reader looking for one tool's handler must scroll past ~30 unrelated tools
- Adding a tool requires touching ~3 spots in the same file (struct definition, handler impl, `tools()` list)
- The `parse_datetime` / priority / status parsers duplicated from CLI live in this file too (sibling card consolidates those)
- Error-enrichment helpers (`mcp_enrich_add_error`, near line 84) are co-located with unrelated tool definitions

**How.** Split tools by entity into focused modules. The top-level `lib.rs` becomes the registration index — declares the modules, builds the tool list — and nothing else.

---

## Proposed solution

### Step 1 — Catalogue the tools

Approximate distribution from a scan of `lib.rs`:

| Tool group | Approx. tools | Lines |
|---|---|---|
| Board tools | 4 (create, get, list, update) | ~200 |
| Column tools | 4-5 | ~200 |
| Card tools | 6-7 (CRUD, move, archive, restore, update fields) | ~450 |
| Card relation tools | 5 (add/remove parent/child, list children, list parents) | ~250 |
| Sprint tools | 5-6 | ~250 |
| Tag tools | 3-4 | ~150 |
| McpContext wrapper + boilerplate | ~150 |
| Error enrichment + parsers | ~150 |
| Tests | ~300 |

### Step 2 — File layout

```
crates/kanban-mcp/src/
├── lib.rs              # tool registration + server entry point only
├── context.rs          # McpContext wrapper
├── errors.rs           # mcp_enrich_*, McpError
├── parsers.rs          # OR: drop entirely, call kanban-core parsers (sibling card)
└── tools/
    ├── mod.rs
    ├── board.rs
    ├── column.rs
    ├── card.rs
    ├── card_relations.rs
    ├── sprint.rs
    └── tag.rs
```

Each `tools/*.rs` file declares its tool structs (with `#[schemars]` annotations) and implements their handlers via methods on `McpContext`.

### Step 3 — Tool registration pattern

The current `lib.rs` likely has one giant `tools()` function listing every tool. After the split:

```rust
// lib.rs
pub fn tools() -> Vec<Box<dyn McpTool>> {
    let mut v = Vec::new();
    v.extend(tools::board::all());
    v.extend(tools::column::all());
    v.extend(tools::card::all());
    v.extend(tools::card_relations::all());
    v.extend(tools::sprint::all());
    v.extend(tools::tag::all());
    v
}
```

Each `tools/*.rs` exports an `all()` returning its registered tools.

### Step 4 — Coordination with parser-consolidation card

Sibling card *"Consolidate ad-hoc parsers (datetime/priority/status) into kanban-core"* removes ~150 lines from this file by deleting the duplicated parsers. **Land that card first** — it makes this split easier by removing one less thing to move.

### Step 5 — Tests

`crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs:1757-1769` has parser tests that move with the parsers (and may be deleted entirely once they live in kanban-core's test module). The crate's `tests/integration.rs` integration tests don't change.

### Step 6 — Verify

```bash
cargo test --package kanban-mcp
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
```

Behaviour change: none.

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs` < 400 lines (registration + server entry only)
- [ ] Each `tools/*.rs` file < 500 lines
- [ ] `crates/kanban-mcp/tests/integration.rs` still passes unchanged
- [ ] Tool descriptions visible to MCP clients are byte-for-byte identical (smoke test by serialising the tool list and diffing before/after)
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean

## Dependencies / ordering

- Land parser consolidation (sibling card) first — strictly easier merge
- Land before any new MCP tools — adding tools after the split is dramatically easier; adding more before makes the eventual split bigger

## Why this is lower priority than TUI handler splits

MCP is a thin layer over the service. It sees less daily traffic than the TUI handlers, and merge conflicts are rarer. Still violates the rule, still slows down anyone touching MCP, still worth doing — but TUI splits ship first.
cards.title: refactor(mcp): decompose lib.rs into per-entity tool modules
refactor(domain): introduce invert_optional helper for command capture_inverse
refactor(domain): hoist dependency-error enrichment from CLI/MCP into kanban-domain

cards.description:
## Where, Why, How

**Where.** Both CLI and MCP have a "derive a card's board ID by following card → column → board" helper. They live in different files with different error messages, but the algorithm is the same:

- `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/card.rs:243-262` — `card_board_id`
- `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs:299-309` — `card_board`

Both try the live `cards` collection first, fall back to `archived_cards`, then look up the card's `column_id` in the columns collection and return that column's `board_id`.

**Why.** "Given a card, what board is it on?" is a domain-level question, not a per-layer one. The service is the canonical place for it — both consumers should call it.

The current copies are 80% identical; the deltas are cosmetic (`?` vs `match`, slightly different error messages). The cross-layer audit flagged this as one of three derived helpers that are duplicated rather than hoisted.

**How.** Add a `card_board_id(&self, card_id: CardId) -> KanbanResult<BoardId>` method to `KanbanOperations` (as a default method implementation, since it composes existing trait methods). Migrate both layers to call it. Delete the duplicates.

---

## Offenders

### `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/card.rs:243-262`

```rust
fn card_board_id(ctx: &CliContext, card_id: Uuid) -> KanbanCliResult<Uuid> {
    let card = ctx
        .get_card(card_id)
        .or_else(|| ctx.get_archived_card(card_id).map(|ac| ac.card.clone()))
        .ok_or_else(|| /* CLI error */)?;
    let column = ctx
        .columns()
        .iter()
        .find(|c| c.id == card.column_id)
        .ok_or_else(|| /* CLI error */)?;
    Ok(column.board_id)
}
```

### `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs:299-309`

```rust
fn card_board(ctx: &McpContext, id: Uuid) -> Result<Uuid, McpError> {
    let card = ctx
        .get_card(id)
        .or_else(|| ctx.get_archived_card(id).map(|ac| ac.card.clone()))
        .ok_or_else(|| /* MCP error */)?;
    let column = ctx
        .columns()
        .iter()
        .find(|c| c.id == card.column_id)
        .ok_or_else(|| /* MCP error */)?;
    Ok(column.board_id)
}
```

The deltas: function name (`card_board_id` vs `card_board`), result type, error construction. Logic is line-for-line identical.

---

## Proposed solution

### Step 1 — Add to `KanbanOperations` trait

```rust
// crates/kanban-domain/src/operations.rs

pub trait KanbanOperations {
    // ... existing methods ...

    /// Returns the board id a card belongs to, consulting live cards
    /// then archived cards. Errors with `not_found` when the card
    /// doesn't exist in either collection, or `Internal` when the
    /// card references a non-existent column (data corruption).
    fn card_board_id(&self, card_id: CardId) -> KanbanResult<BoardId> {
        let card = self
            .get_card(card_id)
            .map(|c| c.clone())
            .or_else(|| self.get_archived_card(card_id).map(|ac| ac.card.clone()))
            .ok_or_else(|| KanbanError::not_found("card", card_id))?;
        let column = self
            .columns()
            .iter()
            .find(|c| c.id == card.column_id)
            .ok_or_else(|| KanbanError::Internal(format!(
                "card {} references nonexistent column {}",
                card_id, card.column_id,
            )))?;
        Ok(column.board_id)
    }
}
```

Default implementation composes the existing trait methods, so every implementor (KanbanContext, TuiContext, CliContext, McpContext, the wrappers) gets it for free.

### Step 2 — Migrate CLI

```rust
// crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/card.rs — delete the helper, replace call sites
let board_id = ctx.card_board_id(card_id)?;
```

The error is now a `KanbanError`; the existing `KanbanCliError::Domain(#[from] KanbanError)` variant absorbs it via `?` with no manual mapping.

### Step 3 — Migrate MCP

```rust
// crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs — delete the helper, replace call sites
let board_id = ctx.card_board_id(card_id).map_err(kanban_err_to_mcp)?;
```

The MCP boundary already has a `kanban_err_to_mcp` adapter; one call per site, no duplication.

### Step 4 — Tests

Add one unit test on `KanbanContext` (or `InMemoryStore` if `KanbanOperations` defaults are testable there) covering:
- Live card → expected board
- Archived card → expected board
- Unknown card id → `not_found`
- Card with dangling `column_id` → `Internal` (referential-integrity violation; KAN-257 covers detecting this proactively)

Existing CLI/MCP integration tests pass unchanged — message texts are owned by the new shared error, not the duplicates.

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `KanbanOperations::card_board_id` exists as a default trait method
- [ ] No `card_board_id` / `card_board` helper functions remain in `kanban-cli` or `kanban-mcp`
- [ ] All previously-existing call sites compile and pass tests
- [ ] One new domain-level test covers the four cases (live / archived / unknown / dangling)
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean

## Estimated impact

- ~30 lines deleted across CLI and MCP
- ~25 lines added (trait method + test)
- Net: small
- Removes a coordination point: any change to "how do I find a card's board" now lives in one place

## Why now

When KAN-575 (newtype IDs) lands, this helper's signature changes from `Uuid -> Uuid` to `CardId -> BoardId`. Hoisting first means one signature change, not two. Do this before — or together with — KAN-575.
cards.title: refactor(domain): add card_board_id to KanbanOperations, drop duplicates in CLI/MCP
refactor(cli): introduce resolve_or_error! macro for identifier-resolution boilerplate

cards.description:
## Where, Why, How

**Where.** Every command enum has three identical `match self` dispatchers — one for `execute`, one for `description`, one for `capture_inverse`. The top-level `Command` enum (`crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/mod.rs:33-70`) and each per-entity enum (`BoardCommand`, `CardCommand`, `ColumnCommand`, `SprintCommand`, `DependencyCommand`, `CascadeCommand`) all have them.

- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/mod.rs:33-42` — `Command::execute`
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/mod.rs:44-52` — `Command::description`
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/mod.rs:62-70` — `Command::capture_inverse`
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/board_commands.rs` — same shape for `BoardCommand`
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/card_commands.rs` — same shape for `CardCommand`
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/column_commands.rs` — same shape for `ColumnCommand`
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/sprint_commands.rs` — same shape for `SprintCommand`
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/dependency_commands.rs` — same shape for `DependencyCommand`
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/cascade_commands.rs` — same shape for `CascadeCommand`

**Why.** Six enums × three methods = 18 match-blocks of pure dispatch. Each variant added to a command enum requires editing 3-4 dispatchers manually. The dispatchers contain no logic — every arm is `Command::X(cmd) => cmd.method(args)`. This is exactly the problem `enum_dispatch` solves.

Today, when a new command variant is added, the contributor must remember to update all three dispatchers in the enum's module plus all three in `commands/mod.rs`. Missing one fails to compile (good), but the resulting error message points at the dispatcher, not at the missing variant. A derive macro makes the link explicit and removes 60+ lines of boilerplate.

**How.** Adopt the `enum_dispatch` crate (mature, ~12M downloads, BSD-2-Clause). Apply `#[enum_dispatch]` to a `CommandExecute` trait covering the three methods; apply `#[enum_dispatch(CommandExecute)]` to each command enum. The macro generates every dispatcher.

---

## Offenders

### `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/mod.rs:33-70`

```rust
impl Command {
    pub fn execute(&self, context: &CommandContext) -> KanbanResult<()> {
        match self {
            Command::Board(cmd) => cmd.execute(context),
            Command::Column(cmd) => cmd.execute(context),
            Command::Card(cmd) => cmd.execute(context),
            Command::Sprint(cmd) => cmd.execute(context),
            Command::Dependency(cmd) => cmd.execute(context),
            Command::Cascade(cmd) => cmd.execute(context),
        }
    }
    pub fn description(&self) -> String {
        match self {
            Command::Board(cmd) => cmd.description(),
            // ... 5 more arms ...
        }
    }
    pub fn capture_inverse(&self, context: &CommandContext) -> InverseCommand {
        match self {
            Command::Board(cmd) => cmd.capture_inverse(context),
            // ... 5 more arms ...
        }
    }
}
```

Multiply by 6 enums = 18 match blocks of mechanical dispatch.

### Per-entity enums (sampled `BoardCommand` shape)

```rust
impl BoardCommand {
    pub fn execute(&self, context: &CommandContext) -> KanbanResult<()> {
        match self {
            BoardCommand::Create(cmd) => cmd.execute(context),
            BoardCommand::Update(cmd) => cmd.execute(context),
            BoardCommand::Delete(cmd) => cmd.execute(context),
            BoardCommand::ApplySettings(cmd) => cmd.execute(context),
            BoardCommand::SetTaskSort(cmd) => cmd.execute(context),
            // ...
        }
    }
    // ... same for description, capture_inverse ...
}
```

---

## Proposed solution

### Step 1 — Add `enum_dispatch` to workspace

```toml
# Cargo.toml (workspace root)
[workspace.dependencies]
enum_dispatch = "0.3"
```

```toml
# crates/kanban-domain/Cargo.toml
[dependencies]
enum_dispatch.workspace = true
```

### Step 2 — Define the dispatch trait

```rust
// crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/mod.rs

#[enum_dispatch::enum_dispatch]
pub trait CommandExecute {
    fn execute(&self, context: &CommandContext) -> KanbanResult<()>;
    fn description(&self) -> String;
    fn capture_inverse(&self, context: &CommandContext) -> InverseCommand;
}
```

### Step 3 — Tag the enums

```rust
#[enum_dispatch::enum_dispatch(CommandExecute)]
pub enum Command {
    Board(BoardCommand),
    Column(ColumnCommand),
    Card(CardCommand),
    Sprint(SprintCommand),
    Dependency(DependencyCommand),
    Cascade(CascadeCommand),
}

#[enum_dispatch::enum_dispatch(CommandExecute)]
pub enum BoardCommand {
    Create(CreateBoard),
    Update(UpdateBoard),
    // ...
}
```

Repeat for all six command enums.

### Step 4 — Implement the trait on leaf command structs

Each leaf command (`CreateBoard`, `UpdateCard`, etc.) already has `execute` / `description` / `capture_inverse` methods. They become a `impl CommandExecute for CreateBoard { ... }` block instead of free-standing impls. Mechanical.

### Step 5 — Delete the hand-rolled dispatchers

Every `match self { ... }` block in the six command enums goes away. The macro generates them.

### Step 6 — Verify

```bash
cargo build --workspace
cargo test --workspace
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
```

Behaviour change: zero. Every dispatcher generated by the macro is byte-equivalent to the hand-rolled one.

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `enum_dispatch` is a workspace dependency
- [ ] `CommandExecute` trait defined with the three methods
- [ ] All six command enums tagged `#[enum_dispatch(CommandExecute)]`
- [ ] All leaf command structs implement `CommandExecute`
- [ ] No hand-rolled `match self { X(cmd) => cmd.method(...) }` dispatchers remain
- [ ] All existing tests pass unchanged
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean
- [ ] A new test verifies a representative command (e.g., `CreateCard`) round-trips through `Command::execute` via the macro

## Estimated impact

- ~60 lines deleted (18 match blocks × ~3.3 lines each on average)
- ~15 lines added (trait definition + macro tags)
- Net: ~-45 lines
- New variants now require implementing the trait once on the leaf type; dispatcher is generated

## Risks

- **New external dependency.** `enum_dispatch` is well-maintained and small, but it's still one more crate. Alternative: write a local `dispatch_command!` declarative macro in ~30 lines. Trade-off: in-house macro = no external dep but more LOC and less expressive; crate = more polish but adds a dep.
- **Macro debugging.** When something goes wrong, the error message points to the macro expansion, not the source. Mitigation: use `cargo expand` during initial integration; the macro is mature enough that error messages are usually clear.

## Out of scope

- Refactoring the leaf command implementations themselves (size, naming).
- The handler-side execution flow — only the dispatcher layer changes.

## Why now

When KAN-578 (split `detail_view_handlers.rs`) and KAN-577 (decompose `handle_sprint_detail_key`) land, more commands will move around. Removing the hand-rolled dispatchers before that work simplifies the cascading changes. After: adding a new command variant is one impl block, no dispatcher edits.
cards.title: refactor(domain): adopt enum_dispatch for Command/*Command dispatchers
refactor(tui): add App::execute_board_command helper, flatten card_handlers pyramid

cards.description:
# Pre-work for the generalization sprint

This section unblocks all the other top-level sections by laying down the kanban-core scaffolding they need: empty submodule files, lib.rs re-exports, the dependency additions, and the small `CoreError` extensions that the abstract trait surface depends on.

Doing this as a small upfront PR lets the other sections (Backend, Command, UndoStack, Context, etc.) land in parallel without merge-conflicting on `kanban-core/src/lib.rs` or duplicating dep additions.

## Why pre-work matters

`kanban-core/src/` is a flat list of 10 source items today. The sprint will add 6-7 new top-level items (`backend`, `command`, `context`, `undo`, plus optional `path`, `fs`, `config_io`). If each agent's PR also touches `lib.rs` to declare their module, they all collide. Carving out the structure first lets each downstream PR be additive and rebase-friendly.

Same logic applies to dependency additions: `tracing` (for `Context::replace_backend` log), and any of `tempfile`/`dunce`/`dirs` if the sweep cards land. Add them all upfront, justify each, and let the consumers be one-liners.

## Suggested approach

1. Reorganize `kanban-core/src/` with empty placeholder `.rs` files for every new module the sprint will add, plus the `pub mod` declarations in `lib.rs`.
2. Add the new third-party deps (`tracing` at minimum; `tempfile`/`dunce`/`dirs` if sweep cards are in scope).
3. Add the new `CoreError` variant(s) plus the `From<KanbanError> for CoreError` impl in `kanban-domain` that lets `KanbanResult` flow into the generic dispatcher's `CoreResult` closure body.

## Alternatives considered

- **Skip scaffolding; let each agent's PR add what it needs.** Rejected: produces a lib.rs merge nightmare and risks two PRs adding the same dep with different versions. The pre-work card is one cheap PR that removes ~6 sources of conflict.
- **Subdirectory layout (backend/, context/, etc.) instead of flat files.** Rejected: forces moving existing files (graph/, input.rs, etc.) and rewriting imports across the workspace. Defer to a follow-up cleanup if desired.

## Children

- Reorganize kanban-core module layout (empty placeholder files)
- Add tracing + tempfile + dunce + dirs deps to kanban-core
- Add CoreError::Internal variant and From<KanbanError> for CoreError impl

## Definition of Done

- cargo build --workspace green with the new empty modules in place.
- All planned downstream sections can target the placeholder files without further lib.rs edits.
- New CoreError variant is callable from kanban-domain via From<KanbanError>.

## Dependencies

This section is a prerequisite for sections "Move Backend trait", "Move Command trait", "Move UndoStack", "Move Context". Should be picked up and merged first.
cards.title: Scaffolding: prepare kanban-core for new submodules
Move Backend trait into kanban-core
refactor(tui): introduce keybindings! declarative macro for KeybindingProvider impls
Move Command trait + BackendExecutor into kanban-core
Move UndoStack into kanban-core
Move Context<B, Cmd> into kanban-core

cards.description:
# Section: Wire kanban-service through new core abstractions

After Backend, Command, UndoStack, and Context are in kanban-core, this section refactors kanban-service to consume them. The end state: `KanbanContext` becomes a thin wrapper around `Context<dyn KanbanBackend, Command>`, holding only the kanban-specific fields (`app_config`, `conflict_pending`) and exposing 15 one-line pass-through methods plus all the kanban business logic (`KanbanOperations`, `GraphOperations`, `migrate_sprint_logs`, batch helpers).

The public API of `KanbanContext` is preserved — no changes needed in kanban-cli, kanban-tui, kanban-mcp, or kanban-mcp.

## Suggested approach

1. **Slim `KanbanBackend`**: extend `Backend<Snapshot = Snapshot> + DataStore + CommandStore` instead of redeclaring lifecycle methods. Keep `as_data_store()` and `persistence_metadata()` on the kanban trait (both reference kanban-specific types, which is fine — kanban-service can import kanban-persistence).

2. **Split each concrete backend's impl block** into two: one `impl Backend for X` (lifecycle), one `impl KanbanBackend for X` (kanban-specific upcast + metadata). Three backends: `InMemoryStore` (in kanban-domain), `SqliteBackend`, `JsonDataStore`.

3. **Add `BackendExecutor<Command>` impl** in a new `crates/kanban-service/src/command_executor.rs`. Routes `ctx()` to `CommandContext { store: as_data_store() }`, routes `store()` to `as_data_store()`, routes `append_to_audit()` to `CommandStore::append_commands` with error mapping.

4. **Wrap KanbanContext**: change the struct to `{ inner: Context<dyn KanbanBackend, Command>, app_config, conflict_pending }`. Convert every lifecycle-pipeline method to a one-line pass-through. Bodies of `execute`, `undo`, `redo`, `save`, `reload`, `replace_backend`, `is_dirty`, `mark_dirty`, `mark_clean`, `clear_history`, `undo_depth`, `redo_depth`, `can_undo`, `can_redo` shrink to a single `self.inner.<method>()` call.

5. **Verify kanban-cli / kanban-tui / kanban-mcp compile unchanged**. They depend on `KanbanContext`'s public surface, which is preserved through the wrapper.

6. **Convert `import_data` to use `apply_batch`** (or stay on a hand-coded transaction body if it's the cleanest expression of "bulk import without audit-log writes"). This is the one remaining call site that doesn't go through `execute`/`undo`/`redo`.

## Alternatives at wrapper-API level

- **Pass-through wrapper (CHOSEN)**: every error mapping (CoreError → KanbanError) happens at one boundary per method, easy to add logging/metrics in one method without touching the rest.
- **Inline `inner` field as `pub`**: leaks the generic type parameter into the public API and gives consumers a way to bypass the wrapper. **Rejected.**
- **Deref<Target = Context<...>>**: see "Move Context" section's alternatives. **Rejected.**

## Children (atomic units)

- Switch KanbanBackend to extend Backend<Snapshot = Snapshot>
- Split SqliteBackend impl into Backend + KanbanBackend
- Split JsonBackend impl into Backend + KanbanBackend
- Implement BackendExecutor<Command> for dyn KanbanBackend
- Wrap KanbanContext around inner Context<dyn KanbanBackend, Command>
- Convert KanbanContext::import_data to apply_batch
- Verify kanban-cli / kanban-tui / kanban-mcp need zero source changes

## Dependencies

Depends on: Move Backend, Move Command + BackendExecutor, Move UndoStack, Move Context.
Blocks: nothing.

## Definition of Done

- `KanbanBackend: Backend<Snapshot = kanban_domain::Snapshot> + DataStore + CommandStore + Send + Sync`.
- Three `impl Backend for X` blocks exist (InMemoryStore in domain, Sqlite/Json in service).
- `command_executor.rs` provides `impl BackendExecutor<Command> for dyn KanbanBackend`.
- `KanbanContext` has three fields (`inner`, `app_config`, `conflict_pending`); none of `backend`, `undo_stack`, `dirty`.
- The 15 pipeline pass-through methods are each one line.
- `cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace` green.
- `git status` after `cargo build` shows no source changes in kanban-cli, kanban-tui, kanban-mcp (proves public API unchanged).
cards.title: Compose kanban-service through new core abstractions
Move audit log (CommandRecord + CommandLog) into kanban-core

cards.description:
# Section: Decouple kanban-persistence from kanban-domain

`crates/kanban-persistence/Cargo.toml:18` declares a hard dep on `kanban-domain`. Three leaks justify it today:

1. **`snapshot_serde.rs`** (production code) types its byte helpers on the concrete `kanban_domain::Snapshot`.
2. **`test_helpers/helpers.rs`** builds a `Snapshot` fixture out of `Board`, `Card`, `Column`, `Sprint`, `ArchivedCard`, `DependencyGraph`.
3. **`error.rs:39-69`** defines `impl From<PersistenceError> for kanban_domain::KanbanError`.

The Output (SRS) project wants to reuse kanban-persistence as a project-agnostic byte-storage layer. Remove the dep.

## Suggested direction

- **`snapshot_serde` → kanban-domain** (next to where `Snapshot` lives). The dep arrow flips: kanban-domain becomes a consumer of `kanban-persistence` for `PersistenceResult` / `PersistenceError`. This is the natural layering — domain is "above" persistence in the same way it's above kanban-core.
- **`From<PersistenceError> for KanbanError` impl → kanban-domain** (where `KanbanError` lives). Same dep-arrow flip; resolves the orphan-style awkwardness of persistence having to know domain error variants.
- **`fully_populated_snapshot` fixture → `kanban_domain::test_helpers`** (gated behind a new `test-helpers` feature on kanban-domain).
- **`store_contract_tests!` macro → kept in kanban-persistence, refactored** to take a `Fn() -> Vec<u8>` seed closure. The macro stays generic; callers supply the kanban-flavored bytes externally.
- **Bonus: add `kanban_persistence::json::{encode, decode}` free functions** so the cross-project consumer gets a generic JSON helper without going through the now-domain-typed `snapshot_serde`.

## Alternatives considered

- **Move `snapshot_serde` to kanban-domain (CHOSEN, option A)**: locality (the byte form *is* a property of Snapshot) + dep arrow flip is natural + carries the `From` impl with it.
- **Move `snapshot_serde` to kanban-service (option B)**: forces `kanban-persistence-json`, `-sqlite`, `kanban-tui` to gain a kanban-service dep just to construct snapshot bytes in tests. Widens the dep graph. **Rejected.**
- **Generic `snapshot_serde<T: Serialize>` kept in persistence (option C)**: solves only the snapshot leak, not the `From` impl leak. Adopted as **partial bonus** (the generic helpers also live in `kanban_persistence::json`) but not the primary move.

## Per-leak relocation map

| Source | Destination |
| --- | --- |
| `kanban-persistence/src/snapshot_serde.rs` | `kanban-domain/src/snapshot_serde.rs` (forwards to `kanban_persistence::json::{encode, decode}`) |
| `kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/helpers.rs` (fully_populated_snapshot fn) | `kanban-domain/src/test_helpers.rs` behind a `test-helpers` feature |
| `kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/contract.rs` (Snapshot-typed body) | Stays in persistence, refactored to take `Fn() -> Vec<u8>` seed |
| `kanban-persistence/src/error.rs` (`From<PersistenceError> for KanbanError` impl) | `kanban-domain/src/error.rs` (relocated impl block + tests) |

## Caller updates required

~24 call sites across `kanban-cli`, `kanban-service` (4 files), `kanban-tui` (3 files), `kanban-persistence-json` (1 test), `kanban-persistence-sqlite` (5 test sites). Each changes its import from `kanban_persistence::snapshot_*` to `kanban_domain::snapshot_*`. The 24 sites are enumerated in the child cards.

## Children (atomic units)

- Add generic kanban_persistence::json::{encode, decode} free-functions
- Move snapshot_serde from persistence to kanban-domain
- Move From<PersistenceError> for KanbanError impl to kanban-domain
- Move fully_populated_snapshot fixture to kanban-domain::test_helpers
- Refactor store_contract_tests! macro to take a seed closure
- Remove kanban-domain dep from kanban-persistence/Cargo.toml + add regression guard

## Dependencies

Independent of the other top-level sections. Can land in parallel with the Backend/Command/UndoStack/Context work since it touches different files.

## Definition of Done

- `crates/kanban-persistence/Cargo.toml` has no `kanban-domain = ...` line.
- `cargo build --workspace --all-features && cargo test --workspace --all-features` clean.
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/` contains no `use kanban_domain` lines.
- New `crates/kanban-persistence/tests/no_domain_dep.rs` regression guard test passes.
- CHANGELOG.md `## Unreleased` entry explaining the snapshot_serde rename.
cards.title: Decouple kanban-persistence from kanban-domain

cards.description:
# Section: Sweep extras + DON'T-MOVE docs

Three small generalization wins discovered during the sweep that have zero kanban content but currently live in kanban-service, plus documentation cards calling out things that look generalizable but aren't.

## Three lift candidates

1. **`kanban_service::path::validate_path`** → `kanban_core::path::validate_path`. Pure path-traversal-prevention helper. Used by kanban-cli (app.rs:284), kanban-mcp (server.rs:11,118), kanban-service. Output SRS app needs the same security primitive. ~140 LOC including tests. Return type changes from `KanbanResult<PathBuf>` to `CoreResult<PathBuf>`; kanban-service keeps a 5-line shim that maps `CoreError::Validation` → `KanbanError::validation` so downstream callers don't see signature change.

2. **`store_manager` Windows file-cleanup helpers** (`remove_file_with_windows_retry`, `cleanup_destination_files`) at `crates/kanban-service/src/store_manager.rs:338-366` → `kanban_core::fs`. Pure FS utility with linear-backoff retry for Windows SQLite handle latency. Zero kanban content. Rename `cleanup_destination_files` → `cleanup_sqlite_artifacts` for clarity.

3. **`config.rs` file-I/O scaffolding** (`config_path`, atomic `write_config_file`, format dispatch on file extension) → `kanban_core::config_io`. Parameterize `config_path` on app name (currently hardcoded `"kanban"`). Output SRS app needs the same atomic write + 0o600 perms + `~/.config/<app>/config.toml` pattern. The AppConfig-specific bits (`strip_defaults`, `effective_configuration_location`, `validate`) stay in kanban-service and call into the new core helpers.

## DON'T-MOVE documentation

Three files lack a top-level "why this stays here" comment. Future contributors will be tempted to "clean these up" into kanban-core and break the no-domain-leak rule. This card adds module-level doc comments that encode the architectural intent:

- `kanban-service/src/cascade.rs` — schema-specific cascade-delete order. Stays. The cascade *pattern* generalizes via `kanban_core::graph::Cascadable`; this file is the kanban-specific instance.
- `kanban-service/src/store_manager.rs` (excluding the FS helpers being lifted) — wraps the kanban `Snapshot`/`AllBoardsExport` shape. The cross-platform FS helpers lift to core (see above) but the migrate/repair logic stays.
- `kanban-core/src/config.rs` — `AppConfig` looks generic because it lives in core but its fields (`storage_backend`, `default_card_prefix`, `default_sprint_prefix`, etc.) bake in the kanban data model. Output's SRS app should define its own `OutputConfig` struct and reuse only the `config_io` helpers.

## Alternatives considered

- **Don't lift; leave the utilities in service**: Output app reimplements each. Wasteful and produces drift. **Rejected.**
- **Lift only `validate_path` (the most universal); leave the others**: arbitrary cutoff. The three are all clearly generic. **Rejected.**
- **Lift via deeper redesigns (e.g., `Storage` trait abstraction over filesystem)**: over-engineering for three utilities. **Rejected.**

## Children (atomic units)

- Lift validate_path to kanban_core::path (with kanban-service shim for backward compat)
- Lift Windows file-cleanup to kanban_core::fs (rename to cleanup_sqlite_artifacts)
- Extract config_io module to kanban-core (atomic_write, config_path_for_app, save/load with format dispatch)
- DON'T-MOVE doc: cascade.rs module-level comment
- DON'T-MOVE doc: store_manager.rs module-level comment
- DON'T-MOVE doc: kanban-core/src/config.rs module-level comment

## Dependencies

Depends on: Scaffolding (deps additions for tempfile/dunce/dirs). Otherwise independent of the other sections.

## Definition of Done

- `kanban_core::{path, fs, config_io}` modules exist with the three lifted utilities.
- kanban-service shims (where needed) preserve existing public API.
- Three module-level doc comments added (cascade, store_manager, kanban_core::config).
- `cargo test --workspace` green.
- `cargo doc --no-deps -p kanban-core -p kanban-service` renders the new docs without warnings.
cards.title: Sweep extras: lift utilities + add DON'T-MOVE docs
Reorganize kanban-core to accept new submodules

# Pre-work: add third-party dependencies to kanban-core

## Motivation
Subsequent cards in the sprint need filesystem and observability primitives that `kanban-core` does not currently depend on. Adding them in one upfront PR lets every other card focus on its actual work.

## Current state
`crates/kanban-core/Cargo.toml` `[dependencies]` lists:

```toml
anyhow.workspace = true
thiserror.workspace = true
serde.workspace = true
uuid.workspace = true
chrono.workspace = true
async-trait.workspace = true
```

Missing deps needed by downstream cards:
- **tracing**: used by `Context::replace_backend` for "Replacing backend; undo/redo history discarded" log (Move Context section).
- **tempfile**: needed by `config_io::atomic_write` (Sweep section).
- **dunce**: needed by `path::validate_path` (Sweep section).
- **dirs**: needed by `config_io::config_path_for_app` (Sweep section).

## Target shape
```toml
[dependencies]
anyhow.workspace = true
thiserror.workspace = true
serde.workspace = true
uuid.workspace = true
chrono.workspace = true
async-trait.workspace = true
tracing.workspace = true
tempfile.workspace = true
dunce.workspace = true
dirs = "5.0"   # match the version used in kanban-service
```

## Steps
1. Verify `tracing`, `tempfile`, `dunce` are already in the workspace's `[workspace.dependencies]` table (they are — used by kanban-service and kanban-persistence).
2. Confirm `dirs = "5.0"` is the correct pin by checking `crates/kanban-service/Cargo.toml`.
3. Update `crates/kanban-core/Cargo.toml`.
4. `cargo build -p kanban-core` to verify deps resolve.

## Alternatives
- **Let each card add its own dep**: produces a noisy diff per card; risks two cards adding the same dep with different versions. **Rejected.**
- **Promote `dirs` to a workspace dep**: reasonable follow-up, not required this sprint.

## Edge cases
- Some kanban-core consumers may not have wanted a tracing or tokio dep transitively. Acceptable — the goal of the sprint is to share async backend infrastructure.

## Tests
None. Cargo build is the test.

## DoD
- `cargo build -p kanban-core` passes.
- `cargo build --workspace` passes.
- New deps appear in `Cargo.lock`.

cards.description:
# Pre-work: error type bridging between kanban-core and kanban-domain

## Motivation
The generic `Context::execute` closure (added in the Move Context section) needs to bubble `KanbanResult` errors out of `with_transaction` as `CoreResult`. Today, `CoreError` has only `Validation` and `Config` variants. Add `CoreError::Internal(String)` so the bridge has a target variant, and add `impl From<KanbanError> for CoreError` in kanban-domain so `?` propagates seamlessly through the closure.

## Current state
`crates/kanban-core/src/error.rs`:
```rust
#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum CoreError {
    #[error("validation error: {0}")]
    Validation(String),
    #[error("config error: {0}")]
    Config(String),
}

pub type CoreResult<T> = Result<T, CoreError>;
```

`crates/kanban-domain/src/error.rs:343` already has the reverse direction:
```rust
impl From<kanban_core::CoreError> for KanbanError {
    fn from(err: kanban_core::CoreError) -> Self {
        match err {
            kanban_core::CoreError::Validation(msg) => KanbanError::validation(msg),
            kanban_core::CoreError::Config(msg) => KanbanError::Internal(msg),
        }
    }
}
```

## Target shape
Extend `CoreError`:
```rust
#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum CoreError {
    #[error("validation error: {0}")]
    Validation(String),
    #[error("config error: {0}")]
    Config(String),
    #[error("internal error: {0}")]
    Internal(String),
}
```

Update the existing `From<CoreError> for KanbanError` impl to cover the new variant:
```rust
impl From<kanban_core::CoreError> for KanbanError {
    fn from(err: kanban_core::CoreError) -> Self {
        match err {
            kanban_core::CoreError::Validation(msg) => KanbanError::validation(msg),
            kanban_core::CoreError::Config(msg)    => KanbanError::Internal(msg),
            kanban_core::CoreError::Internal(msg)  => KanbanError::Internal(msg),
        }
    }
}
```

Add the one-way `From<KanbanError> for CoreError` (always lossy, always Internal):
```rust
impl From<KanbanError> for kanban_core::CoreError {
    fn from(e: KanbanError) -> Self {
        kanban_core::CoreError::Internal(e.to_string())
    }
}
```

## Steps
1. Add `CoreError::Internal` variant in `crates/kanban-core/src/error.rs`.
2. Update the `From<CoreError> for KanbanError` impl in `crates/kanban-domain/src/error.rs` for exhaustive match.
3. Add the `From<KanbanError> for CoreError` impl in the same file.
4. Add unit tests asserting Display format and roundtrip.

## Edge cases
- The `From<KanbanError> for CoreError` is one-way-lossy on purpose. Typed-variant discrimination (e.g. `KanbanError::ConflictDetected`, `KanbanError::UnsupportedFutureVersion`) happens *outside* `with_transaction` closures — verified by inspection of context.rs:191-260, no closure-internal code matches on typed variants.

## Tests
Inline in `kanban-core/src/error.rs`:
- `test_core_error_internal_display`: assert `"internal error: foo"` format.

Inline in `kanban-domain/src/error.rs`:
- `test_kanban_error_converts_to_core_error_internal`: assert any `KanbanError` maps to `CoreError::Internal` with preserved Display.

## DoD
- `cargo test -p kanban-core` green.
- `cargo test -p kanban-domain` green.
- Both directions of conversion exist and round-trip via `Display`.

cards.description:
# Impl kanban_core::Backend for InMemoryStore

## Motivation
`InMemoryStore` is the simplest backend and lives in kanban-domain. Adding the `Backend` impl here is risk-free (delegates to existing `DataStore` methods) and unblocks the Compose section (where `KanbanBackend for InMemoryStore` shrinks to one line because lifecycle stuff has moved off it).

## Current state
`crates/kanban-domain/src/in_memory_store.rs` has `impl DataStore for InMemoryStore` and `impl CommandStore for InMemoryStore`. No `Backend` impl exists.

`DataStore::snapshot` and `DataStore::apply_snapshot` are at `crates/kanban-domain/src/data_store.rs:116-117`.

`kanban-domain/Cargo.toml` already depends on `kanban-core`, `async-trait`, `uuid`. Nothing to add.

## Target shape
Append to `crates/kanban-domain/src/in_memory_store.rs`:

```rust
use kanban_core::{Backend, CoreError, CoreResult};

#[async_trait::async_trait]
impl Backend for InMemoryStore {
    type Snapshot = crate::Snapshot;

    fn snapshot(&self) -> CoreResult<Self::Snapshot> {
        DataStore::snapshot(self).map_err(|e| CoreError::Internal(e.to_string()))
    }

    fn apply_snapshot(&self, s: Self::Snapshot) -> CoreResult<()> {
        DataStore::apply_snapshot(self, s).map_err(|e| CoreError::Internal(e.to_string()))
    }
    // flush/reload/needs_flush/needs_save_worker/instance_id use defaults (correct for in-memory).
}
```

Note the fully-qualified `DataStore::snapshot(self)` call to disambiguate from `Backend::snapshot` (would otherwise recurse).

## Steps
1. Confirm the Backend trait card has merged.
2. Add the impl above to `crates/kanban-domain/src/in_memory_store.rs`.
3. Add inline tests.

## Tests
Inline tests in `in_memory_store.rs`:
- `test_in_memory_store_satisfies_backend_trait`: trait-object cast.
- `test_in_memory_store_backend_snapshot_roundtrip`: snapshot, mutate, apply_snapshot, verify mutation undone.
- `test_in_memory_store_backend_flush_is_noop`.
- `test_in_memory_store_backend_with_transaction_rolls_back`: end-to-end transaction using only the Backend trait (no KanbanBackend reference).

## Edge cases
- `DataStore::snapshot` returns `KanbanResult<Snapshot>`; map through `CoreError::Internal(e.to_string())`. Acceptable lossiness — same loss the reverse `From<CoreError> for KanbanError` already does.
- `async_trait` on a trait with only sync `fn`s is fine; it just doesn't transform anything. We still need `#[async_trait]` because the Backend trait itself uses async for `flush`/`reload`.

## DoD
- `cargo test -p kanban-domain` green.
- `cargo build -p kanban-service` still compiles (current `KanbanBackend for InMemoryStore` is unaffected; the Compose section will tighten the bound).

# Migrate kanban-service to the relocated UndoStack

## Motivation
With `kanban_core::UndoStack<Cmd>` available, delete the local `undo_stack` module from kanban-service and switch `KanbanContext`'s field type. The diff is small and bisectable: this card produces a state where kanban-service still has its own monolithic `KanbanContext`, just using the new core `UndoStack` for storage.

## Current state
- `crates/kanban-service/src/undo_stack.rs` (the whole file).
- `crates/kanban-service/src/lib.rs:23` declares `pub mod undo_stack;`.
- `crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs:52` holds `undo_stack: crate::undo_stack::UndoStack`.
- The execute/undo/redo/clear_history/etc. methods touch `self.undo_stack`.

## Target shape
1. Delete `crates/kanban-service/src/undo_stack.rs`.
2. Edit `crates/kanban-service/src/lib.rs:23` — drop `pub mod undo_stack;`.
3. Edit `crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs`:
   - Change field: `undo_stack: kanban_core::UndoStack<Command>`.
   - Change ctor: `undo_stack: kanban_core::UndoStack::new()`.
   - Change UndoEntry construction in execute (line 207): `kanban_core::UndoEntry { forward: commands, inverse: inverses }`.
4. All other call sites (`peek_undo`, `commit_undo`, `clear`, etc.) keep their existing form — the method names and signatures are identical between the deleted local type and `kanban_core::UndoStack<Command>`.

## Steps
1. Confirm `kanban_core::UndoStack` and `kanban_core::Command for kanban_domain::Command` have all merged.
2. Add `use kanban_core::{UndoEntry, UndoStack};` near the top of context.rs.
3. Make the field + ctor + UndoEntry construction edits.
4. Delete `kanban-service/src/undo_stack.rs`.
5. Remove `pub mod undo_stack;` from lib.rs.
6. `cargo build -p kanban-service && cargo test -p kanban-service`.

## Edge cases
- **External imports**: grep workspace for `use kanban_service::undo_stack` — if zero hits (likely, given `mod` not `pub mod`), no compat alias needed. If hits exist, add `pub type UndoStack = kanban_core::UndoStack<Command>;` in service for one release.
- **`UndoEntry` constructor**: needs the generic parameter explicit (`UndoEntry::<Command>` or via type inference from the field).
- **Orphan rule**: The `impl kanban_core::Command for kanban_domain::commands::Command` must exist before this card (provided by Section 3 / Command trait section). If it doesn't, this card won't compile.

## Tests
No new tests. Existing `kanban-service/tests/undo_redo.rs` continues to cover end-to-end.

## DoD
- `kanban-service/src/undo_stack.rs` deleted.
- `pub mod undo_stack;` removed from `kanban-service/src/lib.rs`.
- `KanbanContext::undo_stack: kanban_core::UndoStack<Command>` compiles.
- `cargo test --workspace` green.

# Verification card: prove KanbanContext's public API is preserved

## Motivation
The whole composition refactor (Section 6) is justified by "no caller sees a signature change". This card is the verification step. If a downstream caller needs a source edit, the wrapper's pass-through layer missed a method or changed a return type — go back and fix the wrapper.

## What to check

1. Run the workspace build:
   ```
   cargo build --workspace --all-features
   ```

2. Run all tests:
   ```
   cargo test --workspace --all-features
   ```

3. Confirm no source changes in downstream crates:
   ```
   git diff --stat crates/kanban-cli/ crates/kanban-tui/ crates/kanban-mcp/
   ```
   Expected output: empty.

4. Grep for any imports that newly resolve through kanban-core where they used to resolve through kanban-service:
   ```
   git grep "use kanban_core" crates/kanban-cli crates/kanban-tui crates/kanban-mcp
   ```
   Expected: zero new lines (the wrapper hides everything).

## Steps
1. After all of Section 6's cards have merged, do a clean build.
2. Run the 4 checks above.
3. If anything fails, identify the missing pass-through or behavioral change in `KanbanContext` and fix.
4. Commit the verification result as a comment on this card or in the sprint retrospective.

## Edge cases
- The TUI's `tui_context.rs:30` calls `ctx.backend().needs_save_worker()`. After 6.4, `ctx.backend()` returns `Arc<dyn KanbanBackend>` (unchanged) and `needs_save_worker()` resolves to the `Backend` trait method via the supertrait bound. Should work without edits.
- `settings_view.rs:171` calls `ctx.backend().instance_id()` — same reasoning.
- The MCP server calls `ctx.execute(...)`, `ctx.undo()`, `ctx.save()` — all preserved via the wrapper.

## DoD
- `cargo build --workspace --all-features` green.
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` green.
- `git diff --stat` for kanban-cli/tui/mcp is empty.
- No new `use kanban_core` imports in downstream crates.

cards.description:
# Backward-compatible alias for CommandStore

## Motivation
Once `kanban_core::CommandLog<C>` exists, replace `kanban_domain::CommandStore` with a domain-specific alias plus blanket impl. Existing call sites compile unchanged.

## Current state
`crates/kanban-domain/src/command_store.rs:6-22`:
```rust
pub trait CommandStore: Send + Sync {
    fn append_commands(&self, cmds: &[Command]) -> KanbanResult<u64>;
    fn command_count(&self) -> KanbanResult<u64>;
    fn load_commands(&self, from: u64, to: u64) -> KanbanResult<Vec<Vec<Command>>>;
    fn load_all_commands(&self) -> KanbanResult<(Vec<Vec<Command>>, u64)>;
}
```

Three impls:
- `kanban-domain/src/in_memory_store.rs:463-485`
- `kanban-service/src/json_backend.rs:301-314`
- `kanban-service/src/sqlite_backend.rs:184+`

## Target shape
```rust
//! Domain-specific alias for kanban_core::CommandLog. New code should
//! use `CommandLog<Command, Err = KanbanError>` directly; this alias
//! exists for backward compat with existing call sites.

use crate::commands::Command;
use crate::KanbanError;
pub use kanban_core::{CommandLog, CommandRecord};

pub trait CommandStore: CommandLog<Command, Err = KanbanError> {}
impl<T: CommandLog<Command, Err = KanbanError>> CommandStore for T {}
```

Method rename: `append_commands(&[Command])` → `append(&CommandRecord<Command>)`, `command_count` → `count`, `load_commands` → `load`, `load_all_commands` → `load_all`. Callers update incrementally.

## Steps
1. Confirm card 7.1 merged.
2. Replace `CommandStore` trait declaration with the alias above.
3. The three concrete impls (InMemoryStore, JsonBackend, SqliteBackend) need to switch from `impl CommandStore for X` (old methods) to `impl CommandLog<Command> for X` (new methods). Cards 7.3, 7.4, 7.5 handle each.
4. `cargo build -p kanban-domain` — should pass once at least one impl is converted (blanket impl picks it up).

## Caller-side method-name migration
- `backend.append_commands(cmds)?` → `backend.append(&CommandRecord::new(cmds.to_vec()))?` (one site: context.rs:202, handled in card 7.6).
- `backend.command_count()?` → `backend.count()?` (sites: context.rs:76; tests).
- `backend.load_commands(0, n)?` → `backend.load(0, n)?` (sites: TUI command-log panel; tests).

Until the per-backend cards (7.3/7.4/7.5) land, the trait alias relies on the old methods staying on the impls. Make this card land with at least one impl converted (InMemoryStore is simplest).

## Edge cases
- The blanket `impl<T: CommandLog<Command, Err = KanbanError>> CommandStore for T {}` means anywhere that bounds `T: CommandStore` keeps working — it now means "implements CommandLog<Command> for KanbanError".
- Trait object: `dyn CommandStore` is no longer object-safe in the alias-trait form (blanket impls + supertraits with generic params interact awkwardly). Verify by grepping for `dyn CommandStore` — if found, may need to keep CommandStore as a primary trait with delegation.

## Tests
Existing `kanban-domain` command_store tests continue to compile and pass once the impl rewires (cards 7.3-7.5 each carry their per-impl tests).

## DoD
- `kanban-domain/src/command_store.rs` re-exports CommandLog/CommandRecord and aliases CommandStore.
- `cargo build -p kanban-domain` green (with InMemoryStore impl from card 7.3).

Migrate InMemoryStore audit log to CommandLog<Command>

cards.description:
# JSON file format migration: Vec<Vec<Command>> → Vec<CommandRecord<Command>>

## Motivation
JsonBackend persists the audit log to disk as part of the JSON state file. Migrating to CommandRecord requires both an impl change and a backward-compat loader so existing `kanban.json` files still load.

## Current state
`crates/kanban-service/src/json_backend.rs:301-314`:
```rust
impl CommandStore for JsonDataStore {
    fn append_commands(&self, cmds: &[Command]) -> KanbanResult<u64> { /* writes Vec<Vec<Command>> */ }
    fn command_count(&self) -> KanbanResult<u64> { /* ... */ }
    fn load_commands(&self, from: u64, to: u64) -> KanbanResult<Vec<Vec<Command>>> { /* ... */ }
}
```

On-disk JSON schema for the command log field: a flat `Vec<Vec<Command>>` array.

## Target shape
1. **Trait impl swap**: `impl CommandLog<Command> for JsonDataStore` with new method signatures returning `CommandRecord<Command>`.

2. **In-memory state**: the cached `InMemoryStore` already gets the new shape via card 7.3.

3. **Backward-compat loader**: when parsing a kanban.json file, detect the legacy `Vec<Vec<Command>>` shape and wrap each inner vec in a `CommandRecord` with `id = Uuid::new_v4()` and `timestamp = file mtime or Utc::now()`. Idempotent — second load sees the new shape and passes through.

```rust
#[derive(Deserialize)]
#[serde(untagged)]
enum CommandLogJson {
    New(Vec<CommandRecord<Command>>),
    Legacy(Vec<Vec<Command>>),
}

impl From<CommandLogJson> for Vec<CommandRecord<Command>> {
    fn from(v: CommandLogJson) -> Self {
        match v {
            CommandLogJson::New(records) => records,
            CommandLogJson::Legacy(batches) => batches
                .into_iter()
                .map(CommandRecord::new)
                .collect(),
        }
    }
}
```

4. **Write path** always emits the new shape.

## Steps
1. Confirm cards 7.1, 7.2, 7.3 merged.
2. Update the JSON envelope struct in json_backend.rs to use `CommandLogJson` for the command log field.
3. Replace `impl CommandStore for JsonDataStore` with `impl CommandLog<Command>` + new method bodies.
4. Add a fixture-based test (see Tests).
5. `cargo test -p kanban-service json_backend`.

## Edge cases
- **Idempotent migration**: a file loaded once is rewritten in the new shape on the next flush. Re-loading sees `CommandLogJson::New` and skips the conversion.
- **`do_flush` write path**: must serialize the new shape. Existing `do_flush` body is largely unchanged.
- **mtime fallback**: if file metadata is unavailable, default to `Utc::now()` — the legacy records get current timestamps. Acceptable: legacy timestamps weren't tracked anyway.

## Tests
Add `crates/kanban-service/tests/audit_log_migration.rs`:
- `test_load_legacy_command_log_shape_succeeds`: prepare a JSON file with `commands: [[{...}], [{...}]]` (old shape), load via JsonDataStore, assert 2 CommandRecords loaded, each with non-nil id and a recent timestamp.
- `test_load_new_command_log_shape_succeeds`: prepare a JSON file with `commands: [{"id":..., "timestamp":..., "commands":[{...}]}]` (new shape), load, assert the id round-trips.
- `test_flush_emits_new_shape_after_loading_legacy`: load legacy, flush, re-parse the file as raw JSON, assert it has objects (not arrays) at the inner level.

## DoD
- JsonDataStore implements `CommandLog<Command>`.
- Existing kanban.json files in the repo's `test/` dir load successfully.
- New tests pass.
- `cargo test --workspace` green.

cards.description:
# Relocate snapshot_serde + update 24 callers

## Motivation
`crates/kanban-persistence/src/snapshot_serde.rs` is the largest leak: it hard-codes `kanban_domain::Snapshot`. Move it to kanban-domain alongside the type whose bytes it serializes. Dep arrow flips: kanban-domain depends on kanban-persistence for `PersistenceResult`/`PersistenceError`.

## Current state
`crates/kanban-persistence/src/snapshot_serde.rs`:
```rust
use crate::{PersistenceError, PersistenceResult};
use kanban_domain::Snapshot;

pub fn snapshot_to_json_bytes(snapshot: &Snapshot) -> PersistenceResult<Vec<u8>> {
    serde_json::to_vec_pretty(snapshot).map_err(|e| PersistenceError::Serialization(e.to_string()))
}

pub fn snapshot_from_json_bytes(bytes: &[u8]) -> PersistenceResult<Snapshot> {
    serde_json::from_slice(bytes).map_err(|e| PersistenceError::Serialization(e.to_string()))
}
```

Re-exported in `crates/kanban-persistence/src/lib.rs:17`.

24 caller sites across the workspace (enumerated below).

## Target shape

New file `crates/kanban-domain/src/snapshot_serde.rs`:

```rust
//! JSON byte encoding for `Snapshot`. Thin wrapper over
//! `kanban_persistence::json::{encode, decode}` typed on the domain type.

use crate::Snapshot;
use kanban_persistence::{json, PersistenceResult};

pub fn to_json_bytes(snapshot: &Snapshot) -> PersistenceResult<Vec<u8>> {
    json::encode(snapshot)
}

pub fn from_json_bytes(bytes: &[u8]) -> PersistenceResult<Snapshot> {
    json::decode(bytes)
}
```

Register in `crates/kanban-domain/src/lib.rs`:
```rust
pub mod snapshot_serde;
pub use snapshot_serde::{from_json_bytes as snapshot_from_json_bytes,
                         to_json_bytes as snapshot_to_json_bytes};
```

The crate-root re-exports keep call sites short (`kanban_domain::snapshot_to_json_bytes`).

Add `kanban-persistence = { path = "../kanban-persistence", version = "^0.6" }` to `crates/kanban-domain/Cargo.toml`.

Delete `crates/kanban-persistence/src/snapshot_serde.rs` and the `pub use` in `crates/kanban-persistence/src/lib.rs:17`.

## Caller updates (24 sites)

- `crates/kanban-cli/src/app.rs:124`
- `crates/kanban-service/src/store_manager.rs:5,275,529`
- `crates/kanban-service/src/json_backend.rs:9-10,490,613`
- `crates/kanban-service/tests/context_open.rs:159,165`
- `crates/kanban-persistence-json/src/json_file_store.rs:969`
- `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs:1945,2240,2382,2426,2441`
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/state/snapshot.rs:48`
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:2795`
- `crates/kanban-tui/tests/conflict_detection_tests.rs:30,57,97,132,192,236,290,312,337`

For each: change import from `kanban_persistence::snapshot_*` to `kanban_domain::snapshot_*`.

## Steps
1. Confirm card 8.1 (generic json helpers) merged.
2. Create `crates/kanban-domain/src/snapshot_serde.rs` with the body above. Port the two `#[cfg(test)]` tests from the source.
3. Add `pub mod snapshot_serde;` + re-exports to `crates/kanban-domain/src/lib.rs`.
4. Add `kanban-persistence` dep to `crates/kanban-domain/Cargo.toml`.
5. Delete `crates/kanban-persistence/src/snapshot_serde.rs` and the lib.rs re-export.
6. Update all 24 caller sites (split into per-crate commits if convenient).
7. `cargo build --workspace --all-features && cargo test --workspace --all-features`.

## Alternatives
- **Move to kanban-service**: rejected — forces persistence-json/sqlite/tui to depend on service. Widens dep graph.
- **Generic snapshot_serde<T: Serialize> kept in persistence**: solves only this leak, not the From impl. Adopted as bonus (the generic helpers from card 8.1) but not the primary move.

## Edge cases
- **`PersistenceError` exposure**: after this card, `kanban_domain::snapshot_serde` returns `PersistenceResult`. kanban-domain consumers transitively see `PersistenceError` — fine, `KanbanError` is built via the `From` impl (card 8.3).
- **Atomic rollout**: doing all 24 edits in one PR is mechanical but large. Optionally split: PR1 introduces the new module + deprecated re-exports in persistence; PR2-N update callers; PR(N+1) deletes the re-exports.

## Tests
- Port the 2 existing snapshot_serde tests (round-trip, invalid JSON) to the new location.
- All existing workspace tests continue to pass.

## DoD
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/snapshot_serde.rs` deleted.
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/snapshot_serde.rs` exists.
- `crates/kanban-domain/Cargo.toml` has `kanban-persistence` dep.
- All 24 caller files updated.
- `cargo build --workspace --all-features && cargo test --workspace --all-features` clean.
cards.title: Move snapshot_serde from kanban-persistence to kanban-domain
Move From<PersistenceError> for KanbanError impl to kanban-domain

cards.description:
# Relocate the kanban-specific test fixture

## Motivation
`crates/kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/helpers.rs` is 148 lines of kanban-domain fixture construction (`Board`, `Card`, `Column`, `Sprint`, etc.). Has nothing to do with persistence beyond being a Snapshot that contract tests serialize to bytes. Move to kanban-domain alongside the types.

## Current state
`crates/kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/helpers.rs:1-7`:
```rust
use chrono::Utc;
use kanban_domain::card::{Card, CardPriority, CardStatus};
use kanban_domain::sprint::{Sprint, SprintStatus};
use kanban_domain::Snapshot;
use kanban_domain::{ArchivedCard, Board, Column, DependencyGraph, SprintLog};
use uuid::Uuid;

pub fn fully_populated_snapshot() -> Snapshot { ... }
```

Re-exports at `crates/kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/mod.rs:4`:
```rust
pub use helpers::fully_populated_snapshot;
```

Callers:
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/contract.rs:1` (internal; handled in next card)
- `crates/kanban-service/src/test_helpers/helpers.rs:1` (one-line re-export)
- `crates/kanban-service/src/test_helpers/contract/lifecycle.rs:1,377`

## Target shape
New file `crates/kanban-domain/src/test_helpers.rs`:
```rust
//! Test fixtures for kanban-domain types.
//! Gated behind the `test-helpers` feature.

use crate::card::{Card, CardPriority, CardStatus};
use crate::sprint::{Sprint, SprintStatus};
use crate::{ArchivedCard, Board, Column, DependencyGraph, Snapshot, SprintLog};
use chrono::Utc;
use uuid::Uuid;

pub fn fully_populated_snapshot() -> Snapshot {
    // ... port verbatim from kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/helpers.rs:8-147
    // with kanban_domain:: paths shortened to crate::
}
```

Add feature in `crates/kanban-domain/Cargo.toml`:
```toml
[features]
test-helpers = []
```

Wire module in `crates/kanban-domain/src/lib.rs`:
```rust
#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-helpers"))]
pub mod test_helpers;
```

Update `crates/kanban-service/Cargo.toml`:
```toml
[features]
test-helpers = ["kanban-persistence/test-helpers", "kanban-domain/test-helpers"]
```

Update `crates/kanban-service/src/test_helpers/helpers.rs:1`:
```rust
pub use kanban_domain::test_helpers::fully_populated_snapshot;
```

Delete `crates/kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/helpers.rs` and remove `pub mod helpers; pub use helpers::fully_populated_snapshot;` from `crates/kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/mod.rs`.

## Steps
1. Confirm cards 8.2 (domain depends on persistence) merged.
2. Create the new fixture file with the function body ported.
3. Add the `test-helpers` feature to kanban-domain.
4. Wire the conditional module.
5. Update kanban-service's `test-helpers` feature.
6. Update kanban-service's helpers.rs re-export.
7. Update the in-progress `crates/kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/contract.rs:1` import to point at the new location (the next card refactors away this dependency entirely).
8. Delete kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/helpers.rs.
9. Remove the pub mod / pub use lines from kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/mod.rs.
10. `cargo build --workspace --all-features && cargo test --workspace --all-features`.

## Alternatives
- **Move to kanban-service::test_helpers**: service already re-exports it. But the fixture references no service-layer symbols. Wrong home. **Rejected.**
- **Skip the feature flag** (use `#[cfg(test)]` only): breaks external test consumers (integration tests in kanban-persistence-json/tests/contract.rs) because they're in a different crate. **Rejected.**

## Edge cases
- `kanban-domain`'s `test-helpers` feature is a no-op default (no extra deps; the fixture uses only chrono and uuid, both already domain deps).
- `EdgeBase` import: stays as `use kanban_core::EdgeBase;` (kanban-domain already deps kanban-core).

## Tests
The existing test in `kanban-service/src/test_helpers/contract/lifecycle.rs:377` (`test_full_roundtrip_preserves_all_fields`) keeps passing via the service re-export.

## DoD
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/test_helpers/helpers.rs` deleted.
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/test_helpers.rs` exists.
- kanban-domain has `test-helpers` feature; kanban-service's `test-helpers` enables it.
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features` clean.
cards.title: Move fully_populated_snapshot fixture to kanban_domain::test_helpers
Refactor store_contract_tests! macro to take a seed closure

cards.description:
# Final sweep: delete the dep, add a CI guard

## Motivation
After cards 8.2/8.3/8.4/8.5, kanban-persistence has zero `kanban_domain` references in `src/`. The dep line in Cargo.toml is dead weight. Remove it and add a regression guard so it doesn't sneak back in.

## Current state
`crates/kanban-persistence/Cargo.toml:16-18`:
```toml
[dependencies]
kanban-core = { path = "../kanban-core", version = "^0.6" }
kanban-domain = { path = "../kanban-domain", version = "^0.6" }
```

## Target shape
`crates/kanban-persistence/Cargo.toml`:
```toml
[dependencies]
kanban-core = { path = "../kanban-core", version = "^0.6" }
# kanban-domain dep removed: persistence is domain-agnostic. See
# tests/no_domain_dep.rs for the regression guard.
```

Add a regression-guard test at `crates/kanban-persistence/tests/no_domain_dep.rs`:
```rust
//! Regression guard: kanban-persistence must remain domain-agnostic.
//! If this test fails because someone added a kanban-domain import,
//! reconsider — the helper probably belongs in kanban-domain or
//! kanban-service.

#[test]
fn kanban_persistence_must_not_depend_on_kanban_domain() {
    let manifest = include_str!("../Cargo.toml");
    assert!(
        !manifest.contains("kanban-domain"),
        "kanban-persistence/Cargo.toml mentions kanban-domain; \
         the persistence crate must remain domain-agnostic. \
         See crates/kanban-persistence/tests/no_domain_dep.rs."
    );
}
```

## Steps
1. Confirm all 8.* sibling cards merged.
2. Run `grep -rn "kanban_domain" crates/kanban-persistence/src` — must return empty.
3. Run `grep -rn "kanban_domain" crates/kanban-persistence/tests` — must return empty.
4. Delete the `kanban-domain = ...` line from Cargo.toml.
5. Create `crates/kanban-persistence/tests/no_domain_dep.rs` with the guard.
6. `cargo build --workspace --all-features && cargo test --workspace --all-features`.
7. `cargo tree -p kanban-persistence | grep kanban-domain` — must return empty.
8. Add a CHANGELOG entry under `## Unreleased`.

## Alternatives
- **Skip the regression guard**: future change can re-introduce the dep without signal. The guard is cheap insurance.
- **Use cargo-deny `[bans]`**: heavier; requires cargo-deny in CI. Hand-rolled test is enough for one crate.

## Edge cases
- Nothing in `[dev-dependencies]` of `kanban-persistence/Cargo.toml` mentions kanban-domain. Verify by inspection.
- Workspace's `Cargo.lock` regenerates without kanban-domain in kanban-persistence's closure.

## Tests
- `cargo build --workspace --all-features`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features`
- The new regression-guard test passes.

## DoD
- `crates/kanban-persistence/Cargo.toml` has no `kanban-domain` line.
- `cargo tree -p kanban-persistence` does not list kanban-domain.
- Regression-guard test exists and passes.
- CHANGELOG entry under `## Unreleased` explaining the snapshot_serde rename for downstream consumers.
cards.title: Remove kanban-domain dep from kanban-persistence + add regression guard
Lift validate_path to kanban_core::path (with kanban-service shim)
Lift Windows file-cleanup helpers to kanban_core::fs
Extract config_io module to kanban-core
docs(service): explain why cascade.rs cannot move to kanban-core
docs(service): explain why store_manager.rs is service-scoped
docs(core): document that AppConfig is kanban-shaped and shouldn't be genericised

cards.description:
## Summary

The card-metadata external editor (`e` on the Metadata section of Card
Detail view) is the **only remaining due_date input path in the
codebase that rejects `YYYY-MM-DD`**. CLI, MCP, and Sprint date args
already accept both `YYYY-MM-DD` and full RFC3339. This card brings the
TUI editor path to parity, extracts the duplicated parser into a
shared helper, and removes the silent-drop behaviour that hides parse
failures from users.

Tracks GitHub issue: https://github.com/fulsomenko/kanban/issues/312

## Motivation

From the issue (Jose Ortiz, 2026-06-04):

> I have been having a small issue which is updating the metadata of a
> task. I would edit the meta data via my editor (neovim) and save and
> quit but in the task itself, the metadata is still the same as if I
> never edited it. I have been doing YYYY-MM-DD.

Comment (kbilleter):

> I resorted to \"YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00+00.00\"

Owner response committed to YYYY-MM-DD as the first enhancement.

The bug is doubly misleading because the TUI **displays** due_date in
date-style format (`Due: 2024-01-15 14:30` from
`card_detail_sections.rs:34`'s `\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M\"`) while the editor
**accepts** only full RFC3339. Users reasonably infer \"the format I see
is the format I write\" and the silent drop confirms nothing.

## Where due_date input is parsed today

| Surface | File:line | Behaviour |
|---|---|---|
| CLI `card create/update --due-date` | `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/card.rs:411-417` | Lenient: RFC3339 → YYYY-MM-DD fallback |
| CLI `sprint` date args | `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/sprint.rs:7-13` | Lenient: same pattern |
| MCP `create_card` / `update_card` | `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs:169-174` | Lenient: same pattern; schema advertises both at line 514 |
